title: Epic 4: Infrastructure Cutover
description: The point of no return. Run crumb import --from-beads on existing .beads/issues.jsonl data. Run crumb doctor to validate migrated data. Delete .beads/, scrub-pii.sh, sync-to-claude.sh, install-hooks.sh, Dolt config. Run verification gate confirming zero bd references remain in active files (grep -rl '\bbd ' --include='*.md' --include='*.sh' | grep -v _archive/). Blocked by Epics 1-3.

title: Run crumb import --from-beads on existing data
description:
Execute the data migration from Beads to Crumbs format. Run crumb import --from-beads .beads/issues.jsonl to convert all existing Beads issues. Verify the migration output: check that epic count, task count, and dependency structure match the source data. Spot-check priority mapping (0→P0, 1→P1, etc.), type conversion (epic→trail), and dependency conversion (parent-child→links.parent, blocks→links.blocked_by). This is the first step of the point-of-no-return cutover sequence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] crumb import --from-beads .beads/issues.jsonl completes without errors (exit 0)
- [ ] Number of imported trails matches number of beads epics (verify with crumb trail list count)
- [ ] Number of imported crumbs matches number of beads non-epic issues (verify with crumb list count)
- [ ] Priority mapping spot-check: at least 3 issues verified to have correct P0-P4 mapping
- [ ] Dependency conversion spot-check: at least 3 parent-child and 3 blocks relationships verified
- [ ] config.json next_crumb_id and next_trail_id exceed highest imported IDs

title: Validate migrated data with crumb doctor
description:
Run crumb doctor to validate the integrity of the migrated .crumbs/tasks.jsonl. Check for: malformed JSON lines, dangling blocked_by references, dangling parent links, duplicate IDs, orphan crumbs. Fix any issues found using crumb doctor --fix or manual corrections. The data must be clean before proceeding to deletion.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] crumb doctor runs and reports findings (or 'No issues found')
- [ ] Zero errors (dangling parent links, duplicate IDs) — all errors resolved before proceeding
- [ ] Warnings (dangling blocked_by, orphan crumbs) reviewed and either fixed or documented as acceptable
- [ ] crumb doctor run a second time after fixes confirms clean state
- [ ] crumb ready returns a valid set of ready-to-work tasks (sanity check)

title: Run verification gate — zero bd references remaining
description:
Execute the final verification gate confirming complete migration. Run: grep -rl '\bbd ' --include='*.md' --include='*.sh' | grep -v _archive/ — must return zero results. Also verify: no .beads/ path references remain (grep -rl '\.beads/' --include='*.md' --include='*.sh' | grep -v _archive/). No 'Beads' or 'Dolt' references remain in active documentation (excluding changelogs and archive). This is the acceptance test for the entire migration.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] grep -rl '\bbd ' --include='*.md' --include='*.sh' | grep -v _archive/ returns empty (zero matches)
- [ ] grep -rl '\.beads/' --include='*.md' --include='*.sh' | grep -v _archive/ returns empty
- [ ] No active documentation references Beads or Dolt (excluding CHANGELOG, _archive/)
- [ ] crumb doctor reports clean state
- [ ] crumb ready returns valid results — the system is operational post-migration

title: Delete legacy Beads/Dolt artifacts
description:
Remove all Beads/Dolt infrastructure from the repository. Delete: .beads/ directory (issues.jsonl, config.yaml, dolt/, agent-summaries/), scripts/scrub-pii.sh, scripts/sync-to-claude.sh, scripts/install-hooks.sh, config.yaml (Dolt config at repo root). Remove pre-commit PII scrubbing hook and pre-push sync hook from .git/hooks/ or hook configuration. Archive .beads/ via git stash or .beads.bak/ before deletion as a safety net.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] .beads/ directory backed up (stash or .bak) before deletion
- [ ] .beads/ directory deleted from working tree
- [ ] scripts/scrub-pii.sh deleted
- [ ] scripts/sync-to-claude.sh deleted
- [ ] scripts/install-hooks.sh deleted
- [ ] config.yaml (Dolt config) deleted from repo root
- [ ] Pre-commit and pre-push hooks referencing beads/dolt removed or updated

title: Convert skills to marketplace plugin format for ant-farm: namespace
description: Currently skills are installed as standalone ~/.claude/skills/ant-farm-<name>/SKILL.md files, which makes them appear as /ant-farm-init, /ant-farm-plan, etc. Beads and superpowers use the marketplace plugin format (~/.claude/plugins/) with a commands/ directory, giving them colon-namespaced invocation like /beads:init. Convert ant-farm to the same plugin format so skills are invocable as /ant-farm:init, /ant-farm:plan, /ant-farm:status, /ant-farm:work. Update setup.sh accordingly.

title: Add init subcommand to crumb.py
description: crumb.py has no built-in way to bootstrap .crumbs/. Every command dies with 'no .crumbs/ directory found ... Run /ant-farm:init first' — but that only works inside Claude Code with the ant-farm skill installed. Add a 'crumb init' subcommand that creates .crumbs/, config.json, tasks.jsonl, and .gitignore entry. Should accept --prefix flag (or prompt interactively). This makes crumb.py self-contained and usable outside of Claude Code.

title: Migration Polish
Placeholder Conventions Cleanup
description: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md issues + cross-template placeholder syntax inconsistencies. 19 P3 bugs covering enforcement gaps, naming ambiguity, audit staleness, and angle-bracket vs curly-brace convention drift.

title: Handoff Safety
description: Fix silent failures, missing error handling, and underspecified workflows in inter-agent handoff paths

title: Documentation & Setup
description: 11 P3 bugs across README.md, SETUP.md, AGENTS.md, installation guides. Covers pantry-review deprecation cleanup, missing file references, fork instructions, and formatting issues.

title: Future Work
description: Low-priority polish and improvements from review sessions

title: Orchestration Workflow Polish
description: 12 P3 bugs in RULES.md workflow steps, queen-state.md, SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE. Covers cross-file navigation gaps, term definition duplication, session bootstrapping, and formatting.

title: Future Work
(EPIC) Create Session Exec Summary
description: Add a Scribe subagent to the orchestration pipeline that produces a 1-page executive summary of each session's work. The Scribe runs at Step 5b (after verification, before push), reads all session artifacts + commit diffs + open beads, and writes both an exec-summary.md (local session artifact) and a CHANGELOG.md entry (committed). A Pest Control ESV (Exec Summary Verification) checkpoint at Step 5c acts as a hard gate before push, running 6 mechanical checks: task coverage, commit coverage, open bead accuracy, CHANGELOG derivation fidelity, section completeness, and metric consistency. This replaces the Queen's current manual CHANGELOG authoring at Step 4.

title: Agent Definitions
description: Fix agent definition files that have generic prompts or missing guidance, causing cold agents to fail their intended workflows

title: Verification & Checkpoints
description: Define precise thresholds, clarify pipeline boundaries, and add complete error recovery procedures for checkpoint execution

title: Cross-File Contracts
description: Resolve contradictions, conflicts, and undefined authority chains across orchestration template files

title: Audit Findings: Orchestration Documentation Drift
description:
Findings from the systematic documentation drift audit performed on 2026-02-21. The audit cross-referenced all orchestration documentation (RULES.md, templates, CLAUDE.md, GLOSSARY, README, CONTRIBUTING) against the actual implementation (agent files, scripts, hooks, session artifacts) across 8 domains using 4 parallel audit agents.

Full audit report: docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

32 total findings: 4 S1 (Breaking), 15 S2 (Misleading), 13 S3 (Cosmetic).
This epic tracks the S1 and S2 fixes. S3 findings are documented in the audit report for opportunistic cleanup.

title: Terminology & Naming
description: Define extraction rules, standardize placeholder conventions, variable names, and terminology across all templates

title: Pantry & Scout Template Polish
description: 20 P3 bugs in pantry.md and scout.md agent templates. Covers guard edge cases, fail-fast logic, metadata handling, briefing format, TOCTOU races, and validation gaps.

title: Shell Script Fixes
description: 8 P3 bugs in shell scripts: fill-review-slots.sh, compose-review-skeletons.sh, install-hooks.sh, parse-progress-log.sh, scrub-pii.sh. Comments, error handling, bracket style consistency.

title: Big Head Fixes: Quality Descriptions + Cross-Session Dedup
description: Big Head files beads after consolidating Nitpicker review reports but has two deficiencies: (1) every bd create command example across templates omits --description/--body-file, so agents follow the concrete examples and file beads with titles only (no descriptions), and (2) Big Head deduplicates within a review round but never queries existing beads, causing identical findings to be filed across sessions (e.g., ant-farm-q3o6 and ant-farm-y719 — same title, filed 5.5 hours apart by different sessions). This epic adds a description template using --body-file to all bd create examples and a cross-session dedup step that queries bd list/bd search before filing. Changes span 4 files: big-head-skeleton.md, reviews.md, agents/big-head.md, and pantry.md.

title: Documentation Gaps
description: Add missing documentation: archive warnings, contribution guide, wave management rules, and state recovery procedures

title: Setup & Forkability
description: Fix sync script, hook installation, PII exposure, and documentation issues blocking adoption and forkability

title: Review Loop Convergence
description: Add convergence mechanics to the review pipeline so fix cycles terminate instead of looping indefinitely. Round 1 uses the existing 4-reviewer pipeline unchanged. Round 2+ narrows to 2 reviewers (Correctness + Edge Cases) scoped to fix commits only, with P3s auto-filed to a "Future Work" epic. The loop terminates when a round produces zero P1/P2 findings. Implementation plan with exact edit content: docs/plans/2026-02-19-review-loop-convergence.md

title: Review Pipeline Polish
description: 35 P3 bugs across reviews.md, big-head.md, big-head-skeleton.md, nitpicker.md, dirt-pusher-skeleton.md, CCB/DMVDC verification. Covers review rounds, gates, failure paths, step numbering, severity levels, and cross-file consistency.

title: Bead Backlog Audit: Triage 176 Open Issues Against Current Codebase
description:
176 open beads exist, mostly P3 bugs filed during documentation drift audits (sessions 068ecc83, db790c8d, e7ff7c0d). The codebase has changed significantly since filing — commits 1b0037e (review script merger), 3f52803 (parse-progress-log fix), 17a6e03 (checkpoints Pest Control correction), 7569c5e (model reference update), and others have modified the exact files these beads reference.

Current state: No systematic triage has been done. Some beads are likely already fixed, some are duplicated (16 known exact-title pairs), and some may be irrelevant (e.g., beads about archived pantry-review.md). Without triage, the backlog is noisy and makes it hard to identify genuinely actionable work.

Architecture: Three-pass approach with file-scoped batching.
- Pass 0: Mechanical pre-processing — export, exact-title dedup, partition 168 non-epic beads into 9 file-scoped batches
- Pass 1: 9 parallel verification agents — each reads its batch of beads + the relevant codebase files, assigns a verdict per bead (STILL_VALID, ALREADY_FIXED, IRRELEVANT, NEEDS_CONTEXT, DUPLICATE_SUSPECT)
- Pass 2: 1 consolidation agent — merges all batch outputs into a unified triage report with duplicate clusters, epic health summaries, and priority re-calibration suggestions

All artifacts are stored in .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/.

Success Criteria:
- [ ] Every open bead (176) has exactly one triage verdict
- [ ] Duplicate clusters are identified and resolved (keep/close recommendations)
- [ ] Final triage report is produced as audit/final-triage-report.md
- [ ] User reviews and approves report before any bd close commands run
- [ ] Net backlog reduction is documented with before/after counts

title: Review-Fix Loop Upgrade
description: Upgrade the review-fix-review loop to use a persistent Nitpicker team that stays alive across all rounds. Fix Dirt Pushers and fix Pest Control agents spawn into the team, enabling direct reviewer communication, a tight DMVDC inner loop, and proper round 2+ team-based reviews (eliminating the sequential fallback). Includes CCB model upgrade to sonnet, root cause spot-checking, and material failure escalation. Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-22-persistent-review-team-design.md

title: Placeholder guard incomplete for round-1 paths when REVIEW_ROUND is corrupt
description:
## Root Cause
The placeholder substitution guard in `big-head-skeleton.md:L554-L593` validates clarity/drift report paths only inside an `if [ "$REVIEW_ROUND" -eq 1 ]` block. If `REVIEW_ROUND` itself contains unresolved placeholders, the outer case statement exits before reaching the round-1 path validation. This means a corrupt REVIEW_ROUND masks whether clarity/drift paths also have unresolved placeholders.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L554-L593` — placeholder validation ordering (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
The implemented fix (commit 365a0d9) uses a conditional approach rather than fully unconditional checking. Correctness and edge-cases paths are checked unconditionally. Clarity and drift paths are checked conditionally (round 1 only), because round 2+ briefs contain unsubstituted angle-bracket placeholders for those paths by design — unconditional checking would always trigger a false PLACEHOLDER_ERROR.

The REVIEW_ROUND pre-validation invariant provides the safety guarantee: the `case` statement catches corrupt REVIEW_ROUND values before execution reaches the conditional clarity/drift branch, so a corrupt REVIEW_ROUND cannot silently skip validation.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md`: Correctness/edge-cases placeholder checks outside `if [ "$REVIEW_ROUND" -eq 1 ]` block; clarity/drift checks inside (round 1 only)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctness and edge-cases report paths are checked for unresolved placeholders unconditionally (outside any round-number conditional)
- [ ] Clarity and drift report paths are checked conditionally (round 1 only), with a comment explaining that round 2+ briefs contain intentional unsubstituted placeholders for those paths
- [ ] A corrupt REVIEW_ROUND value triggers an error via the pre-validation `case` statement before reaching the conditional branch — the REVIEW_ROUND pre-validation invariant is documented as the safety guarantee
- [ ] The polling loop itself remains gated on REVIEW_ROUND for round-appropriate behavior

title: Installation guide omits pre-commit PII scrub hook documentation
description: Root cause: install-hooks.sh now installs both pre-push (sync) and pre-commit (PII scrub) hooks, but docs/installation-guide.md only describes the pre-push hook. The pre-commit hook's purpose, behavior, backup path (.git/hooks/pre-commit.bak), and verification steps are absent. New adopters have no documentation for what the pre-commit hook does. Fix: Add 'Pre-commit PII Scrub Hook' subsection to installation guide explaining purpose (strips emails from issues.jsonl), trigger (when issues.jsonl is staged), and verification. Acceptance criteria: (1) Both hooks documented. (2) Pre-commit.bak backup mentioned. Found by: correctness-reviewer (P2), excellence-reviewer (P2).

title: Cross-file step numbering mismatch between reviews.md and big-head-skeleton.md
description: reviews.md uses Step 0/0a/1/2/3/4 for Big Head consolidation protocol while big-head-skeleton.md uses steps 1-9. No documented mapping between the two schemes. Cross-references like 'see steps 8-9' in RULES.md:103 and pantry.md:252 require consulting two files. Fix: add one-line mapping note (e.g. 'reviews.md Step 4 = skeleton steps 8-9: SendMessage to Pest Control, await verdict'). Acceptance: reader can look up any cross-file step reference and find corresponding content.

title: Add append-only progress log for Queen crash recovery
description:
Root cause: If the Queen crashes mid-workflow (context limit, timeout, API error), the next session has no structured way to determine what completed and what didn't. The user must manually inspect session artifacts, git log, and beads status to reconstruct state. This is slow, error-prone, and blocks efficient session resumption.

Fix: Give the Queen a single new responsibility — append one line to a progress log after each workflow milestone. The log is write-only during normal operation (no reads, no file opens — just echo >>). Recovery sessions read it once to determine the resume point.

File location: {session-dir}/progress.log

Format: TIMESTAMP | STEP_NAME | key=value pairs

Milestone entries (one per workflow step):
- SCOUT_COMPLETE — metadata dir path, task count, blocked count
- PANTRY_IMPL_COMPLETE — prompt count, skeleton count, file paths
- DIRT_PUSHER_COMPLETE — one entry per dirt-pusher with task ID, status (success/failed), summary path or error
- REVIEW_SLOTS_FILLED — prompts dir path
- NITPICKER_TEAM_SPAWNED — member count
- BIG_HEAD_CONSOLIDATED — report path
- PC_CHECKPOINT_VERDICT — pass/fail, details
- BEADS_FILED — count
- SESSION_CLOSE_STARTED — (marks landing-the-plane initiated)

Design properties:
- Zero read cost during normal operation: Queen never opens the file to read, only appends
- Negligible context cost: one echo >> per milestone, not per tool call
- Recovery is a single read: next session reads the log once to find last completed step
- Artifact paths embedded in each entry so recovery session knows where to find intermediate results
- Human-readable format (not JSON) for quick manual inspection

Recovery workflow (manual for now, automated recovery is a separate future issue):
- New session checks for {session-dir}/progress.log
- Reads last entry to determine resume point
- User/Queen decides whether to resume or start fresh

Affected files:
- orchestration/RULES.md — Add progress log append after each workflow step
- orchestration/templates/scout.md — No change (Queen appends after Scout returns)
- bd prime or session startup — No change for now (manual recovery)

Acceptance criteria:
1. Queen appends exactly one log entry after each workflow milestone listed above
2. Log is append-only — Queen never reads or overwrites the file during normal operation
3. Each entry includes enough context (paths, counts, status) to determine resume point
4. Progress log is written to {session-dir}/progress.log
5. Format is human-readable with pipe-delimited fields

title: fix: GLOSSARY pre-push hook entry omits sync-to-claude.sh details
description:
GLOSSARY.md:58 defines the pre-push hook as syncing "agents/*.md to ~/.claude/agents/ and orchestration/ files to ~/.claude/orchestration/" — but omits:
- The _archive/ exclusion from rsync
- That only 2 of 6 scripts (fill-review-slots.sh, compose-review-skeletons.sh) are synced
- The CLAUDE.md copy step
- The non-delete policy (preserving custom files in ~/.claude/orchestration/)

The script itself (sync-to-claude.sh:23-44) documents all of this inline. The GLOSSARY entry is a summary, not a specification, so the gaps are cosmetic — sync behavior is not a runtime decision input.

Fix: Expand the GLOSSARY pre-push hook entry to note the key behaviors.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] GLOSSARY pre-push hook entry mentions _archive/ exclusion, selective script sync, CLAUDE.md copy, and non-delete policy

Audit reference: DRIFT-023 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: WWD mode selection rule missing from checkpoints.md
description: Root cause: checkpoints.md describes serial/batch WWD modes but omits the mode selection rule (present only in RULES.md:129-130). Pest Control reads checkpoints.md at runtime and has no instruction for choosing which mode applies. Also a dual-maintenance surface: two independently written descriptions of the same feature. Fix: Add mode selection rule to checkpoints.md WWD section, add cross-reference to RULES.md Step 3 as authoritative source. Found by: Correctness (CO-F001), Excellence (EX-F002).

title: Parallelize review prompt composition with implementation via bash scripts
description:
Root cause: Review prompt composition (Pantry Section 2) currently runs as a separate LLM subagent invocation after implementation finishes, adding unnecessary latency. The review prompts are formulaic — they follow rigid templates with variable slots for commit range, changed files, and task IDs. This work is mechanical text assembly, not compositional reasoning, yet it consumes a full LLM round-trip.

Fix: Split review prompt composition into two bash scripts called at different workflow stages:

Script 1 — skeleton assembly (called by Pantry during Section 1):
- Reads reviews.md and nitpicker-skeleton.md templates
- Assembles 4 review data files + Big Head consolidation file with slot markers ({{COMMIT_RANGE}}, {{CHANGED_FILES}}, {{TASK_IDS}}, {{TIMESTAMP}})
- Writes review skeletons to {session-dir}/review-skeletons/
- Pantry verifies output files exist and have correct structure
- Pantry returns skeleton paths alongside implementation prompt paths

Script 2 — slot filling (called by Queen after dirt-pushers finish):
- Takes session dir, commit range, changed file list, task IDs, timestamp as arguments
- Runs sed/envsubst to fill slot markers with actual values
- Writes final review prompts to {session-dir}/prompts/
- Writes combined previews to {session-dir}/previews/
- Returns exit code only — Queen never reads file contents

Impact on Queen context: zero. Queen passes paths to Nitpicker team without opening files. Only an error message reaches the Queen if a script fails.

Affected files:
- pantry.md — Add Step 1.5 after impl prompt composition: call Script 1, verify output
- pantry.md Section 2 — Remove or mark as deprecated (replaced by scripts)
- RULES.md — Update post-implementation step to call Script 2 instead of spawning review Pantry
- scripts/ — New: compose-review-skeletons.sh, fill-review-slots.sh

Acceptance criteria:
1. Pantry Section 1 invocation produces both implementation prompts AND review skeletons
2. No second Pantry invocation is needed for review prompt composition
3. Queen context window absorbs zero template content from review prompt composition
4. Review prompts produced by the scripts are identical in structure to current Pantry Section 2 output
5. Script failures surface as error messages to the Queen, not silent failures

title: Review-reports directory creation timing unclear in RULES.md Step 3b
description: RULES.md Step 3b doesn't specify when to create .beads/agent-summaries/{epic-id}/review-reports/ relative to spawning Pantry (review mode). reviews.md says 'before composing review prompts' but RULES.md doesn't mention it. Add explicit directory creation to RULES.md Step 3b.

title: Step 0 wildcard glob may match stale reports from prior review cycles
README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role
nitpicker.md frontmatter description too long; second sentence ignored by Scout
description:
Root cause: agents/nitpicker.md:3 YAML description grew to 2 sentences after adding scope fence details. Scout agent discovery (Step 2.5) extracts only the first sentence. The second sentence adds no functional value in frontmatter and makes nitpicker inconsistent with other single-sentence agent descriptions.

Affected surfaces:
- agents/nitpicker.md:3

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F2), Excellence (P3 - F8)

Fix: Shorten frontmatter description to one sentence. Move details to the body.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Frontmatter description is a single sentence
- [ ] No information loss (details moved to body)

title: RULES.md accumulated style inconsistencies (headings, parenthetical, magic value)
description:
## Root Cause

`orchestration/RULES.md` has accumulated style and precision inconsistencies across multiple editing passes. Three independent surfaces in the same file share the root cause of incremental edits without a style normalization pass:

1. Sub-step heading inconsistency (line 139): Steps 0-6 use `**Step N:**` but sub-steps mix `**Step Nb:**` (top-level) with `**3b-i.**` (child), creating visual ambiguity about the nesting hierarchy.

2. Redundant parenthetical in ASCII diagram (lines 209-211): The comment `(replaces pantry-review)` next to `fill-review-slots.sh` is redundant — the deprecation is documented in GLOSSARY.md and README.md. It may confuse new readers into thinking `pantry-review` is a known alternative.

3. Magic value in session ID generation (line 383): `head -c 8` truncates the SHA hash to 8 hex chars (32 bits) with no comment explaining why this uniqueness level is sufficient.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:139` — inconsistent sub-step heading style (from Clarity C-02)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:209-211` — stale "(replaces pantry-review)" parenthetical (from Clarity C-03)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:383` — magic `head -c 8` value with no explanatory comment (from Clarity C-04)

## Fix
Standardize sub-step labels to a consistent visual hierarchy. Remove the redundant `(replaces pantry-review)` parenthetical from the diagram. Add an inline comment explaining the 8-hex-char session ID uniqueness rationale.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Standardize sub-step heading format around line 139; remove parenthetical on lines 209-211; add comment on line 383

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sub-step labels in Step 3b use a consistent format (either all `**Step Nx:**` or a documented hierarchy)
- [ ] The "(replaces pantry-review)" parenthetical is removed from the ASCII diagram
- [ ] Line 383 has an inline comment explaining why 8 hex chars is sufficient for session ID uniqueness

title: PII scrub regression: bd sync reverted email scrub in issues.jsonl
AGG-024: Rename Pantry output to eliminate data file term collision
description:
The Pantry writes orchestration artifacts called data files, but RULES.md uses project data files in the Information Diet prohibition. Despite a clarifying note, the collision persists and risks misinterpretation by cold agents.

Fix: Rename Pantry output to prompt files or task briefs and update all references across pantry.md, RULES.md, and implementation.md.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] grep for data file in pantry.md returns zero matches (replaced with new term)
- [ ] RULES.md Information Diet section has no terminological overlap with Pantry output naming
- [ ] All files referencing Pantry output use the new consistent term

title: fill-review-slots.sh: fill_all_slots block comment describes wrong map format
description: Block comment says NUL-terminated sentinel but actual format is tab-delimited newlines.

title: Skeleton format spec hints do not match Pantry actual output sections
description: The skeleton templates have format spec hints that do not exactly match the sections the Pantry actually writes. dirt-pusher-skeleton.md (line 30) says 'Sections: Context, Scope Boundaries, Focus' but the Pantry also writes a 'Summary Doc Sections' section. nitpicker-skeleton.md (line 19) says 'Sections: Scope, Files, Focus, Detailed Instructions' but the Pantry writes sections including commit range, file list, report output path, bead-filing prohibition, and messaging guidelines. Additionally, big-head-skeleton.md has no format hint at all, unlike the other two skeletons. Affected surfaces: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:30, nitpicker-skeleton.md:19, big-head-skeleton.md:53. Fix: Update skeleton hints to match Pantry output, or add '(approximate)' qualifier. Add format hint to big-head-skeleton.md.

title: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md Pre-Push checklist missing ESV gate
description:
## Root Cause
SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md "Landing the Plane Checklist" Pre-Push Verification section does not include ESV PASS as a gate before git push. RULES.md Step 5c defines ESV as a hard gate blocking git push.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:L267-L291` (from Drift review)

## Fix
Add ESV PASS checklist item before git push section.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md`: Add ESV checklist item

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pre-Push checklist includes ESV PASS (Step 5c) item

title: Big Head failure artifact timestamp coexistence under re-spawn not documented
description: The Big Head failure artifact filename is review-consolidated-{TIMESTAMP}-FAILED.md, where {TIMESTAMP} is Queen-provided. If Big Head is re-spawned after failure, a new timestamp would be provided, so the old failure artifact and new success artifact coexist (correct by design). However, this dependency on fresh timestamps at re-spawn is not documented. Affected surfaces: big-head-skeleton.md:58, big-head-skeleton.md:20. Fix: No code change needed. Add a comment noting that both -FAILED and success files may coexist after a retry cycle, and that re-spawn requires a fresh TIMESTAMP value.

title: Documentation precision gaps in GLOSSARY.md and CONTRIBUTING.md
description:
## Root Cause

Documentation reference files (GLOSSARY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md) contain dense or ambiguous prose that forces readers to infer meaning from context rather than finding it explicitly stated. Three surfaces share the pattern of documentation accumulating without a precision review pass:

1. The `pre-push hook` glossary entry (GLOSSARY.md:58) is a single run-on sentence containing six distinct facts. Every other entry in the glossary uses 1-3 short sentences; this one is a wall of text with no visual breaks.

2. The "Model Assignments table" cross-reference (CONTRIBUTING.md:31) says `orchestration/RULES.md` without a section anchor or line number. RULES.md is a long file; the table is on line 335. A contributor must search the file.

3. The rsync exclusion description (CONTRIBUTING.md:165) says "excluding `scripts/`" — ambiguous because `scripts/` at the repo root is a distinct directory from `orchestration/scripts/`. The current wording relies on context to disambiguate.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:58` — dense pre-push hook entry (from Clarity C-01)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:31` — vague "Model Assignments table" cross-reference without section anchor (from Clarity C-07)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:165` — ambiguous "scripts/" directory reference in rsync description (from Clarity C-08)

## Fix
Split the glossary entry into sub-sentences or a bulleted list. Add a section anchor or parenthetical to the CONTRIBUTING.md cross-reference. Clarify the directory path in the rsync description to specify `orchestration/scripts/`.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md`: Split the pre-push hook entry on line 58 into shorter sentences or bullets
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: Add section anchor on line 31 (e.g., `(see "Model Assignments" table)`); clarify directory path on line 165

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GLOSSARY.md pre-push hook entry is split into multiple sentences or uses a bulleted list
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md Model Assignments cross-reference includes a section name or line hint
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md rsync exclusion description unambiguously names `orchestration/scripts/`

title: Missing <IF ROUND 1> markers on placeholder guard path list in reviews.md
description: The ant-farm-l3d5 fix added a placeholder substitution guard at orchestration/templates/reviews.md:519-537 that iterates over all 4 review report paths (correctness, edge-cases, clarity, excellence) to detect unsubstituted template tokens. However, the <IF ROUND 1> / </IF ROUND 1> markers were only applied to the clarity/excellence entries in the polling while-loop body (lines 553-556), NOT to the guard's for-loop (lines 520-524). This asymmetry means Pantry may leave clarity/excellence paths with unsubstituted tokens in the guard, triggering a false-positive PLACEHOLDER_ERROR=1 and exit 1 in round 2+ sessions. Fix: Add <IF ROUND 1> / </IF ROUND 1> markers around lines 523-524 in the guard's for-loop, matching the convention used in the while-loop body.

title: Dual placeholder conventions for round-conditional blocks
description: Root cause: pantry.md uses {PANTRY_ROUND_1_CHECK_START/END} braces while reviews.md uses <IF ROUND 1> angle brackets for the same conditional construct. Risk of unresolved markers in composed briefs. Affected files: orchestration/templates/pantry.md:382-416,399-419 and orchestration/templates/reviews.md:466-545. Fix: Standardize on one marker convention. Add Pantry self-check for leftover markers. Acceptance criteria: (1) One consistent marker convention. (2) Pantry validates composed briefs for leftover markers. Found by: Excellence (EX-1, EX-5 both P3).

title: fix: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md uses stale Boss-Bot term and Claude Sonnet 4.5 model

- Line 340: "Implementation files read in boss-bot window"
- Line 342: "Boss-bot stayed focused"

"Boss-Bot" has no GLOSSARY entry and is not used anywhere else in the framework. The orchestrator role is "Queen" per GLOSSARY.md:79 and RULES.md throughout. "Claude Sonnet 4.5" is both a stale model name and the wrong tier (Queen runs on opus).

Fix: Replace all Boss-Bot/boss-bot with Queen/the Queen. Replace "Claude Sonnet 4.5" with `opus` or point to the Model Assignments table. Consider archiving SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md if it is no longer actively used (the Scout now generates briefing.md automatically).

Acceptance Criteria:

- [ ] Model reference updated to current tier

Audit reference: DRIFT-022 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Installation guide uninstall uses wrong path ~/.git/ instead of .git/
description: Root cause: docs/installation-guide.md:193 uses 'rm ~/.git/hooks/pre-push' which resolves to the user's home directory .git, not the repo's .git. Any user running this verbatim will either hit 'No such file' or delete a hook from the wrong repo. Fix: Change to 'rm .git/hooks/pre-push' (relative to repo root). Also add uninstall for pre-commit hook. Acceptance criteria: (1) Uninstall path is .git/hooks/pre-push. (2) Both hooks (pre-push and pre-commit) have uninstall instructions. Found by: clarity-reviewer (P2), excellence-reviewer (P2).

title: Incomplete 'data file' to 'task brief' rename from ant-farm-0o4
description: Root cause: Task ant-farm-0o4 required renaming Pantry output from 'data file' to 'task brief' across all files. The rename was applied to pantry.md but missed: (1) dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:41 -- agent-facing text still says 'see data file for section list', (2) big-head-skeleton.md:19 -- still says 'Big Head consolidation data file', (3) README.md lines 57-59, 70, 90, 99, 172, 174 -- 8 occurrences still use 'data file' for Pantry output. Fix: Replace 'data file' with 'task brief' in dirt-pusher-skeleton.md and README.md Pantry-output contexts. Use 'consolidation brief' for big-head-skeleton.md. Acceptance criteria: (1) No 'data file' references to Pantry output remain. (2) ant-farm-0o4 AC#3 fully met. Found by: correctness-reviewer (P2), excellence-reviewer (P3).

title: AGG-045: Specify wave parallelism and concurrency rules
description:
README and RULES say prepare next wave while current runs but theres no decision tree for mid-wave failures, early completions, or backfilling. The max 7 Dirt Pushers limit is unjustified. Retry spawn counting against the limit is unclear.

Fix: Add wave management section to RULES.md: justify 7-agent limit, specify retry counting, provide decision tree for failures and backfilling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md contains a Wave Management section with the 7-agent limit justification
- [ ] Retry spawns are explicitly counted (or not) against the concurrent agent limit
- [ ] A decision tree or flowchart covers mid-wave failure, early completion, and backfill scenarios

title: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md bd close ordering creates failure window before summary doc
description:
Root cause: The 'bd close {TASK_ID}' instruction in dirt-pusher-skeleton.md appears as a standalone line after the 6-step numbered list (line 42), structurally disconnected from the step sequence. The instruction is ambiguous about when it executes relative to Step 6 (write summary doc). If an agent closes the bead before writing the summary doc and then crashes, the task is marked closed with no summary artifact. Checkpoint B (checkpoints.md:246) requires reading the summary doc and would find nothing, but the task is already closed.

Affected surfaces:
- dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:42 -- 'After committing: bd close {TASK_ID}' appears orphaned after step list

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 10, P3), Edge Cases (Finding 6, P2)
Edge-cases reviewer rated P2 due to the failure window between bead closure and summary doc creation. Taking highest: P2.

Fix: Move 'bd close {TASK_ID}' to an explicit position after Step 6 with a note: 'Close the bead ONLY after the summary doc is successfully written.' Consider making it Step 7.

Acceptance criteria:
- bd close instruction is explicitly sequenced after summary doc write (Step 6)
- Either incorporated into a numbered step or clearly labeled as post-Step-6
- No ambiguity about when bead closure occurs relative to artifact creation

Crash recovery missing session directory existence check
description:
## Root Cause
The crash recovery block in `RULES.md:L64-L75` calls `parse-progress-log.sh` without first verifying the session directory exists on disk. If the directory was pruned, the script exits with an opaque "cannot read log" error instead of a diagnostic message.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L64-L75` — crash recovery detection block (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Add `[ -d "${prior_SESSION_DIR}" ] || echo "Session directory not found: ${prior_SESSION_DIR}"` pre-check before running the script.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Add directory existence check before calling parse-progress-log.sh in the crash recovery block

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A missing session directory produces a clear "Session directory not found" error before the script runs
- [ ] The existing exit-code handling for parse-progress-log.sh remains unchanged
- [ ] The Queen's error message to the user includes the specific path that was not found

title: nitpicker.md NOT YOUR RESPONSIBILITY blocks use inconsistent scope vocabulary

For example, the Clarity block describes Excellence scope as 'performance inefficiencies, security vulnerabilities, architecture concerns, scalability issues, best practice violations' while the Correctness block says 'performance of correct code, architectural elegance, security hardening beyond functional correctness.' Same domain, different terms.

Affected surfaces:

Fix: Unify the ownership language so the same domain always uses the same shorthand labels across all 4 blocks.

Acceptance criteria:

title: Pest Control timeout failure artifact destructively overwrites existing consolidated summary
description:
## Root Cause

The Pest Control timeout bash block at `reviews.md:777` writes a failure stub to `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` -- the same path where Big Head has already written the full consolidated summary in a previous workflow step. By the time the Pest Control timeout fires, consolidation is complete and the full report (root cause groups, deduplication log, priority breakdown) already exists at that path. The failure artifact write uses `cat >` (truncate-and-write), which overwrites and permanently destroys the consolidated findings.

The failure artifact convention (defined in `big-head-skeleton.md:77-86`) is designed for cases where consolidation has NOT yet produced output (e.g., polling timeout before reports arrive). At Pest Control timeout, consolidation is complete and output already exists -- the convention does not apply here, yet the fix for ant-farm-84qf mechanically applied the same pattern to all failure paths without distinguishing pre-consolidation from post-consolidation stages.

Contrast with the polling-timeout bash block (`reviews.md:588`): that block correctly writes the failure artifact BEFORE any consolidated output exists, so overwriting is safe there.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:777` -- Pest Control timeout bash block overwrites the existing consolidated summary

## Fix

Do not overwrite the already-written consolidated summary. Instead, write the Pest Control timeout failure artifact to a separate file (e.g., `review-consolidated-<timestamp>-pc-timeout.md`) so the consolidated findings survive. Update the escalation message to Queen to provide both paths: the consolidated summary path (full findings) and the timeout failure path (Pest Control status).

Alternatively, append a failure status section to the existing consolidated summary rather than overwriting it, using `cat >>` instead of `cat >`.

## Changes Needed

1. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:~777` -- Change the `cat >` target to a separate file path (e.g., append `-pc-timeout` before the extension), or change `cat >` to `cat >>` to append rather than overwrite
2. Update the escalation SendMessage text (near L785) to reference both the consolidated summary path and the timeout artifact path

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] When Pest Control times out, the full consolidated summary written in a previous step is preserved at its original path
- [ ] A separate failure artifact or appended section captures the Pest Control timeout status
- [ ] The escalation message to Queen provides enough information to locate both the consolidated findings and the timeout status

title: [Review RC-6] big-head-skeleton.md references stale TIMED_OUT variable
description: P2: big-head-skeleton.md:91 references TIMED_OUT variable but reviews.md commit 306a457 renamed it to REPORTS_FOUND. This stale reference will cause confusion if anyone reads the skeleton. Fix: update to REPORTS_FOUND.

title: Canonical term definitions copy-pasted across 3 templates with no single source of truth
[OUT-OF-SCOPE] tag has no enforcement in Big Head severity merging logic
description:
## Root Cause

Round 2+ reviewer instructions define an `[OUT-OF-SCOPE]` tag for findings outside fix-verification scope, but Big Head treats all findings identically for dedup and root-cause grouping regardless of tag. There is no mechanism to prevent out-of-scope P3 findings from inflating the priority of a root-cause group when merged with in-scope findings. The tag is informational only with no enforcement path in the consolidation workflow.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L199-207` -- Round 2+ Reviewer Instructions define `[OUT-OF-SCOPE]` tag usage
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L208` -- States "Big Head treats all findings identically for dedup and root-cause grouping regardless of tag"
- Big Head consolidation workflow (both `big-head-skeleton.md` and `reviews.md` Big Head protocol) -- no filtering or special handling for `[OUT-OF-SCOPE]` tagged findings during severity merging

## Fix

Add an explicit instruction to Big Head's consolidation protocol: when merging findings into a root-cause group, only use in-scope severity levels for priority calculation. `[OUT-OF-SCOPE]` findings contribute affected surfaces and context to the root-cause group but do not influence the group's combined priority level.

Example addition to Big Head instructions:
> "If an [OUT-OF-SCOPE] finding is merged with an in-scope finding in the same root-cause group, do NOT use the out-of-scope finding's severity when computing the group's combined priority. Use only in-scope severities. The out-of-scope finding's affected surfaces are still listed in the group."

## Changes Needed

1. `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md` -- Add [OUT-OF-SCOPE] severity filtering instruction to the root-cause grouping step
2. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md` -- Add the same instruction to the Big Head Consolidation Protocol section
3. `agents/big-head.md` -- Add a note about [OUT-OF-SCOPE] tag handling in the "Watch for" section (priority inflation bullet)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Big Head instructions explicitly state that [OUT-OF-SCOPE] findings do not contribute severity to root-cause group priority
- The rule appears in both the skeleton and reviews.md Big Head protocol
- A round 2+ consolidation with mixed in-scope P2 and out-of-scope P3 findings in the same root-cause group would use P2 (not inflate beyond P2 based on the P3)

title: Pantry-review deprecation not fully propagated to reader attributions
description:
## Root Cause

When `pantry-review` was deprecated and replaced by `build-review-prompts.sh`, the "who reads reviews.md" attribution was not updated in multiple reference tables and prose sections. The deprecation was acknowledged with strikethrough markup, but reader attributions still name the Pantry as the consumer of `reviews.md`.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:47` -- FORBIDDEN reads list says Pantry reads reviews.md (from drift review)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:440` -- Template Lookup says "read by the Pantry" (from drift review)
- `README.md:252` -- Information diet prose says Pantry reads reviews.md (from drift review)
- `README.md:301` -- Deprecated pantry-review row lacks cross-ref to replacement (from clarity review)
- `README.md:352` -- File reference table says "The Pantry (review mode)" (from drift review)
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:28` -- pantry-review.md listed without removal status (from clarity review)
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:82` -- Pantry role mixes active/deprecated file; says "Reads implementation or review templates" (from clarity+drift review)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:95` -- Template inventory "Read by" mentions deprecated Pantry review mode (from clarity review)

## Fix

Update all reader attributions to name `build-review-prompts.sh` as the replacement. Remove or clearly annotate deprecated pantry-review entries. Update GLOSSARY Pantry role description to "Reads implementation templates" only.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md:47`: change reader to `build-review-prompts.sh` at Step 3b-ii
- `orchestration/RULES.md:440`: change "read by the Pantry" to "read by `build-review-prompts.sh` at Step 3b"
- `README.md:252`: split Pantry attribution; reviews.md is read by `build-review-prompts.sh`
- `README.md:301`: add cross-reference to `fill-review-slots.sh`/`build-review-prompts.sh`
- `README.md:352`: change "The Pantry (review mode)" to "`build-review-prompts.sh` (Step 3b-ii)"
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:28`: clarify pantry-review.md is removed/no longer deployed
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:82`: change "Reads implementation or review templates" to "Reads implementation templates"
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:95`: remove Pantry from "Read by" column for reviews.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No file in the repo attributes reviews.md readership to the Pantry
- [ ] GLOSSARY Pantry role description says "Reads implementation templates" (not "or review templates")
- [ ] README file reference table names `build-review-prompts.sh` as the reader of reviews.md

title: SESSION_ID collision: same-second Queens produce identical session directory
Bead metadata / traceability inconsistencies (commit msg, acceptance criteria, line numbers)
Strengthen IF ROUND 1 markers from interpretive to executable
description: The <IF ROUND 1> / </IF ROUND 1> markers in reviews.md are bash comments interpreted by an LLM (Big Head/Pantry) rather than executed by a script. If the LLM fails to strip the block in round 2+, the placeholder guard falsely flags clarity/excellence paths, causing PLACEHOLDER_ERROR halt. Affects: orchestration/templates/reviews.md:533-547 (placeholder guard) and reviews.md:566-569 (polling loop body, pre-existing). Failure mode is loud halt, not silent wrong behavior. Future improvement: make the conditional blocks executable (processed by a script) rather than relying on LLM interpretation.

title: parse-progress-log.sh bash/zsh incompatibility breaks crash recovery on macOS
description: Root cause: scripts/parse-progress-log.sh uses declare -A (bash 4+ associative arrays) but ships with #\!/usr/bin/env bash. On macOS, bash 3.2 (no associative array support) and zsh (different associative array semantics) are the available shells. The script's crash recovery function is completely broken on the primary target platform. Bash 3.2: declare -A fails with exit code 2, which RULES.md Step 0 interprets as 'session completed' — recovery silently skipped. Zsh: declare -A runs but all array lookups fail silently — all steps show as pending, resume always starts at step 0. Affected files: scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:25,113-115,130-133,179. Fix: Add bash version guard (exit 1 on bash < 4) or rewrite step detection using POSIX-compatible arrays/grep. Acceptance criteria: (1) Script exits with clear error on bash < 4 (not exit code 2), OR script works on bash 3.2 and zsh. (2) All step detection, completion check, and resume point determination produce correct results on macOS. Found by: Edge Cases (EC-1 P1, EC-2 P1), Correctness (CR-7 P1), Excellence (EX-3 P3). Severity conflict: P1 vs P3 (2-level gap) — flagged for calibration review.

title: Checkpoints epic ID resolution table is overspecified and ambiguous
description: checkpoints.md provides an epic ID resolution table but mixes epic ID format (3-char) with task ID patterns (full format). Does not clearly handle standalone tasks (e.g. hs_website-596y with no .N suffix). Simplify to plain English with annotated examples.

title: fill-review-slots.sh fill_slot spawns separate awk per slot substitution
description:
Root cause: fill-review-slots.sh:151-183 fill_slot is called once per slot, spawning awk + temp file I/O each time. For round 1: 7 slots x 4 types + 5 for big-head = 33 awk invocations. Could be simplified to a single pass per file.

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/fill-review-slots.sh:151-183

Found by: Excellence (P3 - F4). Single reviewer, standalone finding. Performance polish item.

Fix: Combine all slot fills into a single awk pass per file, or accumulate substitutions and apply once.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Slot filling uses fewer process spawns per file
- [ ] Output is identical to current approach

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md Tier 2 {session-dir} vs Tier 1 {SESSION_DIR} naming ambiguity

The duplication means path format changes must be updated in two places within the same template.

Pass 1-C: Verify 34 beads against reviews.md and big-head templates
description:
Scope: Verify 34 open beads that reference reviews.md, big-head-skeleton.md, big-head.md, or compose-review-skeletons.sh against the current file state.

Bead IDs: ant-farm-0bez, ant-farm-0kwo, ant-farm-10ff, ant-farm-1s5k, ant-farm-3jiq, ant-farm-4ome, ant-farm-58f1, ant-farm-5n8h, ant-farm-5xo7, ant-farm-6i1, ant-farm-93n, ant-farm-c606, ant-farm-ees2, ant-farm-ek7x, ant-farm-ew0, ant-farm-hm05, ant-farm-hpgz, ant-farm-igem, ant-farm-lbcy, ant-farm-ldha, ant-farm-mv6b, ant-farm-n0dw, ant-farm-ng0e, ant-farm-o2zl, ant-farm-ogyk, ant-farm-oi3, ant-farm-p0m, ant-farm-retj, ant-farm-ut66, ant-farm-uu8u, ant-farm-w1w8, ant-farm-wk1a, ant-farm-wqkm, ant-farm-z1qp

Codebase files to read: orchestration/templates/reviews.md (primary), orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md, agents/big-head.md, orchestration/PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md (for ant-farm-lbcy cross-ref)

Context: This is the largest batch. These beads cover review pipeline issues: polling loop placeholders, section ordering, skeleton format mismatches, compose-review-skeletons.sh issues (note: this script was merged into scripts/build-review-prompts.sh in commit 1b0037e — beads referencing the old script name may be ALREADY_FIXED or need re-scoping), Big Head failure artifacts, Nitpicker cross-review messaging, and placeholder convention gaps. Known exact duplicates: ant-farm-4ome/ant-farm-n0dw (polling loop placeholders), ant-farm-58f1/ant-farm-hpgz (slot marker comment), ant-farm-c606/ant-farm-wqkm (SESSION_DIR check), ant-farm-ees2/ant-farm-z1qp (exit 1 redundancy), ant-farm-ek7x/ant-farm-w1w8 (section ordering).

IMPORTANT: compose-review-skeletons.sh was merged into build-review-prompts.sh (commit 1b0037e). Beads referencing compose-review-skeletons.sh need special attention — the issue may be fixed, moved to the new file, or irrelevant.

Process for each bead:
1. Read the bead's full record from .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-C-input.jsonl
2. Read the bead's title and description (18 of 34 lack descriptions — infer intent from title)
3. Check the referenced location(s) in the current codebase
4. Assign verdict: STILL_VALID, ALREADY_FIXED, IRRELEVANT, NEEDS_CONTEXT, or DUPLICATE_SUSPECT
5. For DUPLICATE_SUSPECT, check against .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/all-bead-titles.txt

Output: Write JSON array to .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-C-output.json

Per-bead output schema: {"bead_id": "...", "title": "...", "verdict": "...", "confidence": "...", "evidence": "...", "priority_check": "...", "duplicate_of": null | "...", "notes": ""}

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Output file contains exactly 34 entries
- [ ] Output is valid JSON array
- [ ] Beads referencing compose-review-skeletons.sh note that the script was merged into build-review-prompts.sh
- [ ] Known duplicates (5 pairs) are marked DUPLICATE_SUSPECT with cross-references
- [ ] Every ALREADY_FIXED verdict cites specific evidence

title: parse-progress-log.sh trap ordering -- trap installed after map_init
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md uses stale timestamp format HHMMSS
Pre-commit hook blocks ALL commits when scrub-pii.sh is missing (ordering bug)
description: The installed pre-commit hook checks for scrub-pii.sh existence/executability (install-hooks.sh:74-77) BEFORE the staged-file guard (line 80) that checks whether issues.jsonl is even staged. ALL commits are blocked when the script is absent. Additionally, docs/installation-guide.md:47 says 'skips silently' contradicting the actual blocking behavior. Fix: move the executable check inside the staged-file guard; update installation-guide.md:47 to document blocking behavior. Consolidated from: Clarity F1, Edge Cases F1, Edge Cases F4, Excellence F3, Correctness F5.

title: Remove _standalone references from scout.md metadata templates
description:
scout.md still references _standalone in its per-task metadata format (line 74: 'Epic: {epic-id or _standalone}') and session summary (line 165: 'use _standalone for tasks not in any epic'). The _standalone artifact directory concept was removed in commits dabb764/f838cef (session-scoping shift).

Fix: Remove _standalone from scout.md metadata templates. Tasks without an epic parent should show 'Epic: none' or omit the field. Keep {epic-id} as a context field since the Queen still passes epic IDs to agents for context.

Acceptance Criteria:

- [ ] Tasks without epic parents are handled gracefully (no placeholder value needed)
- [ ] {epic-id} remains available as context metadata for tasks that do have an epic parent

title: install-hooks.sh backup uses fixed filename, losing backup history on re-run
install-hooks.sh generated hook lacks descriptive error on sync script failure
AGG-002: Fix pre-push hook not being versioned or installable
description:
The repo claims agents sync on git push via pre-push hook, but .git/hooks/pre-push is local-only outside version control. No installer script, no documented symlink step, no explanation of .beads/hooks/pre-push vs .git/hooks/pre-push relationship.

Fix: Provide a tracked hook installer script (or documented symlink step) and clarify the hook file relationships.

Hardcoded line references and incomplete sync descriptions drift
description:
## Root Cause

Cross-file references use hardcoded line numbers or incomplete descriptions that become stale as target files are edited. This differs from ant-farm-9d4e (which covers step number references in templates) -- this bead covers file line-number ranges in cross-file references and incomplete sync behavior descriptions.

## Affected Surfaces

- `CONTRIBUTING.md:42` -- line range "77-85" for GLOSSARY Ant Metaphor Roles table is stale; table now ends at line 87 (from drift review)
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:58` -- pre-push hook description omits `scripts/` from rsync exclusion list (from drift review)

## Fix

Update CONTRIBUTING.md line range or remove hardcoded line numbers entirely. Add `scripts/` to the GLOSSARY rsync exclusion description.

## Changes Needed

- `CONTRIBUTING.md:42`: Update line range to match current GLOSSARY, or remove hardcoded line numbers and just reference "the Ant Metaphor Roles table"
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:58`: Add "and `scripts/`" to the rsync exclusion description

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md line reference for GLOSSARY table matches the actual table extent, or uses a name-based reference

title: pantry-review.md (archived) tense inconsistency
AGG-043: Fix stale and inconsistent README file reference table
description:
The README File Reference table has issues: queen-state.md described as schema but is a template, Read by column uses inconsistent casing, path prefixes mix conventions, and the table doesnt match RULES.md read-ownership rules.

Fix: Audit all entries for accuracy, standardize casing, ensure consistent path prefixes from repo root.

title: AGG-027: Standardize review type naming between display titles and short names
description:
reviews.md uses Correctness Redux as a display title while filenames and skeleton placeholders use correctness. This inconsistency increases parsing friction in chained prompts.

Fix: Standardize on either the display title or short name consistently in templates and filenames. Document the mapping if both forms are needed.

title: scrub-pii.sh assumes perl is installed without checking
description: Root cause: scripts/scrub-pii.sh:50 uses 'perl -i -pe' without checking if perl is available. In minimal Docker/CI environments, perl may be absent. The script would silently fail to scrub, and the post-scrub check would report 'email patterns still present' without indicating perl was missing. Fix: Add 'command -v perl >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo ERROR; exit 1; }' at script top. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: fix: scrub-pii.sh and pre-commit hook not described in SETUP.md or README.md
description:
The pre-commit hook runs scripts/scrub-pii.sh to strip email addresses from .beads/issues.jsonl before staging. This is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md:176-178 but absent from SETUP.md and README.md.

install-hooks.sh installs both hooks (pre-push for sync, pre-commit for PII scrub). Developers following SETUP.md will learn about the pre-push hook but not the pre-commit hook. They may not understand warnings from the pre-commit hook or why PII scrubbing exists.

Fix: Add a note to SETUP.md Quick Setup section explaining both hooks are installed.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] SETUP.md mentions both pre-push (sync) and pre-commit (PII scrub) hooks
- [ ] README.md optionally mentions PII scrubbing in the setup section

Audit reference: DRIFT-032 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: extract_agent_section "exactly one delimiter" assumption unguarded
compose-review-skeletons.sh slot marker comment omits TASK_IDS
Crash recovery dir-check template uses unquoted path variable
description:
## Root Cause
The fix for ant-farm-1rof (crash recovery missing session directory existence check) added a dir-check at RULES.md:70-73 using `[ -d "<prior_SESSION_DIR>" ]`. The angle-bracket placeholder is a template variable filled by the Queen at runtime. The template does not show the path as a properly-quoted shell variable, creating a minor quoting gap if the path were to contain spaces or metacharacters. In practice, session directory names follow the `_session-YYYYMMDD-HHmmss` format, so risk is negligible.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:70-73` -- unquoted path variable in dir-check template (from Edge Cases review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dir-check template uses properly-quoted shell variable syntax consistent with SESSION_DIR usage elsewhere in RULES.md

title: pantry.md Review Mode does not generate Big Head preview file for CCO audit
description:
Root cause: pantry.md Review Mode Step 5 creates preview files for the 4 Nitpicker review types by combining nitpicker-skeleton.md with each data file. However, no preview is generated for Big Head's consolidation prompt. If CCO is meant to audit all prompts before team creation, the Big Head prompt is excluded from preview-based auditing.

Affected surfaces:
- pantry.md:137-146 -- Step 5 (Write Combined Review Previews) omits Big Head

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 9, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 completeness item.

Fix: Either (a) add a Step 5b to create a Big Head preview, or (b) explicitly document that Big Head's prompt is excluded from CCO preview audit and explain why.

Acceptance criteria:
- Either a Big Head preview is generated, or the exclusion is explicitly documented with rationale

title: AGG-006: Create installation and safety documentation for hook/sync workflow
description:
No single document explains how to install the pre-push hook, what the sync script overwrites, how to back up ~/.claude/ content, or how to uninstall the orchestration wiring. Information is scattered across SETUP.md, the sync script, and the hook file.

Fix: Create a dedicated installation guide covering hook installation, sync behavior, backup strategy, and uninstall steps.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A dedicated installation guide document exists with all four sections (install, sync, backup, uninstall)
- [ ] The guide includes exact commands for each step that work on a fresh clone
- [ ] SETUP.md cross-references the new guide instead of duplicating scattered instructions

title: parse-progress-log.sh no warning for unrecognized step key in log
description:
## Root Cause
parse-progress-log.sh log parsing loop accepts any step_key with a valid timestamp but emits no warning when the step_key is not in the known STEP_KEYS set, making corrupted logs harder to debug.

## Affected Surfaces

Add stderr warning when step_key is not in known set.

## Changes Needed

## Acceptance Criteria
