title: AGG-039: Add complete error recovery procedures
description:
The retry limits table omits Pantry and Scout entirely. No retry path exists for Pantry CCO failures. Stuck-agent escalation lacks diagnostic steps. No wave-level failure threshold exists.

Fix: Add Pantry/Scout to retry table (1 retry, then escalate). Add stuck-agent diagnostic procedure. Add wave failure threshold: if >50% fail, pause and present batch summary to user.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md retry limits table includes entries for Pantry and Scout with retry counts
- [ ] A step-by-step stuck-agent diagnostic procedure is documented (check summary doc, git log, etc.)
- [ ] A wave-level failure threshold (e.g., >50%) triggers pause and user notification

title: Bash-specific parameter expansion in RULES.md inline validation code
description:
## Root Cause

`orchestration/RULES.md:154-173` contains a validation block that uses `${VAR//[[:space:]]/}` — a bash-specific parameter expansion — in inline prose-embedded shell code intended to be interpreted by the Queen. The execution environment is guaranteed bash (the Queen runs bash), so this is not a functional bug. However, the code is expressed as inline instructions that could be copy-pasted into a `#!/bin/sh` context, where `[[:space:]]` in `${VAR//...}` behaves differently. The comment on line 162 notes the bash dependency but the implicit assumption is not obvious to a reader unfamiliar with the distinction.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:154-173` — bash-specific `${VAR//[[:space:]]/}` in inline validation code (from Edge Cases EC-004)

## Fix
No functional change required — the current code works correctly in the guaranteed bash environment. Optionally, add a brief comment noting the bash requirement more prominently, or use a POSIX-compatible alternative if portability becomes a goal.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Optionally add a comment near lines 154-173 noting the bash-specific expansion, or leave as-is given the existing comment on line 162

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The bash dependency for the `${VAR//[[:space:]]/}` pattern is either documented with a visible comment or replaced with a POSIX alternative

title: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md convention mismatches: emoji risk labels and time estimates
description:
## Root Cause
SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md uses emoji risk labels and time estimate fields that contradict codebase conventions (no emoji, no time estimates). Since this template is designed to be copied, these conflicts will propagate.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:L45-L47` -- emoji risk labels (from Clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:L64, L68, L72, L85, L90, L97, L105` -- time estimate fields (from Clarity review)

## Fix
Replace emoji risk labels with plain text. Remove or annotate time estimate fields.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md`: Replace emoji, address time estimates

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No emoji in risk labels
- [ ] Time estimate fields removed or annotated as violating convention

title: Minor formatting inconsistencies in checkpoints.md, scout.md, big-head-skeleton.md
reviews.md Round 2+ spawn instructions contradict persistent-team model
description:
## Root Cause
`reviews.md` lines 82 and 934 still instruct the Queen to create a new Nitpicker team for Round 2+. This contradicts `RULES.md:226` which now explicitly says "Round 2+: do NOT spawn a new team — re-task Correctness and Edge Cases reviewers via SendMessage." The one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint means following reviews.md's instruction would silently fail or shadow the persisted team.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:82` — Round 2+ team creation instruction (from drift review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:934` — Nitpicker Checklist Round 2+ instruction (from drift review)

## Fix
Update both locations to replace team-creation instructions with re-tasking-via-SendMessage instructions consistent with `RULES.md:226`.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Update line 82 and line 934 to say the team is persistent and reviewers are re-tasked via SendMessage, not spawned fresh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] reviews.md line 82 no longer instructs creating a new team for Round 2+
- [ ] reviews.md line 934 no longer instructs creating a new team for Round 2+
- [ ] Both locations reference the persistent-team model and SendMessage re-tasking consistent with RULES.md:226

title: Mechanism descriptions accurate but incomplete — readers must infer missing pieces
description:
## Root Cause

Several cross-references and mechanism descriptions are substantively correct but omit enough detail that readers must infer the missing pieces. This includes under-explained parentheticals in pipeline mechanics, compound table entries that break formatting patterns, misleading "embed" instructions that imply manual work when the mechanism is automatic, and imprecise cross-file section references.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:L129` -- "(single message)" parenthetical and "wave N verification done" under-explained (clarity review)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L386` -- Hard Gates Reviews row compound entry breaks table pattern (clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L56-59` -- "Embed report paths" implies manual embedding when automatic (clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L123` -- "reviews.md Step 4" imprecise cross-reference (drift review)

## Fix

1. Expand "(single message)" to "**in a single Task call to achieve concurrency**"
2. Expand "wave N verification done" to "wave N WWD + DMVDC both PASS"
3. Split Hard Gates Reviews row notes into a separate annotation below the table; change "Blocks" to "Presenting findings to user (Step 3c)"
4. Rewrite big-head-skeleton.md Step 3 to: "Big Head's spawn prompt already includes `{DATA_FILE_PATH}` pointing to the consolidation brief written by build-review-prompts.sh, which contains all expected report paths. No manual path embedding or follow-up SendMessage is required."
5. Change cross-reference to "reviews.md (Big Head Consolidation Protocol > Step 4: Checkpoint Gate)"

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Expand wave pipelining descriptions at ~L129; restructure Hard Gates Reviews row at ~L386
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md`: Rewrite Step 3 at ~L56; update cross-reference at ~L123

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Wave pipelining step 1 explicitly says "in a single Task call" instead of "(single message)"
- [ ] Wave pipelining step 4 says "wave N WWD + DMVDC both PASS" instead of "wave N verification done"
- [ ] Hard Gates Reviews row has a single-line Artifact entry; additional notes are in a separate annotation
- [ ] big-head-skeleton.md Step 3 does not imply manual path embedding
- [ ] big-head-skeleton.md cross-reference to reviews.md includes the full section path

title: Big Head Step 0 glob matching can silently consolidate stale reports from prior review rounds
SESSION_DIR variable not quoted in shell mkdir command in RULES.md
description:
Root cause: RULES.md line 115 uses mkdir -p ${SESSION_DIR}/{task-metadata,previews,prompts} without quoting ${SESSION_DIR}. While the value is a hash (no spaces), shell best practice is to quote variable expansions.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:115 -- unquoted ${SESSION_DIR} in mkdir command

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 5, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 best-practice polish item.

Fix: Change to mkdir -p "${SESSION_DIR}"/{task-metadata,previews,prompts}

Acceptance criteria:
- SESSION_DIR is quoted in the mkdir command

title: Create Scribe skeleton template
description:
Create the Scribe agent's instruction template at orchestration/templates/scribe-skeleton.md. The Scribe is a general-purpose subagent (model: sonnet) spawned by the Queen at Step 5b to produce a 1-page session executive summary and derive a CHANGELOG entry from it. The template follows the same structural pattern as dirt-pusher-skeleton.md (Step 0: read data file, then execute instructions).

The Scribe receives 4 inputs from the Queen via its spawn prompt: session directory path, commit range, open bead IDs, and CHANGELOG.md path. It reads 7 source types: briefing.md (what was planned), summaries/*.md (what each agent reported), review-reports/review-consolidated-*.md (review findings), progress.log (timeline/duration), git diff --stat (files changed), git log --oneline (commit messages), and bd show on open beads (what's still open).

It produces two outputs:
1. {SESSION_DIR}/exec-summary.md — hybrid format with structured 'At a Glance' metrics table followed by 4 body sections (Work Completed, Review Findings, Open Issues, Observations narrative)
2. CHANGELOG.md entry — derived from the exec summary, prepended to the existing file, matching the project's established CHANGELOG convention (session ID + date header, summary line, work completed bullets, review stats table). The CHANGELOG omits the Observations narrative and Open Issues sections.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] File orchestration/templates/scribe-skeleton.md exists and is valid markdown
- [ ] Template includes Step 0 (read data file) matching the pattern in dirt-pusher-skeleton.md lines 28-31
- [ ] Template specifies all 7 input sources to read (briefing.md, summaries/*.md, review-consolidated-*.md, progress.log, git diff --stat, git log --oneline, bd show)
- [ ] Template includes the exact exec-summary.md output format with all 5 required sections: At a Glance (metrics table), Work Completed, Review Findings, Open Issues, Observations
- [ ] Template includes CHANGELOG derivation rules specifying what to include (session header, summary, work completed, review stats) and what to omit (Observations, Open Issues)
- [ ] Template's CHANGELOG format matches the existing convention observable in CHANGELOG.md (verify by reading the current file)
- [ ] Template includes a duration calculation instruction (derive from progress.log first and last timestamps)
- [ ] No placeholder text or TODOs remain in the template — it is ready for agent consumption

title: Add ESV checkpoint to checkpoints.md
description:
Add an Exec Summary Verification (ESV) section to orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md. ESV is a Pest Control checkpoint (model: haiku) that acts as a hard gate before git push at Step 5c. It verifies the Scribe's exec-summary.md and CHANGELOG.md outputs with 6 mechanical checks.

The ESV section must follow the structural pattern of existing checkpoints in the file (WWD at ~line 230, DMVDC at ~line 300, CCB at ~line 470). Each existing checkpoint has: a header, overview paragraph, numbered checks with inputs/pass-conditions, a verdict format, and an artifact output path.

The 6 ESV checks:
1. Task coverage — every task ID from briefing.md or progress.log appears in exec-summary.md
2. Commit coverage — every commit hash from git log --oneline <range> is accounted for in exec-summary.md
3. Open bead accuracy — beads listed as open in exec-summary.md are actually open (bd show cross-check); no unlisted open beads from the session
4. CHANGELOG derivation fidelity — every task ID and commit in exec-summary.md also appears in the new CHANGELOG.md entry
5. Section completeness — all 5 required sections present in exec-summary.md (At a Glance, Work Completed, Review Findings, Open Issues, Observations)
6. Metric consistency — counts in the At a Glance table match actual item counts in the body sections (e.g., Tasks completed: 3 matches 3 bullets in Work Completed)

Artifact output: {SESSION_DIR}/pc/pc-session-esv-{timestamp}.md

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] ESV section added to checkpoints.md with all 6 checks documented (task coverage, commit coverage, open bead accuracy, CHANGELOG fidelity, section completeness, metric consistency)
- [ ] Each check specifies: what inputs the PC agent reads, the exact pass condition, and the failure output format
- [ ] ESV section structure matches the pattern of existing checkpoints (WWD, DMVDC, CCB) — header, overview, numbered checks, verdict format, artifact path
- [ ] Artifact output path documented as {SESSION_DIR}/pc/pc-session-esv-{timestamp}.md
- [ ] Verdict format includes per-check PASS/FAIL status and an overall PASS/FAIL verdict
- [ ] The bd show guard pattern from DMVDC Check 2 (checkpoints.md ~line 332) is applied to the open bead accuracy check (Check 3)

title: Update RULES.md for Scribe workflow
description:
Update orchestration/RULES.md to incorporate the Scribe (Step 5b) and ESV (Step 5c) into the session workflow. This is the authoritative workflow document — all other files reference it.

Changes required:

1. Step 4 (line ~288): Remove CHANGELOG from the documentation step. Change 'update CHANGELOG, README, CLAUDE.md in single commit' to 'update README, CLAUDE.md in single commit (CHANGELOG is written by the Scribe at Step 5b)'.

2. Step 5 (line ~295): Update cross-reference text 'all tasks have CHANGELOG entries' since CHANGELOG is now authored at Step 5b. Change to 'all tasks accounted for in session artifacts'.

3. Add Step 5b (after Step 5): Scribe spawn instructions — subagent_type=general-purpose, model=sonnet. Queen passes: session directory, commit range, open bead IDs, CHANGELOG.md path. Queen reads scribe-skeleton.md once (add to Queen Read Permissions as 'permitted, once per session'). Add progress log entry: {timestamp}|SCRIBE_COMPLETE|exec_summary={SESSION_DIR}/exec-summary.md

4. Add Step 5c (after Step 5b): ESV spawn instructions — subagent_type=pest-control, model=haiku. Hard gate. Add progress log entry: {timestamp}|ESV_PASS|artifact={SESSION_DIR}/pc/pc-session-esv-{timestamp}.md

5. Step 6 (line ~299): Add 'Queen commits Scribe CHANGELOG.md changes' before pull/push sequence. Add git add CHANGELOG.md && git commit instruction.

6. Hard Gates table (line ~302): Add row — ESV PASS | Git push (Step 6) | {SESSION_DIR}/pc/pc-session-esv-{timestamp}.md

7. Retry Limits table (line ~452): Add row — Scribe fails ESV | 1 | Escalate to user with ESV report

8. Agent Types table (line ~327): Add row — Scribe | general-purpose | Retrospective summary + CHANGELOG authoring

9. Model Assignments table (line ~340): Add rows — Scribe | Task (general-purpose) | sonnet | Retrospective only; PC — ESV | Task (pest-control) | haiku | Mechanical checklist

10. Template Lookup table (line ~434): Add row — Scribe skeleton for spawning (Step 5b) | orchestration/templates/scribe-skeleton.md

11. Session Directory section (line ~392): Add exec-summary.md to root-level artifacts list

12. Concurrency Rules (line ~360): Add 'Only the Scribe updates CHANGELOG.md' (replaces current 'Only the Queen updates documentation files' for CHANGELOG specifically)

13. Queen Read Permissions: Add scribe-skeleton.md to PERMITTED (once per session) list. Add exec-summary.md to PERMITTED list.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Step 4 no longer mentions CHANGELOG authoring — grep 'Step 4' in RULES.md returns no CHANGELOG reference
- [ ] Step 5b exists with Scribe spawn instructions (subagent_type=general-purpose, model=sonnet)
- [ ] Step 5c exists with ESV spawn instructions (subagent_type=pest-control, model=haiku)
- [ ] Hard Gates table includes ESV PASS row blocking git push
- [ ] Retry Limits table includes 'Scribe fails ESV: 1 retry' row
- [ ] Agent Types and Model Assignments tables include Scribe and PC-ESV entries
- [ ] Template Lookup table includes scribe-skeleton.md
- [ ] Progress log entries for SCRIBE_COMPLETE and ESV_PASS are documented
- [ ] Session Directory section lists exec-summary.md as a root-level artifact

title: Update crash recovery script for Scribe progress log entries
description:
Update scripts/parse-progress-log.sh to recognize the two new progress log milestones added by the Scribe workflow: SCRIBE_COMPLETE and ESV_PASS. Without this update, crash recovery will not correctly identify incomplete Scribe/ESV steps when resuming a session.

The script maintains a STEP_KEYS array (or equivalent) that lists all valid progress log milestone names in workflow order. The crash recovery logic uses this to determine which step the session was on when it crashed, and generates a resume-plan.md with instructions for resuming from that step.

Changes required:
1. Add SCRIBE_COMPLETE to the step keys list, positioned after XREF_VERIFIED (Step 5) and before SESSION_COMPLETE (Step 6)
2. Add ESV_PASS to the step keys list, positioned after SCRIBE_COMPLETE
3. Ensure the resume plan correctly identifies when a session crashed between Step 5 and Step 6 (Scribe/ESV phase) and generates appropriate resume instructions

The ordering in the step keys should be:
... → REVIEW_COMPLETE → REVIEW_TRIAGED → DOCS_COMMITTED → XREF_VERIFIED → SCRIBE_COMPLETE → ESV_PASS → SESSION_COMPLETE

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] SCRIBE_COMPLETE appears in the step keys list in parse-progress-log.sh, after XREF_VERIFIED
- [ ] ESV_PASS appears in the step keys list in parse-progress-log.sh, after SCRIBE_COMPLETE
- [ ] A progress.log containing XREF_VERIFIED but missing SCRIBE_COMPLETE produces a resume plan that says to resume at Step 5b (Scribe)

- [ ] Existing tests (if any) still pass with the new step keys added — run bash scripts/parse-progress-log.sh with a test session dir to verify

title: bd list grep for Future Work epic is fragile output parsing
Big Head polling placeholder guard misses empty-string substitution
description:
## Root Cause
The Big Head polling script placeholder guard checks for angle brackets and curly braces but not for empty strings, which result from shell-expanded variables when unset. `[ -f "" ]` silently returns false, causing spurious timeout.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L531` (from Edge Cases review)

## Fix
Add `[ -z "$_path" ]` check alongside bracket checks.

## Changes Needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Placeholder guard exits with error on empty path string
- [ ] Error message identifies which path was empty

title: fix: GLOSSARY says 4 checkpoints but framework has 5 (SSV omitted)
description:
GLOSSARY.md:46 defines checkpoint as having "four checkpoints: CCO, WWD, DMVDC, and CCB." GLOSSARY.md:64 repeats "All four checkpoints are executed by Pest Control." checkpoints.md:15 lists five: "All checkpoint verifications (SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) are executed by Pest Control." The Hard Gates table in RULES.md:255-263 has 7 rows (SSV, CCO impl, CCO review, WWD, DMVDC, CCB, Reviews).

Root cause: GLOSSARY.md was written before SSV was added as a checkpoint. The GLOSSARY was not updated when SSV was introduced. The GLOSSARY also does not acknowledge the CCO impl vs CCO review distinction.

Current behavior: The GLOSSARY is the stated single source of truth for term definitions. A reader using it as authoritative will be unaware of SSV and will not understand the two CCO configurations. Someone troubleshooting a failed SSV gate using the GLOSSARY will find no definition for it.

Fix: Update GLOSSARY.md checkpoint definition and acronym table to reflect all 5 checkpoints, note the CCO dual-configuration, and match the Hard Gates table.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:46: Change "four checkpoints" to "five checkpoints" and add SSV
- orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:64: Change "All four" to "All five"
- orchestration/GLOSSARY.md Checkpoint Acronyms table: Add SSV row

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] GLOSSARY lists all 5 checkpoints: SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB
- [ ] GLOSSARY notes CCO runs in two configurations (impl and review)
- [ ] Checkpoint Acronyms table includes SSV with expansion and description
- [ ] Count references ("four" → "five") updated throughout GLOSSARY

title: Delay Big Head bead filing until after Pest Control checkpoint validation
description:
Root cause: Big Head currently files beads for review findings before CCD and DMVDC checkpoints run. If checkpoints identify invalid findings (hallucinated, misattributed, doesn't match actual diff), those beads already exist and require manual cleanup. This is wasted work and pollutes the issue tracker with unvalidated findings.

Fix: Add Pest Control to the Nitpicker team and gate bead filing on checkpoint validation. Big Head consolidates and writes the report as usual but holds off on bd create calls until Pest Control confirms the findings pass checkpoints.

Revised team composition: 4 Nitpickers + Big Head + Pest Control (6 members, still one team)

Flow:
1. 4 Nitpickers produce review reports
2. Big Head consolidates, deduplicates, writes consolidated report to {session-dir}/review-reports/review-consolidated-{timestamp}.md
3. Big Head does NOT file beads yet — messages Pest Control: 'consolidated report ready at {path}'
4. Pest Control runs CCD and DMVDC checkpoints against the consolidated report and actual code/diffs
5. Pest Control messages Big Head with verdict:
   - PASS: all findings validated
   - FAIL: list of specific findings that failed validation, with reasons
6. On PASS: Big Head files beads for all findings
7. On FAIL: Big Head flags the failures to the Queen with specifics (which findings failed, why). User decides whether to drop, adjust, or re-review. Big Head files beads only for the validated findings.

Key design decisions:
- Big Head stays alive in the team waiting for PC's verdict — no re-invocation needed
- PC communicates via team messaging (SendMessage), not file-based handoff
- Queen's context window is not impacted — no report content enters the Queen's window
- On checkpoint failure, Big Head escalates to the Queen rather than silently adjusting or dropping findings

Affected files:
- orchestration/templates/reviews.md — Update Big Head Consolidation Protocol to add checkpoint gate before bead filing
- orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md or agents/big-head.md — Add wait-for-PC step between consolidation and bead filing
- orchestration/RULES.md — Update review team composition to include Pest Control (6 members)
- orchestration/templates/pantry.md Section 2 — Big Head data file needs to reference the PC coordination step

Acceptance criteria:
1. Big Head does not file any beads until Pest Control confirms checkpoint validation
2. Pest Control is spawned as part of the Nitpicker team, not as a separate Queen-orchestrated agent
3. On checkpoint pass, all consolidated findings are filed as beads
4. On checkpoint fail, only validated findings are filed as beads; failures are escalated to Queen with specifics
5. No review report content enters the Queen's context window during this process

title: AGG-018: Align landing instructions between CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md

CLAUDE.md includes a Review-findings gate (Step 3) and specific cleanup commands. AGENTS.md omits the review gate and uses generic cleanup. An agent following AGENTS.md could skip the mandatory review gate.

Fix: Add the review gate to AGENTS.md or cross-reference CLAUDE.md as the authoritative landing procedure.

title: Verification templates missing existence guards (DMVDC summary doc, CCB provenance)
agents/big-head.md step ordering places bead filing before Pest Control checkpoint
description:
## Root Cause

When cross-session dedup and `--body-file` instructions were added to `agents/big-head.md` (ant-farm-asdl.3), two new steps were inserted and existing steps renumbered, but the step ORDER was not updated to match big-head-skeleton.md's required sequence. The agent definition says "file issues" (step 7) BEFORE "write consolidated report" (step 8), while the skeleton requires: write report first, send to Pest Control, file beads ONLY after PASS verdict.

An agent or human following `big-head.md` in isolation would file beads before the Pest Control checkpoint, bypassing the critical validation gate. The skeleton overrides at runtime, so real Big Head instances follow the correct order -- but the agent definition is used during session planning and design review.

## Affected Surfaces

- `agents/big-head.md:L22-24` -- "When consolidating" steps 6 (dedup), 7 (file issues), 8 (write report) are in wrong order relative to the skeleton's steps 7 (dedup), 8 (write summary), 9 (send to Pest Control), 10 (file beads after PASS)

## Fix

Reorder `agents/big-head.md` "When consolidating" steps to match the skeleton's validated sequence:
1. Read all reviewer reports
2. Build a findings inventory
3. Group by root cause
4. Merge into a single issue per root cause
5. Track severity conflicts
6. Deduplicate against existing beads (`bd list --status=open`)
7. Write consolidated report
8. Send to Pest Control and await verdict -- THEN file issues via `bd create --body-file` (PASS branch only)

## Changes Needed

1. `agents/big-head.md` -- Reorder steps 6-8 in the "When consolidating" list so that filing (current step 7) moves after writing the report (current step 8) and includes the Pest Control gate

## Acceptance Criteria

- `agents/big-head.md` step ordering matches `big-head-skeleton.md` sequence: dedup -> write report -> Pest Control -> file beads
- Bead filing step explicitly states it occurs only after Pest Control PASS verdict
- No step in the agent definition instructs filing before the consolidated report is written

title: reviews.md Big Head polling section placeholder convention undocumented
description:
## Root Cause
The three-tier placeholder substitution system in the reviews.md Big Head polling section (single braces for Queen, double braces for fill-review-slots.sh, intentional angle-bracket literals for guards) is a design decision with no inline documentation. The double-brace {{REVIEW_ROUND}} at L501 has no convention comment, and the angle-bracket literals in the guard at L531-558 could be mistaken for unsubstituted placeholders.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L501` -- double-brace {{REVIEW_ROUND}} (from Clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L531-L558` -- angle-bracket placeholder guard (from Clarity review)

## Fix
Add a comment block near L501 explaining the substitution tiers and another near L531 clarifying that angle-bracket literals are intentional guard targets.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Add convention comments in Big Head polling section

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Double-brace syntax has an explanatory comment
- [ ] Angle-bracket guard has a comment stating the literals are intentional

title: CONTRIBUTING.md cross-file dependency entry omitted from ant-farm-951b fix
description: The R1 consolidated report (RC-1, filed as ant-farm-951b) listed four acceptance criteria for the WAVE_WWD_PASS fix. The fix commit (3f52803) satisfies criteria 1-3 (STEP_KEYS addition, step_label case, step_resume_action case) but does NOT satisfy criterion 4: CONTRIBUTING.md dependency table includes RULES.md progress log -> parse-progress-log.sh relationship. The omission means the next developer adding a progress log milestone to RULES.md has no checklist reminder to sync parse-progress-log.sh -- the exact maintenance gap that caused ant-farm-951b in the first place. Fix: Add a parse-progress-log.sh dependencies section to CONTRIBUTING.md with a row documenting that RULES.md progress log milestone additions require updating STEP_KEYS, step_label(), and step_resume_action() in parse-progress-log.sh. Acceptance criteria: (1) CONTRIBUTING.md contains a dependency entry for RULES.md progress log -> parse-progress-log.sh. (2) Entry lists all three surfaces: STEP_KEYS, step_label(), step_resume_action().

title: Pre-commit hook silently allows PII when scrub script not executable
description: Root cause: The generated pre-commit hook in install-hooks.sh:72-75 checks if scrub-pii.sh is executable and exits 0 (allows commit) with only a WARNING if it's not. This means a missing or non-executable scrub script silently allows PII-containing issues.jsonl into git history. Additionally, install-hooks.sh does not chmod +x scrub-pii.sh after installation. Fix: Either (1) change exit 0 to exit 1 in the non-executable path to block commits when PII protection unavailable, or (2) add 'chmod +x scripts/scrub-pii.sh' to install-hooks.sh. Acceptance criteria: (1) PII cannot enter git history when scrub script is missing/non-executable. (2) install-hooks.sh ensures scrub-pii.sh is executable after installation. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2), clarity-reviewer (P3).

title: README Hard Gates table missing SSV checkpoint
description:
## Root Cause

When SSV (Scout Strategy Verification) was added as the fifth hard gate, the README Hard Gates table was not updated. It still lists only 4 gates (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) while RULES.md and GLOSSARY correctly list 5 including SSV.

Add SSV as the first row in the README Hard Gates table.

## Changes Needed

## Acceptance Criteria

title: AGG-029: Add inline acronym expansions to architecture diagram
description:
The README architecture diagram uses CCO, WWD, DMVDC, and CCB without inline expansions. Full names appear much later in the document. First-time readers encounter unexpanded acronyms with no context.

Fix: Add one-line expansions immediately under the diagram (e.g., CCO = Colony Cartography Office, WWD = Wandering Worker Detection, etc.).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] The README architecture diagram section includes inline acronym expansions
- [ ] All four acronyms (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) are expanded within 5 lines of first use
- [ ] Expansions appear before the detailed description sections later in the document

title: Failure artifact writes missing from bash script blocks on Big Head failure paths
description:
## Root Cause

Failure paths in the Big Head workflow describe failure artifacts in LLM narrative prose only -- the actual bash script blocks that execute on failure exit without writing any artifact to disk. Downstream consumers (Queen, Pest Control) find no file at the expected consolidated output path, which is a worse failure mode than finding a FAILED artifact because absence may be misinterpreted as Big Head never having run.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L586-589` -- polling timeout `exit 1` writes no artifact (the script emits a TIMEOUT message to stdout then exits; no file write)
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L91-99` -- failure artifact write instruction exists in narrative prose using `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` placeholder, but is not inside the bash script block that runs on timeout
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L765-773` -- Pest Control timeout escalation has no failure artifact write instruction at all (only an in-memory SendMessage to Queen)

## Fix

For every failure path (polling timeout, Pest Control timeout), embed the failure artifact write inside the bash script block before `exit 1`, using a shell variable for the output path set at script start:

```bash
cat > "$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH" << 'EOF'
# Big Head Consolidation -- BLOCKED
**Status**: FAILED -- [polling timeout / Pest Control timeout]
**Reports found**: [list]
**Reports missing**: [list]
EOF
exit 1
```

## Changes Needed

1. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md` -- Move the failure artifact write into the polling loop bash block (near L586) before `exit 1`
2. `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md` -- Move the failure artifact prose instruction (L91-99) into an executable code block
3. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md` -- Add a failure artifact write instruction for the Pest Control timeout path (near L773)
4. Ensure `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` is set as a shell variable early in each script block

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every bash script block that can `exit 1` on a failure path writes a FAILED artifact to `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` before exiting
- No failure artifact writes remain only in narrative prose outside of executable code blocks
- Downstream consumers can distinguish "Big Head failed" (FAILED artifact exists) from "Big Head never ran" (no file at path)

title: AGG-005: Fix setup verification test that contradicts Information Diet
description:
The Setup Test it checklist instructs the user to verify Claude runs bd show, but RULES.md prohibits the Queen from running bd show or any bd query commands. Only the Scout should run these.

Fix: Update the verification test to confirm the Scout (not the Queen) runs bd queries.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] SETUP.md test checklist no longer instructs the Queen to run bd show/ready/list
- [ ] Verification step tests that the Scout correctly executes bd queries
- [ ] No contradiction between SETUP.md test steps and RULES.md Information Diet rules

title: ant-farm-bi3 AC#4 ({REVIEW_TIMESTAMP}) introduced then removed
description: Task ant-farm-bi3 AC#4 required introducing {REVIEW_TIMESTAMP} as a canonical placeholder. It was added to pantry.md Section 2, then ant-farm-yb95 deprecated and removed Section 2 body, silently deleting the placeholder. The acceptance criterion is unmet. Fix: re-introduce {REVIEW_TIMESTAMP} in PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md and reference in RULES.md Step 3b-i, or document AC#4 supersession in bead closure. Consolidated from: Correctness F1.

title: Placeholder notation and cross-reference labeling inconsistency
description:
## Root Cause

Multiple placeholder conventions coexist across documentation without clear differentiation rules. The RULES.md Hard Gates table uses `{timestamp}` (informal prose notation) which could be confused with actual template placeholders. Cross-reference labeling (checkpoint names, file references) is also inconsistent within the same file.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:303-309` -- `{timestamp}` lowercase in table uses ambiguous brace notation (from clarity review)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:114` -- "Check 4" should be labeled "CCO Check 4" for consistency (from clarity review)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:115-116` -- mixed formatting for file refs vs checkpoint names in same sentence (from clarity review)

## Fix

Replace `{timestamp}` with `<timestamp>` in prose contexts to distinguish from actual template placeholders. Standardize checkpoint labels to include the checkpoint name prefix. Apply consistent formatting for file references vs. checkpoint names.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md:303-309`: Replace `{timestamp}` with `<timestamp>` in Hard Gates table
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:114`: Change "Check 4" to "CCO Check 4"
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:115-116`: Apply consistent formatting convention for file refs and checkpoint names

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No informal `{lowercase}` placeholder notation in prose tables (use angle brackets for prose placeholders)
- [ ] All checkpoint references in CONTRIBUTING.md include the checkpoint name prefix (e.g., "CCO Check 4")
- [ ] File references and checkpoint names use consistent formatting within each file

title: RULES.md lifecycle metadata mixed into operational step instructions
description:
## Root Cause

Steps in RULES.md embed "should you run this?" metadata (sunset clauses, conditional pre-checks) at the same indentation and formatting level as "how to run this" action instructions. This makes it hard to distinguish active steps from conditional/deprecated ones at a glance.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:L227-269` -- Step 3b-v sunset guidance mixed into operational steps (clarity review)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L64-75` -- crash recovery block reads as peer step, not conditional pre-check (clarity review)

## Fix

1. Add `**Status: ACTIVE (temporary)**` banner at the top of Step 3b-v. Move the sunset clause to directly below the banner. Keep only operational steps below.
2. Promote crash recovery block to a clearly labelled sub-step (e.g., **Step 0a: Crash Recovery Check**) with a bold `**[CONDITIONAL -- only if a prior session is indicated]**` callout.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Add status banner to Step 3b-v (~L227); restructure crash recovery block at ~L64 as Step 0a with conditional callout

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Step 3b-v has a status banner indicating it is active but temporary, with sunset clause immediately below
- [ ] Crash recovery block is visually distinct from Step 0's main sequence (either sub-step label or conditional callout)
- [ ] No operational instructions appear above the status/conditional banners in either section

title: AGG-026: Standardize agent name casing and article usage
description:
Agent names are capitalized and articled inconsistently: the Queen / Queen / The Queen, the Nitpickers / Nitpicker team, Big Head always title case. Filenames use kebab-case while prose mixes forms.

Fix: Standardize: lowercase article in prose (the Queen, the Scout), title case for role names, kebab-case in filenames. Document convention in glossary.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] All prose references use consistent article/casing pattern (e.g., the Queen not The Queen)
- [ ] All filenames use kebab-case for agent names
- [ ] The naming convention is documented in the glossary

title: Pass 1-G: Verify 10 beads against shell scripts
description:
Scope: Verify 10 open beads that reference shell scripts (build-review-prompts.sh, parse-progress-log.sh, install-hooks.sh, scrub-pii.sh) against the current file state.

Bead IDs: ant-farm-0t31, ant-farm-2585, ant-farm-456u, ant-farm-5365, ant-farm-71ye, ant-farm-9p4i, ant-farm-dsaa, ant-farm-o7ji, ant-farm-w2gj, ant-farm-w6lr

Codebase files to read: scripts/build-review-prompts.sh (primary — note: this is the merged replacement for compose-review-skeletons.sh and fill-review-slots.sh per commit 1b0037e), scripts/parse-progress-log.sh, scripts/install-hooks.sh, scripts/scrub-pii.sh

Context: These beads cover shell script issues: fill_all_slots block comment errors, undocumented assumptions (tabs in paths, printf %b), trap ordering in parse-progress-log.sh, UNREACHABLE comment reasoning, scrub-pii.sh pattern divergence, and install-hooks.sh error handling asymmetry. Commit 1b0037e merged review skeleton scripts into single-pass build-review-prompts.sh — beads referencing fill-review-slots.sh or compose-review-skeletons.sh may be ALREADY_FIXED or need re-scoping to the merged file. Commit 3f52803 fixed WAVE_WWD_PASS in parse-progress-log.sh. Known exact duplicates: ant-farm-456u/ant-farm-w6lr (trap ordering), ant-farm-dsaa/ant-farm-o7ji (UNREACHABLE comment).

Process for each bead:
1. Read the bead's full record from .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-G-input.jsonl
2. Read the bead's title and description (5 of 10 lack descriptions — infer intent from title)
3. Check the referenced location(s) in the current codebase
4. Assign verdict: STILL_VALID, ALREADY_FIXED, IRRELEVANT, NEEDS_CONTEXT, or DUPLICATE_SUSPECT
5. For DUPLICATE_SUSPECT, check against .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/all-bead-titles.txt

Output: Write JSON array to .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-G-output.json

Per-bead output schema: {"bead_id": "...", "title": "...", "verdict": "...", "confidence": "...", "evidence": "...", "priority_check": "...", "duplicate_of": null | "...", "notes": ""}

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Output file contains exactly 10 entries
- [ ] Output is valid JSON array
- [ ] Beads referencing fill-review-slots.sh or compose-review-skeletons.sh note the merger into build-review-prompts.sh
- [ ] Known duplicates (456u/w6lr, dsaa/o7ji) are marked DUPLICATE_SUSPECT
- [ ] Every ALREADY_FIXED verdict cites specific evidence

title: build-review-prompts.sh fill_slot temp file leak on awk failure
description:
## Root Cause

`build-review-prompts.sh`'s `fill_slot` function creates a temp file via `mktemp` and a `.tmp` file via awk redirection. On awk failure (disk full, permission error), neither temp file is cleaned up because `set -e` exits before the `rm -f` at the end of the function runs.

## Affected Surfaces

- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh:L141-175` -- fill_slot temp file leak on awk failure (edge-cases review)

## Fix

Use a `trap "rm -f '$tmpval' '${file}.tmp'" EXIT` inside the function or wrap in a subshell with cleanup to ensure both temp files are removed on any exit path.

## Changes Needed

- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh`: Add trap-based cleanup in `fill_slot` function (~L141)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] fill_slot cleans up both temp files ($tmpval and ${file}.tmp) on any exit path including awk failure
- [ ] No temp files accumulate when fill_slot fails

title: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE example values not clearly marked as examples
description:
## Root Cause

SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md contains example content (time estimates, pseudocode, emoji severity indicators) that is not clearly distinguished from fill-in-the-blank placeholders. Users may copy verbatim values or misunderstand the API. The pseudocode includes `background=True` which directly contradicts a CLAUDE.md prohibition.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:45-46` -- emoji severity indicators (from clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:74` -- specific time estimates filled in as examples without "example" label (from clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:153-164` -- Python pseudocode with banned `background=True` parameter (from clarity review)

## Fix

Replace time estimates with `___ min` placeholders. Add a note that pseudocode is conceptual and `background=True` must not be used. Replace emoji with plain-text labels (HIGH/MED/LOW) matching conventions used elsewhere.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:45-46`: Replace emoji with plain-text labels
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:74`: Replace specific times with `___ min` placeholders
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:153-164`: Add conceptual-only note; remove or flag `background=True`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No specific time estimates appear as if they are operational values (use placeholders)
- [ ] Pseudocode is labeled as conceptual and does not include `background=True`
- [ ] Severity indicators use plain-text labels consistent with the rest of the orchestration system

title: README.md Step 6 rm -rf contradicts session-artifact retention policy
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md has duplicate {EPIC_ID} entry in Tier 1 examples
Stale review-skeletons/ directory in RULES.md session directory listing
description:
Root cause: build-review-prompts.sh writes to prompts/ and review-reports/ directly. The intermediate review-skeletons/ directory is no longer created, but RULES.md still lists it as a session subdirectory with incorrect metadata.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/RULES.md:369 — Reversed step cross-reference '(see 3b-iii and 3b-ii respectively)' pairs review-skeletons/ with the wrong step
- orchestration/RULES.md:382 — review-skeletons/ listed as valid session subdirectory with creation attribution to deleted compose-review-skeletons.sh script
- orchestration/RULES.md:376 — Directory count says '7 subdirectories total' but actual count is 6

Fix: Remove the review-skeletons/ entry from the session directory listing. Update count from 7 to 6. Fix or remove the parenthetical step cross-reference that pairs directories with steps.

Acceptance criteria: grep -n 'review-skeletons' orchestration/RULES.md returns zero matches. Directory count says '6 subdirectories total'.

title: AGG-047: Consolidate review directory creation to single canonical source
description:
The instruction to create review-reports/ directories appears in three places: RULES.md Step 3b, reviews.md Pre-Spawn Directory Setup, and checkpoints.md references. Repetition creates drift risk if one file updates without others.

Fix: Designate RULES.md as canonical source for directory creation, replace duplicates in reviews.md and checkpoints.md with cross-references.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md is the single source for review directory creation instructions
- [ ] reviews.md references RULES.md instead of duplicating directory creation steps
- [ ] checkpoints.md references RULES.md instead of duplicating directory creation steps

title: RULES.md: mixed [[ and [ bracket styles in validation block
description: Validation code block uses both bash-specific [[ and POSIX [ brackets inconsistently.

title: big-head.md agent definition lacks cross-reference to reviews.md protocol
WAVE_WWD_PASS milestone missing from parse-progress-log.sh STEP_KEYS
description: Root cause: RULES.md:131 introduced WAVE_WWD_PASS progress log milestone but parse-progress-log.sh:62-72 STEP_KEYS array was not updated. Crash recovery cannot detect this milestone, causing incorrect resume plans (re-runs WWD unnecessarily). Unmet acceptance criterion: ant-farm-zuae criterion 4 (detectable in crash recovery). Fix: Add WAVE_WWD_PASS to STEP_KEYS between WAVE_SPAWNED and WAVE_VERIFIED, add step_label and step_resume_action cases, add cross-file dependency entry in CONTRIBUTING.md. Found by: Edge Cases (EC-01), Correctness (CO-F004).

title: AGG-041: Clarify Agent type: code-reviewer role in checkpoints.md
description:
Each checkpoint section says Agent type: code-reviewer but checkpoints are executed by Pest Control. A cold Pest Control agent cant tell whether it should BE code-reviewer or SPAWN code-reviewer subagents.

Fix: Clarify: Pest Control runs each checkpoint using code-reviewer agent type for spawned subagent. Pest Control is the orchestrator, not the executor.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md explicitly states Pest Control orchestrates and code-reviewer executes
- [ ] The Agent type field is annotated with spawned agent type to prevent misinterpretation
- [ ] The Pest Control vs code-reviewer distinction is stated once at the top and referenced throughout

title: RULES.md retry counter interaction between per-checkpoint and session-total limits is unspecified
description:
Root cause: The retry limits table in RULES.md (lines 150-151) says Checkpoint C failures allow 1 retry, and a session total of 5 retries. But it does not specify whether a Checkpoint C re-run (which may spawn a new Big Head instance) counts toward the session total. The interaction between per-checkpoint limits and the global session cap is ambiguous.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:150-151 -- Retry limits table, Checkpoint C row

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 5, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because this is an edge case in retry accounting, not a functional failure.

Fix: Clarify in RULES.md retry table: 'Each Checkpoint C re-run counts as 1 toward both the per-checkpoint limit (1) and the session total (5).'

Acceptance criteria:
- Retry table explicitly states how per-checkpoint retries interact with the session total

title: Pantry told to read implementation.md but no explanation of what to extract
description: pantry.md Step 1 says 'Read this file: implementation.md' but gives no guidance on what data to extract or how it shapes the Pantry's output. A fresh Pantry agent would read it and not know what to do with it. Clarify: is it informational (absorb the policy) or extractive (pull specific data)?

title: Prose {CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH} placeholder lacks substitution clarification comment
description:
## Root Cause

The fix for ant-farm-fz32 (commit 50844a7) moved the `SendMessage` instruction from inside bash blocks to prose outside them in both `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:127` and `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:744`. The prose now contains `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}`, but unlike the bash-block comment at `big-head-skeleton.md:95-97` which explicitly notes that `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` is a shell variable substituted at runtime by `build-review-prompts.sh` via `fill_slot`, there is no equivalent clarification for the prose uses. If `fill_slot` does not substitute placeholders in prose contexts (only inside bash blocks), Big Head would send the literal string `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` to the Queen -- confusing but non-fatal.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:127` -- prose halt instruction contains `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` with no substitution clarification comment (from edge-cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:744` -- identical prose halt instruction contains `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` with no substitution clarification comment (from edge-cases review)

## Fix

Either (a) add a brief inline comment near both prose lines confirming that `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` is substituted by `fill_slot` at build time (consistent with the bash-block comment at `big-head-skeleton.md:95-97`), or (b) verify in `build-review-prompts.sh` that prose substitution occurs and document this in `PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md`. If prose substitution does NOT occur, replace the placeholder in prose with an explicit instruction for Big Head to use the path from the bash block's output.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:127`: Add inline comment clarifying `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` substitution behavior in prose
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:744`: Add matching inline comment

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both prose instructions at `big-head-skeleton.md:127` and `reviews.md:744` have a clarifying comment (or the placeholder is confirmed substituted and documented in PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md)
- [ ] The clarification is consistent with the existing bash-block comment at `big-head-skeleton.md:95-97`
- [ ] No literal `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` string would appear in a SendMessage to the Queen at runtime

title: GLOSSARY.md anchor links use repo-root paths that fail at runtime
Fragile numeric step references in cross-file template links
description:
## Root Cause

Cross-file references between orchestration templates use numeric step numbers instead of step names, making them fragile to any renumbering. When steps are inserted or reordered (as happened during asdl.* changes), these numeric references silently point to the wrong step. There is no convention requiring name-based references for cross-file links.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L318` -- references "big-head-skeleton.md step 10" by number; if a step is inserted before step 10, this reference breaks silently
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L120` -- references "step 7" by number in PASS branch ("skip any marked as duplicates in step 7")
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L153` -- references "step 7" by number in P3 auto-filing section

Note: The reviews.md "Step 2.5" vs skeleton integer step 7 mismatch is covered by existing bead ant-farm-07ai and is not included in this filing.

## Fix

Adopt a convention of referencing steps by descriptive name rather than number in all cross-file references. For example:
- Instead of "see big-head-skeleton.md step 10", write "see big-head-skeleton.md 'Await Pest Control verdict' step (PASS branch)"
- Instead of "skip any marked as duplicates in step 7", write "skip any marked as duplicates in the cross-session dedup step"

## Changes Needed

1. `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L318` -- Replace "step 10" with step name reference
2. `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L120` -- Replace "step 7" with "the cross-session dedup step"
3. `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L153` -- Replace "step 7" with "the cross-session dedup step"
4. Consider adding a convention note to PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md or a shared style guide requiring name-based cross-file step references

## Acceptance Criteria

- No cross-file step reference uses a bare numeric step number as the sole identifier
- Each cross-file reference includes either the step name or a descriptive label that survives renumbering
- Existing internal (same-file) step references may remain numeric if they are clearly within the same document

title: fix: minor bd prohibition wording drift between CLAUDE.md and RULES.md
description:
CLAUDE.md:38 and RULES.md:16 both prohibit the same bd commands but with slightly different formatting:
- CLAUDE.md: "NEVER run `bd show`..." (plain text NEVER)
- RULES.md: "**NEVER** run `bd show`..." (bold NEVER)
- CLAUDE.md: "The Scout subagent does this."
- RULES.md: "the Scout does this"

Functionally identical scope — same 4 commands plus "any bd query command" catch-all. The drift is purely cosmetic.

Fix: Normalize wording across both files. Since sync-to-claude.sh copies project CLAUDE.md to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, only one source needs updating.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] bd prohibition text identical between CLAUDE.md and RULES.md

Audit reference: DRIFT-026 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: SETUP.md references wrong path for SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md
fix: remove 3 dead artifact entries from RULES.md Session Directory list
description:
RULES.md:345-347 lists three session artifacts that have never been created in any of the 13 observed sessions:

1. Line 345: `orchestrator-state*.md — orchestrator snapshots` — zero matches across all _session-* directories
2. Line 346: `step3b-transition-gate.md — review transition gate` — zero matches across all _session-* directories
3. Line 347: `HANDOFF-*.md — handoff documents` — zero matches. One session (_session-8ae30b) has an `EXECUTION_HANDOFF.md` at top level which does not match the documented glob pattern, suggesting an older workflow convention.

Root cause: These entries describe an earlier design that was either never implemented or was replaced by different mechanisms:
- orchestrator-state*.md was likely superseded by the queen-state.md template (orchestration/templates/queen-state.md exists)
- step3b-transition-gate.md describes a gate check that happens in RULES.md Step 3b logic but produces no artifact
- HANDOFF-*.md was likely superseded by session-summary.md and the "Hand off" step in CLAUDE.md

Additionally, two artifacts that ARE produced in every session are missing from the list:
- briefing.md — written by Scout (referenced at RULES.md:86, 99 but absent from artifact list)
- session-summary.md — written by Pantry (referenced in pantry.md:184 but absent from artifact list)

Fix: Remove the 3 dead entries and add the 2 missing ones.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md:345: Remove orchestrator-state*.md entry (or replace with queen-state.md if that is the intended artifact)
- orchestration/RULES.md:346: Remove step3b-transition-gate.md entry
- orchestration/RULES.md:347: Remove HANDOFF-*.md entry
- orchestration/RULES.md:343-349: Add briefing.md and session-summary.md entries

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] No dead artifact entries remain in RULES.md Session Directory list
- [ ] briefing.md listed with note "written by Scout (Step 1a)"
- [ ] session-summary.md listed with note "written by Pantry (optional)"
- [ ] Every artifact listed in RULES.md can be found in at least one actual session directory

Audit reference: DRIFT-012, DRIFT-013, DRIFT-014, DRIFT-024 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Filename typo: review-clarify.md should be review-clarity.md in fallback workflow
Multi-epic checkpoint artifact naming undefined
description: checkpoints.md says 'Use multi for consolidations spanning multiple epics' but RULES.md doesn't mention multi-epic scenarios. A fresh Pest Control wouldn't know when to use 'multi' vs individual epic IDs. Clarify whether multi-epic reviews are supported and document the naming convention.

title: Retry count asymmetry between RULES.md (1 retry) and reviews.md (2-attempt) undocumented
Dirt-pusher-skeleton uses {TASK_ID} placeholder but doesn't define it
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md defines {TASK_TYPE}, {AGENT_TYPE}, {DATA_FILE_PATH} in its placeholder list but uses {TASK_ID} in the commit message template without defining it. A fresh Queen filling in the skeleton would not know what value to substitute. Add {TASK_ID} to the placeholder definitions.

title: parse-progress-log.sh: dead branch comment could be more precise
description: The UNREACHABLE comment could better explain why the branch is unreachable.

title: README.md checkpoints.md file reference description missing ESV
description:
## Root Cause
README.md File Reference entry for checkpoints.md says "All checkpoint definitions (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB)" -- missing ESV (new drift) and SSV (pre-existing).

## Affected Surfaces
- `README.md:L360` (from Drift review)

## Fix
Update description to include all checkpoint acronyms.

## Changes Needed
- `README.md:L360`: Update checkpoints.md description

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] checkpoints.md file reference description lists all 6 checkpoints

title: fix: dummy reviewer prompt created but output report never materializes
description:
RULES.md:186-219 describes spawning a dummy reviewer that writes output to ${SESSION_DIR}/review-reports/dummy-review-${TIMESTAMP}.md. Both _session-068ecc83 and _session-7edaafbb contain the dummy prompt file (prompts/review-dummy.md) but no corresponding output in review-reports/.

The dummy reviewer output is documented as "discarded" and excluded from Big Head consolidation, so its absence does not affect workflow correctness. However, the context-usage instrumentation goal (measuring baseline token consumption) is silently not achieved.

Fix: Add a note to RULES.md Step 3b-v clarifying expected outcome. If consistently failing to produce output, advance the sunset timeline noted at RULES.md:186.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md documents expected dummy reviewer behavior (output may not appear)
- [ ] If dummy reviewer is sunset, remove the step entirely

Audit reference: DRIFT-030 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: scrub-pii.sh grep pattern defined as variable but duplicated inline in verification
description: Root cause: scrub-pii.sh defines PII_PATTERN at line 35 and uses it in --check mode (line 38), but the post-scrub verification (lines 52-53) re-inlines the same regex pattern. If either drifts, check and verification validate against different definitions. Fix: Use PII_PATTERN variable in all three grep calls. Acceptance criteria: Single PII pattern source used in all checks. Found by: clarity-reviewer (P3), edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: Undocumented magic value ctc in scrub-pii.sh
description: The replacement token ctc used for scrubbed PII at scrub-pii.sh:8,50 is not defined or explained anywhere. Fix: add explanatory comment or use self-documenting value like [REDACTED]. Consolidated from: Clarity F10.

title: Explore tmux-based agent spawning for real-time observability
description: Explore running orchestrated subagents in tmux windows instead of background Task agents, so you can observe agent work in real-time. Key research questions: (1) Can the boss-bot launch claude CLI sessions in tmux windows via Bash? (2) How to pipe prompts into those sessions? (3) How to collect results back? (4) Hook-based automation options? Goal: Each spawned agent gets its own tmux window you can switch to with ctrl+b n/p. May influence orchestration templates if feasible.

title: Bash scripting edge cases under set -euo pipefail
description: Multiple bash scripts use constructs that are correct but subtly platform-sensitive under strict error handling. Affected: scrub-pii.sh:52-55 (grep -c/set -e interaction), RULES.md:146-148 (tr+sed whitespace check), install-hooks.sh:27-31 (backup cp failure). Fix: add clarifying comments; simplify whitespace check; wrap backup cp gracefully. Consolidated from: Edge Cases F2, F6, F9, Excellence F4.

title: fix: CONTRIBUTING.md cross-file update checklist omits GLOSSARY.md
description:
CONTRIBUTING.md:37-41 lists files to update when adding an agent:
1. README.md "Custom agents" table
2. RULES.md "Agent Types" and "Model Assignments" tables
3. scout.md (noted as usually unnecessary)

Missing: GLOSSARY.md contains an "Ant Metaphor Roles" table (lines 77-85) listing all agents with file paths, model assignments, and role descriptions. Adding an agent without updating this table leaves the GLOSSARY inconsistent.

Fix: Add GLOSSARY.md to the cross-file update checklist.

Acceptance Criteria:

fix: CCO artifact naming uses session-wide format in practice but checkpoints.md specifies per-task
description:
checkpoints.md:179 specifies per-task CCO artifact naming: `pc-{TASK_SUFFIX}-cco-{timestamp}.md` with example at line 28: `pc-74g1-cco-20260215-001145.md`. In practice, both observed sessions use session-wide naming: `pc-session-cco-{timestamp}.md` because the Queen batches all wave prompts into one CCO run.

Root cause: The CCO specification was written assuming one CCO run per task. In practice, the Queen batches all prompts from a wave into a single CCO audit, producing one session-scoped artifact rather than per-task artifacts. The RULES.md Hard Gates table (line 258) uses a wildcard `*-cco-*.md` that accommodates both patterns — the gate works regardless, but the naming spec is misleading.

Current behavior: A future Pest Control agent following checkpoints.md literally would write per-task files. Anyone querying pc/ for `*{TASK_SUFFIX}-cco-*` will find nothing. The wildcard gate still matches, so no runtime failure, but the naming convention is documented incorrectly.

Fix: Update checkpoints.md CCO section to document that batched CCO runs use `pc-session-cco-{timestamp}.md`. Keep per-task naming as valid for single-task CCO runs. Update the example on line 28.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:179: Add batch naming variant
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:28: Update example to show both patterns

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md documents both per-task and session-wide CCO naming patterns
- [ ] Example on line 28 reflects actual practice
- [ ] Naming convention matches actual artifacts in recent sessions

Audit reference: DRIFT-009 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Incomplete Correctness Redux to Correctness rename propagation
Review timestamp ownership contradicts between pantry.md and checkpoints.md
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 2 says the Pantry generates the review timestamp. checkpoints.md says the Queen generates it. A fresh Pantry and a fresh Pest Control would disagree on who owns this value. Pick one owner, document it, and update both files.

title: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE stale spawn pseudocode and emoji style mismatch
description:
## Root Cause

`orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md` was not updated when the spawn mechanism changed from Python-style calls to the Claude Code Task tool. Two surfaces remain stale:

1. The Conflict Risk Assessment section (lines 43-47) uses colored circle emoji for risk levels, while every other file in the orchestration suite uses plain text. This is a style mismatch introduced when the template was created and never normalized.

2. The Agent Spawn Sequence section (lines 147-165) shows Python-style `spawn()` pseudocode with a `background=True` parameter. The actual spawn mechanism is the Task tool (TypeScript/JSON), and `background=True` directly contradicts the hard prohibition against `run_in_background` in RULES.md/CLAUDE.md. A new adopter could read this as executable guidance.

Both surfaces share the root cause of a missed template update pass when the orchestration mechanism was changed.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:43-47` — emoji risk indicators inconsistent with doc suite style (from Clarity C-05)
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:147-165` — Python pseudocode with `background=True` contradicts RULES.md prohibition (from Clarity C-06)

## Fix
Replace emoji risk indicators with plain text labels (`HIGH RISK`, `MEDIUM RISK`, `LOW RISK`). Replace Python spawn pseudocode with prose description or pseudo-JSON matching the actual Task tool call structure. Add a comment clarifying the spawn sequence is illustrative.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md`: Replace emoji on lines 43-47 with text labels; replace Python pseudocode on lines 147-165 with Task tool pseudo-JSON or prose

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No emoji remain in SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md risk assessment section
- [ ] Spawn sequence section uses Task tool notation (not Python) and does not reference `background=True`
- [ ] A comment or note clarifies the spawn sequence is illustrative, not executable

title: Review type names inconsistent between reviews.md and file naming
description: reviews.md section titles use 'Correctness Redux', 'Edge Cases (P2)' etc. but file names and checkpoints use 'correctness', 'edge-cases'. Drop 'Redux' and priority annotations from section titles to match the canonical short names used in file naming.

title: README Hard Gates table stale for WWD
description: Root cause: RULES.md Hard Gates table was updated with serial/batch blocking semantics for WWD but README.md:L267-273 still reads 'Next agent in wave' (pre-fix text). Fix: Update README.md Hard Gates WWD row to match RULES.md. Found by: Correctness (CO-F002).

title: compose-review-skeletons.sh sed regex converts ALL uppercase tokens without allowlist
description: Root cause: The sed substitution at scripts/compose-review-skeletons.sh:108 and :163 uses regex [A-Z][A-Z_]* to convert ALL {UPPERCASE_WORD} tokens to {{UPPERCASE_WORD}} slot markers. No allowlist limits which tokens are converted. If template prose uses {UPPERCASE_WORD} as display text (e.g. in code examples), it would be silently corrupted into a double-brace slot marker. Additionally, the inline comment claims the regex matches '2+ chars' but [A-Z][A-Z_]* matches 1+ chars (the + should be used instead of *). Fix: Define a canonical slot name list at the top of compose-review-skeletons.sh and use it for both the sed substitution and the comment header. Affected surfaces: scripts/compose-review-skeletons.sh:108, :163. Source: Excellence Review Finding 1 (P2), Clarity Review Finding 8 (P3).

title: Add cross-session dedup step and description template to big-head-skeleton.md
description:
The Big Head skeleton template (orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md) is the canonical spawn prompt for Big Head — build-review-prompts.sh extracts content below the --- separator and injects it directly. Currently, every bd create command in this file omits --description/--body-file (line 111: bd create --type=bug --priority=

--title=""), and there is no step to check existing beads before filing. Four changes required in this file: (1a) Insert a new cross-session dedup step between current step 6 (root cause grouping, ends at line 105) and current step 7 (write summary, line 106). The new step queries bd list --status=open -n 0 --short, compares each root cause against existing bead titles (exact match = skip, near-match = bd search to confirm, no match = file), and logs every dedup decision. This placement ensures dedup results appear in the consolidated summary for Pest Control validation. (1b) Replace the bare bd create command in step 9's PASS branch (line 111) with a --body-file pattern: write a temp file using heredoc with sections for Root Cause (with file:line refs — must be substantive analysis, not a title restatement), Affected Surfaces (file:line per reviewer), Fix, Changes Needed (per-file), and Acceptance Criteria (checkboxes). Then bd create ... --body-file /tmp/bead-desc.md, bd label add, rm -f. (1c) Update the P3 auto-filing command (line 116) with the same --body-file pattern (shorter template: Root Cause, Affected Surfaces, Acceptance Criteria minimum). (1d) Add 'Cross-session dedup log' to the output requirements list (lines 121-125). After all insertions, renumber steps sequentially and update any internal cross-references (e.g., 'skip any marked as duplicates in step 7' must point to the correct new step number). See plan at ~/.claude/plans/ticklish-spinning-rose.md sections 1a-1d for exact replacement text. Acceptance Criteria: - [ ] No bare bd create command remains in big-head-skeleton.md — every instance includes --body-file (verify: grep -n 'bd create' orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md shows --body-file on every match) - [ ] The description template in the PASS branch contains all 5 sections: '## Root Cause', '## Affected Surfaces', '## Fix', '## Changes Needed', '## Acceptance Criteria' - [ ] A cross-session dedup step exists before the write-summary step, containing 'bd list --status=open' and 'bd search' - [ ] The output requirements list includes 'Cross-session dedup log' - [ ] Step numbers are sequential with no gaps or duplicates, and all internal cross-references resolve correctly

title: Add cross-session dedup and description template to reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol
description:
The reviews.md template (orchestration/templates/reviews.md) contains the Big Head Consolidation Protocol with bead filing instructions. Three changes required:

(2a) Insert 'Step 2.5: Deduplicate Against Existing Beads' between 'Step 2: Merge and Deduplicate' (ends at line 672) and 'Step 3: Write Consolidated Summary' (starts at line 674) in the Big Head Consolidation Protocol section. The new step: run bd list --status=open -n 0 --short once, compare each root cause against existing titles (exact match = skip, near-match = bd search to confirm, no match = file), include a 'Cross-Session Dedup' section in the consolidated summary. When uncertain, err on filing.

(2b) Replace the bare bd create command block at lines 775-779 (currently: bd create --type=bug --priority=<combined-priority> --title="<root cause title>" followed by a comment about updating description). Replace with the --body-file pattern: heredoc temp file with Root Cause, Affected Surfaces, Fix, Changes Needed, Acceptance Criteria sections, then bd create --body-file, bd label add, rm -f. Must match the canonical pattern established in big-head-skeleton.md.

(2c) Replace the P3 auto-filing bd create command at lines 795-796 with the same --body-file pattern (shorter: Root Cause, Affected Surfaces, Acceptance Criteria minimum).

See plan at ~/.claude/plans/ticklish-spinning-rose.md sections 2a-2c for exact replacement text.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A 'Step 2.5: Deduplicate Against Existing Beads' section exists between Step 2 and Step 3 in the Big Head Consolidation Protocol, containing 'bd list --status=open' and 'bd search'
- [ ] No bare bd create command remains in the bead filing section (lines 769-800) — every instance includes --body-file
- [ ] The description template in the bead filing section contains all 5 sections: Root Cause, Affected Surfaces, Fix, Changes Needed, Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The P3 auto-filing section (lines 793-797) uses --body-file with at minimum Root Cause, Affected Surfaces, and Acceptance Criteria

title: Update agents/big-head.md with dedup instruction and --body-file reference
description:
The Big Head agent definition (agents/big-head.md) has prose at line 22 that says 'File issues via bd create with: title, description (root cause + all affected surfaces), priority, acceptance criteria' but provides no concrete command example. Agents follow concrete examples over prose, so this instruction is ignored. Two changes required:

(3a) Replace line 22 (current step 6) with two new steps:
- New step 6: 'Before filing, deduplicate against existing open beads: run bd list --status=open -n 0 --short and check for matching titles. Skip filing if a match exists; log the existing bead ID in the consolidation report.'
- New step 7: 'File issues via bd create --body-file with description containing: root cause (with file:line refs), affected surfaces, fix, changes needed, and acceptance criteria. Never use inline -d for multiline descriptions — always write to a temp file and use --body-file.'

(3b) Renumber old step 7 ('Write the consolidated report with:') to step 8. The content of that step is unchanged.

File path: agents/big-head.md. This file is 36 lines total. The change affects lines 22-23 (replacing one step with two, shifting everything after down by one line).

See plan at ~/.claude/plans/ticklish-spinning-rose.md section 3 for exact replacement text.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] agents/big-head.md contains a dedup instruction referencing 'bd list --status=open' before the filing step
- [ ] agents/big-head.md references '--body-file' (not bare 'bd create') in the filing instruction
- [ ] Steps in the 'When consolidating:' list are sequentially numbered 1-8 with no gaps
- [ ] Old step 7 ('Write the consolidated report') is renumbered to step 8 with content unchanged

title: Missing input validation guards on Queen-owned review path (REVIEW_ROUND, CHANGED_FILES, TASK_IDS)
Pantry.md step numbering namespace collision between sections
description:
## Root Cause

Section 1 and Section 2 of `orchestration/templates/pantry.md` both use "Step 1, Step 2, ..." numbering without section-scoped prefixes. If Section 2 is ever un-deprecated or a new section is added, step numbers collide.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L44-222` -- Section 1 uses Step 1-5; Section 2 (~L236+) uses Step 1-6 (clarity review)

## Fix

Prefix outer steps with section scope (e.g., "1.1 Read Templates", "1.2 Compose Task Briefs"). This becomes moot if Section 2 is fully removed per the deprecated Section 2 cleanup.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md`: Add section-scoped prefixes to step headings in Section 1 (and Section 2 if retained)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No two step headings in pantry.md share the same number unless scoped by section prefix
- [ ] Step headings are unambiguous when read out of context (e.g., "Step 1.1" not "Step 1")

title: Automated Queen crash recovery from progress log
description:
Root cause: Even with a progress log (see prerequisite issue), crash recovery is manual — the user or next Queen must read the log, interpret the state, and decide what to resume. This is feasible but adds friction and requires the user to understand the workflow steps.

Fix: Add automated recovery logic that reads the progress log on session startup and presents a structured resume plan. This is the foundation for eventual supervisor-level orchestration.

Recovery logic:
1. On session startup (via bd prime or startup hook), check for {session-dir}/progress.log files in recent session dirs
2. If an incomplete log is found (no SESSION_CLOSE_STARTED entry), parse the last completed step
3. Present a resume plan to the user:
   - What completed (with artifact paths)
   - What was in progress (may need re-run)
   - What hadn't started yet
   - Recommended resume point
4. User approves or chooses to start fresh

This is a stepping stone toward a supervisor agent that could spawn Queens and recover them automatically, but that is out of scope for this issue.

Affected files:
- orchestration/RULES.md or bd prime hook — Add progress log detection on startup
- Potentially a new script: scripts/parse-progress-log.sh

Acceptance criteria:
1. Incomplete progress logs are detected on session startup
2. A structured resume plan is presented showing completed/in-progress/pending steps
3. User can approve resume or choose fresh start
4. No action is taken automatically — user must approve the resume plan

title: AGG-020: Define TASK_SUFFIX extraction rules
description:
Multiple files reference {TASK_SUFFIX} but never define the extraction algorithm. Examples show transformations (e.g., hs_website-74g.1 -> 74g1) but the rule is unstated.

Fix: Add explicit TASK_SUFFIX extraction rules with examples covering all ID patterns in a single canonical location. Cross-reference from scout.md, pantry.md, and checkpoints.md.

Note: {EPIC_ID} was removed from all active templates in commits dabb764/f838cef (session-scoping shift). Only TASK_SUFFIX extraction needs defining.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A single canonical file defines TASK_SUFFIX extraction with regex or step-by-step rules
- [ ] Examples cover all patterns: dotted (ant-farm-74g.1 -> 74g1), undotted (ant-farm-9oa -> 9oa)
- [ ] scout.md, pantry.md, and checkpoints.md cross-reference the canonical definition instead of local definitions

title: [HALF-BAKED] Group co-located tasks into single Dirt Pusher assignments
description:
## Idea

When multiple tasks touch the same file(s), the Scout should group them and assign the group to a single Dirt Pusher instead of spawning separate agents. This avoids git rebase conflicts, gives the agent full context of related changes, and reduces cost/agent count.

The Scout already has the File Modification Matrix (Step 4) that identifies which tasks touch which files — the grouping logic itself is straightforward.

## Motivation

Currently the framework assumes 1 agent = 1 task. When two tasks both modify the same file, they're either serialized into different waves (slow) or run concurrently and risk rebase conflicts. Grouping them into one agent assignment eliminates both problems.

## Blast Radius

This is NOT a small change. The 1-agent-1-task assumption is baked into 6+ components:

| Component | Current assumption | What breaks |
|---|---|---|
| Scout (Step 5) | Wave groupings list task IDs | Needs a "task group" concept — groups of co-located tasks assigned to one agent |
| Pantry | One verdict table row per task, one task brief per agent | Needs to compose combined briefs for task groups |
| Dirt Pusher skeleton | Single {TASK_ID}, single bd close, single summary doc | Needs to iterate: claim all, implement all, close all, write one summary covering multiple tasks |
| Pest Control — DMVDC | Verifies one task's acceptance criteria per agent | Needs to verify multiple tasks' criteria against one agent's commits |
| Pest Control — WWD | Scope check against one task's file list | Scope check against union of grouped tasks' file lists |
| RULES.md | Agent count = task count per wave | Agent count < task count; needs group-aware counting |
| Coverage verification | Counts tasks assigned to waves | Counts tasks assigned to groups assigned to waves |
| Reviews / Big Head | Commit-based, task-agnostic | Minimal impact — reviews look at diffs, not task assignments |

## Key Design Decisions (unresolved)

- Should grouping be mandatory or advisory (Scout recommends, Queen decides)?
- Max tasks per group? (unbounded grouping could overload a single agent)
- How does the summary doc work — one per group or one per task?
- What if grouped tasks have different priorities? (P1 + P3 in same group)
- Does the single-task path need to remain as a separate code path, or can a group of 1 be the universal case?

title: Information Diet section header contradicts its own non-authoritative opening sentence
description:
## Root Cause
`RULES.md:494` section header is "Information Diet (The Queen's Window)" but the opening sentence says it is a non-authoritative "Quick reference" pointing to the authoritative section above. The header and opening text contradict each other about the section's authority level.

## Affected Surfaces

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The section header clearly signals its non-authoritative, quick-reference role
- [ ] No ambiguity about whether readers should rely on this section or the authoritative "Queen Read Permissions" section

title: AGG-007: Add quick-start example to README
description:
README.md explains architecture in depth but never provides a minimal do these 3 things path for new adopters. The value proposition is buried and the first actionable step is in a separate doc.

Fix: Add a Quick Start subsection showing the minimal path from clone to first orchestrated session.

Acceptance Criteria:

- [ ] The section includes numbered steps (3-5) from clone to first orchestrated run
