title: Pass 2: Consolidate batch outputs into final triage report
description:
Scope: Merge all 9 Pass 1 batch outputs plus Pass 0 duplicate data into a single triage report for user review.

Input files:
- .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-{A..I}-output.json (9 files, ~168 entries total)
- .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass0-exact-dupes.json (16 duplicate pairs)
- .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass0-epics-skip.json (8 epic IDs)

Process:
1. Load all 9 batch output files and verify each is valid JSON
2. Verify total entry count = 168 (176 beads minus 8 epics)
3. Build duplicate cluster map: start with 16 exact-dupe pairs from Pass 0, add DUPLICATE_SUSPECT findings from Pass 1, merge transitive duplicates into clusters
4. For one-sided duplicate suspects (only one agent flagged it), mark as "needs human review"
5. Aggregate verdicts into sections, organized for efficient user review

Output: Write the final report to .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/final-triage-report.md

Report sections:
1. Executive Summary — counts by verdict, net reduction from 176
2. Confirmed Duplicates — clusters with recommended keep/close, grouped by confidence (mechanical vs agent-identified)
3. Already Fixed — beads to close, with evidence citations, grouped by file area
4. Irrelevant — beads to close, with reasons
5. Still Valid — beads to keep, organized by epic parent
6. Needs Human Review — low-confidence verdicts, one-sided duplicate suspects, NEEDS_CONTEXT beads
7. Priority Re-calibration — any beads where agents suggested a different priority
8. Epic Health — per-epic summary showing children still valid vs closeable and percentage reduction

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Report accounts for all 176 beads (168 verified + 8 epics noted)
- [ ] No bead ID is missing from the report
- [ ] Duplicate clusters are fully resolved (no orphaned one-sided suspects without human review flag)
- [ ] Executive summary math is correct (sum of all sections = 176)
- [ ] Still Valid section is organized by epic for easy scanning
- [ ] Report is valid markdown with consistent table formatting

title: [Review RC-7] Pre-commit hook installs even when scrub-pii.sh missing
description: P2: install-hooks.sh:103-109 installs the pre-commit hook that depends on scrub-pii.sh even when scrub-pii.sh is missing. The hook will then block all commits. Fix: check for scrub-pii.sh existence before installing the pre-commit hook, or make the hook tolerate a missing script.

title: Pantry template lacks fail-fast for missing task-metadata dir and empty file list
description: Root cause: orchestration/templates/pantry.md has two fail-fast gaps: (1) Step 2 (implementation mode) reads task-metadata/{TASK_SUFFIX}.md but does not check if the task-metadata/ directory itself exists. If Scout crashed, Pantry gets an unhelpful file-not-found error instead of structured failure. (2) Section 2 (review mode) receives a changed-file list from the Queen but has no guard against an empty list, which would produce vacuous review briefs. Additionally, 'Read this file' at line 22 uses ambiguous pronoun reference, and the review timestamp has no canonical placeholder name. Fix: (1) Add directory existence pre-check before reading task metadata. (2) Add fail-fast for empty file list in review mode. (3) Replace 'Read this file' with explicit file name reference. (4) Introduce {REVIEW_TIMESTAMP} placeholder. Acceptance criteria: (1) Missing task-metadata/ produces actionable error. (2) Empty file list produces immediate failure. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2 x2), clarity-reviewer (P3 x2).

title: Future Work
description: Low-priority polish and improvements from review sessions

title: Installation guide verification step leaves test file in repo
description: The Verify the pre-push hook section at docs/installation-guide.md:96-108 instructs users to create docs/test.md and commit it with no cleanup step documented. Fix: add cleanup instructions after verification. Consolidated from: Excellence F11.

title: pantry.md Section 2 deprecation notice insufficiently prominent
TASK_SUFFIX placeholder dual semantics in checkpoints.md (Nitpicker vs Dirt Pusher)
description:
## Root Cause
`checkpoints.md:L489-L493` reuses the `{TASK_SUFFIX}` placeholder in the Nitpicker DMVDC section to mean "review type" (e.g., `review-correctness`), while the Dirt Pusher section uses the same placeholder to mean "bead ID suffix" (e.g., `74g1`). Same name, different semantics in the same file.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Surrounding documentation text is updated to match

title: Cross-file navigation gaps: undeclared sub-step convention, missing path refs, no inline cross-references in checklists
parse-progress-log.sh unreachable branch assigns SESSION_COMPLETE silently
description:
## Root Cause
parse-progress-log.sh has an unreachable branch at L216-218 that silently assigns RESUME_STEP="SESSION_COMPLETE" instead of failing loudly if the branch were ever reached due to a code change.

## Affected Surfaces
- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:L216-L218` (from Clarity review)

## Fix
Replace fallback assignment with an error exit, or leave as-is (comment is adequate).

## Changes Needed
- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:L216-L218`: Consider replacing with error exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unreachable branch either has a loud error exit or a clear comment explaining why the fallback is safe

title: build-review-prompts.sh does not replicate RULES.md input validation guards
description:
## Root Cause

`scripts/build-review-prompts.sh` relies on the Queen having already validated inputs per RULES.md Step 3b-i.5, but does not enforce these guards itself. If the script is called directly or the Queen's guard is bypassed: (1) REVIEW_ROUND=0 passes the `^[0-9]+$` regex and silently produces round-2+ output (2 reviewers only) for an invalid input; (2) an empty `@file` resolution produces prompts with empty file lists.

## Affected Surfaces

- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh:L95-98` -- REVIEW_ROUND validation regex `^[0-9]+$` allows 0 (edge-cases review)
- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh:L74-86` -- resolve_arg does not validate resolved file is non-empty (edge-cases review)

## Fix

After argument parsing (after resolving `@file` args), add a validation block:
1. Change REVIEW_ROUND regex from `^[0-9]+$` to `^[1-9][0-9]*$` (matches RULES.md Step 3b-i.5)
2. Check that CHANGED_FILES is non-empty after `@file` resolution; emit clear error on empty

## Changes Needed

- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh`: Update REVIEW_ROUND regex at ~L95; add non-empty check for CHANGED_FILES after @file resolution at ~L86

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `build-review-prompts.sh` rejects REVIEW_ROUND=0 with a clear error message
- [ ] `build-review-prompts.sh` rejects empty CHANGED_FILES (from empty @file) with a clear error message
- [ ] Both guards match the validation described in RULES.md Step 3b-i.5

title: Missing timeout/error-return protocol for Pest Control reply in Step 4
description: Step 0a has an explicit 30-second timeout with polling loop and error-return protocol for missing reports, but Step 4 (await Pest Control checkpoint verdict) has no equivalent. If Pest Control crashes or fails silently, Big Head waits indefinitely. Affected: reviews.md lines 525-535 and big-head-skeleton.md lines 68-70. Fix: add timeout (e.g. 60s) and error-return protocol to reviews.md Step 4 mirroring Step 0a pattern. On timeout, Big Head escalates to Queen. Acceptance: reviews.md Step 4 includes timeout value and error-return template.

title: Checkpoint A.5 relies on Queen-provided file list with no independent scope validation
description:
Root cause: Checkpoint A.5 (checkpoints.md:189) uses {list files from task description} as the expected file list, provided by the Queen. If the Queen passes an incomplete or incorrect list, A.5 will produce false positives or false negatives for scope violations. The checkpoint has no independent way to derive expected scope.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:189 -- Checkpoint A.5 expected file list placeholder

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 4, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because this is a known limitation of the input-passing design, not a bug.

Fix: Either have Pest Control cross-reference against the original data file at a known path, or document this as a known limitation: 'A.5 accuracy depends on the Queen file list -- review WARN verdicts carefully.'

Acceptance criteria:
- Either A.5 has an independent scope reference, or the limitation is explicitly documented

title: README.md deprecated agent row inconsistently formatted
compose-review-skeletons.sh no SESSION_DIR existence check before mkdir -p
RULES.md Queen Prohibitions still says 'data files' instead of 'project data files' (ant-farm-6jv gap)
description:
Root cause: Commit c3771df (ant-farm-6jv) disambiguated 'data files' in the Information Diet section (line 80) to 'project data files' with an explanatory note. However, the Queen Prohibitions section at RULES.md line 6 still reads 'data files' without the 'project' qualifier. A Queen reading the Prohibitions section first (as instructed) encounters the ambiguity that ant-farm-6jv was meant to fix, before reaching the clarification 74 lines later.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:6 -- 'NEVER read source code, tests, data files, or config files' should say 'project data files'

Contributing reviews: Correctness (Finding 3, P2), Excellence (Finding 4, P3)
Taking highest severity: P2. Correctness reviewer rated P2 because it directly violates ant-farm-6jv acceptance criteria.

Fix: Change 'data files' to 'project data files' on line 6 of RULES.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- RULES.md line 6 reads 'project data files' instead of 'data files'
- Consistent with the Information Diet section disambiguation

title: big-head-skeleton.md wiring Step 3 assumes Queen can SendMessage to team members
description:
Root cause: The wiring instructions in big-head-skeleton.md Step 3 (lines 32-43) tell the Queen to use SendMessage(to='big-head', message=...) after the 4 Nitpickers finish. However, in Claude Code's team model, the Queen (team lead/parent) may not be able to send messages to team members via SendMessage. The fallback on lines 42-43 -- 'Big Head can also discover them from the brief if SendMessage is delayed' -- partially mitigates this, but the primary mechanism documented in Step 3 may not function as described.

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:32-43 -- wiring Step 3 SendMessage instruction

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 6, P2)
Single reviewer finding but architectural in nature. P2 because if the primary communication mechanism does not work, Big Head relies entirely on the fallback path.

Fix: Clarify the actual communication mechanism. Options: (a) make the data file the primary mechanism and remove or demote the SendMessage step, (b) have a Nitpicker reviewer forward paths to Big Head after completion, (c) document that the brief file IS the primary mechanism and SendMessage is supplementary. Whichever approach is chosen, document it as canonical.

Acceptance criteria:
- The primary mechanism for Big Head to receive report paths is clearly documented
- No reliance on a communication pattern that may not be supported by Claude Code's team model
- The fallback path (brief-based discovery) is either promoted to primary or explicitly documented as the backup

title: AGG-013: Resolve user interaction contradiction between reviews.md and RULES.md
description:
reviews.md instructs asking the user about fixing review findings, re-introducing a synchronous blocking step that contradicts RULES.md design of minimizing user interaction to a single approval at Step 1.

Fix: Decide whether post-review user triage is mandatory. If yes, add it to RULES.md with skip conditions. If no, remove the interactive prompt from reviews.md.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md and RULES.md agree on whether post-review user triage is a required step
- [ ] If the step exists, RULES.md documents it with explicit skip conditions
- [ ] No undocumented synchronous blocking steps exist outside RULES.md step sequence

title: ESV Check 2 git log no guard for repo root commit
description:
## Root Cause
ESV Check 2 in `checkpoints.md:L791-L795` uses `git log --oneline {SESSION_START_COMMIT}^..{SESSION_END_COMMIT}`. The `^` suffix requires the commit's parent to exist. If `{SESSION_START_COMMIT}` is the repo's first commit (no parent), git errors.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:L791-L795` — ESV Check 2 git log range (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Add a guard: if `git rev-parse {SESSION_START_COMMIT}^ 2>/dev/null` fails, fall back to `git log {SESSION_START_COMMIT}..{SESSION_END_COMMIT}` with a note.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md`: Add root-commit guard before the git log command in ESV Check 2

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ESV Check 2 handles the case where SESSION_START_COMMIT has no parent
- [ ] The fallback command still covers the correct commit range
- [ ] The guard is documented in the check instructions

title: Scout ready mode gaps: Step 4 references bd blocked not run, assumption undocumented
Documentation polish: minor formatting, redundancy, and structural inconsistencies across orchestration templates
Session state bootstrapping gap: queen-state.md creation not specified in RULES.md Step 0
Add explicit scope fencing to Nitpicker agent definitions per review type
description:
Root cause: All four Nitpicker reviewers (clarity, edge-cases, correctness, excellence) share a single agent MD file with no review-type-specific identity. Differentiation comes entirely from the Pantry-composed data files. This means each reviewer has no baked-in understanding of what is NOT its responsibility, leading to duplicate findings across reviewers that Big Head must deduplicate after the fact.

The most impactful gap is the absence of 'not my job' boundaries — each reviewer can stray into another's territory because nothing in its agent definition fences its scope.

Fix: Add per-review-type specialization blocks to the Nitpicker agent definition(s). For each review type, define:
- Explicit 'NOT your responsibility' list referencing the other three review types
- Type-specific heuristics and patterns to look for (what good findings look like for this type)
- Type-specific severity calibration (e.g., missing null check is P1 for edge-cases, not reportable for clarity)

This can be implemented as either:
(a) Four separate agent MD files sharing a common structure, each with a specialization section, OR
(b) A single agent MD file with conditional sections keyed to a review type variable injected by the data file

Option (b) is preferred for maintainability — keeps report format, output structure, and messaging guidelines in one place while still providing scope fencing. The data file would include a REVIEW_TYPE marker that activates the relevant specialization block.

Affected files:
- agents/nitpicker.md (or ~/.claude/agents/nitpicker.md) — Add per-type specialization blocks with scope fences, heuristics, and severity calibration
- orchestration/templates/reviews.md — May need to reference the new scope boundaries for consistency
- orchestration/templates/pantry.md — Review data files may need a REVIEW_TYPE marker if using conditional blocks

Acceptance criteria:
1. Each Nitpicker reviewer has explicit 'not my job' boundaries that reference the other three review types by name
2. Type-specific severity calibration is defined (what constitutes P1/P2/P3 for each type)
3. Big Head deduplication load is reduced — fewer cross-type duplicate findings at source
4. Shared concerns (report format, output structure, messaging guidelines) remain in one place, not duplicated across 4 files

title: scrub-pii.sh only matches quoted emails, misses unquoted occurrences
description: Root cause: Both --check mode (line 38) and post-scrub verification (line 52) in scrub-pii.sh wrap the PII regex with double-quote anchors, only matching '"email@example.com"' patterns. If an email appears unquoted in a JSON value, URL, comment, or field label, the scrub misses it silently and reports 'all clean'. Fix: Either broaden patterns to match unquoted occurrences, or add a separate broad-match scan in --check mode. Acceptance criteria: (1) --check mode detects emails regardless of quoting context. (2) Scrub handles both quoted and unquoted emails. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2).

title: Timestamp format YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS repeated 5+ times across files
description: The timestamp format is redefined in checkpoints.md at lines 31, 97, 152, 277, 443 and in pantry.md line 101. If the format ever changes, all 5+ locations must be updated. Define once at the top of checkpoints.md and reference it elsewhere.

title: CCB reconciliation formula does not detect orphaned merge targets in dedup log
Fallback workflow missing round-awareness for round 2+
GLOSSARY.md Ant Metaphor Roles table missing Scribe row
description:
## Root Cause
The GLOSSARY.md Ant Metaphor Roles table lists all agent roles but does not include the Scribe, which was added to RULES.md Agent Types table and Model Assignments table.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:L82-L90` (from Correctness review)

## Fix
Add Scribe row to Ant Metaphor Roles table.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md`: Add Scribe row

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ant Metaphor Roles table includes Scribe with correct description

title: Big Head skeleton and reviews.md have divergent failure handling for missing reports
description: Big Head receives contradictory instructions about handling missing Nitpicker reports from two sources: (1) big-head-skeleton.md (lines 57-66) says 'FAIL immediately if any missing' with no waiting; (2) reviews.md Step 0a (lines 354-424) specifies a 30-second polling loop with timeout before failing. Since Big Head reads both (the skeleton at spawn time, the consolidation brief from Pantry which draws on reviews.md), it must resolve the ambiguity at runtime. Additionally, the failure artifact paths differ: skeleton writes to review-consolidated-{TIMESTAMP}-FAILED.md while reviews.md returns an inline structured error. Affected surfaces: big-head-skeleton.md:57-66, reviews.md:354-424. Fix: Designate one template as authoritative. Update skeleton to reference the brief for remediation details, or add the polling protocol to the skeleton.

title: reviews.md polling loop off-by-one gives 28s instead of documented 30s
description:
## Root Cause

The Big Head polling loop in reviews.md increments `ELAPSED` after `sleep`, so the while condition `[ $ELAPSED -lt $POLL_TIMEOUT_SECS ]` exits when ELAPSED equals the timeout. With POLL_TIMEOUT_SECS=30 and POLL_INTERVAL_SECS=2, the loop makes at most 14 iterations (checks at T=0 through T=28), giving 28 seconds of effective wait instead of the documented 30.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L576-595` -- polling while loop off-by-one (edge-cases review)

## Fix

Change the while condition to `[ $ELAPSED -le $POLL_TIMEOUT_SECS ]` or restructure to check after sleeping (do-while pattern).

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Fix polling loop condition at ~L576

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Polling loop effective wait time matches the documented timeout value (30 seconds)
- [ ] Loop condition uses `-le` instead of `-lt` (or equivalent restructuring)

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md audit table line numbers will become stale without commit reference
fix: fill-review-slots.sh @file argument notation undocumented in RULES.md
description:
fill-review-slots.sh (lines 78-94) implements an @file prefix notation allowing multiline arguments to be passed via file paths instead of inline strings. This is particularly useful for <changed-files> and <task-IDs> arguments which often contain newlines.

RULES.md Step 3b-ii (lines 168-170) documents the script invocation with 6 positional arguments but does not mention the @file feature. The core argument count and order match (MATCH on spec), but the convenience feature is undiscoverable from RULES.md alone.

Fix: Optionally add a parenthetical to RULES.md Step 3b-ii noting the @file notation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md mentions @file prefix for multiline arguments (optional)

Audit reference: DRIFT-031 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Add glossary to RULES.md defining bead, epic, wave, dirt pusher
description: Terms like 'bead', 'epic', 'wave', 'dirt pusher', 'nitpicker' are used throughout orchestration files but never defined. A fresh agent must infer meaning from context. Add a Terminology section to RULES.md with concise definitions.

title: Shutdown prohibition wording does not distinguish authorization from dispatch timing
description:
## Root Cause
The fix for ant-farm-evk2 added a shutdown prohibition at RULES.md:19 stating "The only authorized shutdown trigger is the termination check in Step 3c (zero P1/P2 findings)." However, RULES.md:300 (also from the fix) says "Shutdown is authorized at this point -- but do NOT send shutdown_request yet. Proceed to Step 4 first; send shutdown_request to team members during session teardown (Step 6 cleanup)." The prohibition defines the authorization event (Step 3c), while the Step 3c text clarifies dispatch happens at Step 6. A Queen reading only L19 might send shutdown_request immediately upon termination check, contradicting L300.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:19` -- shutdown prohibition wording (from Correctness review)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:300` -- Step 3c dispatch timing instruction (from Correctness review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RULES.md:19 prohibition wording distinguishes authorization (Step 3c) from dispatch (Step 6)

title: parse-progress-log.sh UNREACHABLE comment reasoning incomplete
Pre-push hook sync failure is non-fatal with no rationale comment
description: The pre-push hook treats sync-to-claude.sh failure as a non-fatal warning (exits 0, push continues) at install-hooks.sh:44-46. This is intentional but has no inline comment explaining the design decision. Fix: add rationale comment. Consolidated from: Clarity F11, Excellence F1.

title: fix: MEMORY.md custom agent minimum file requirements TBD caveat may be stale
description:
MEMORY.md:17 states: "Minimum file requirements still TBD — short files (9 lines) failed while full-body agents (200+ lines) work. Needs more testing to find the threshold."

All current agent files exceed 200 lines. CONTRIBUTING.md:33-35 documents the restart requirement but does not mention file size constraints. If the threshold was determined and file size is no longer an issue, the MEMORY.md caveat is misleading. If still unknown, CONTRIBUTING.md is missing a useful warning.

Fix: Determine current status. If file size is no longer a constraint, remove the TBD caveat from MEMORY.md. If still relevant, add a warning to CONTRIBUTING.md.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] MEMORY.md TBD caveat resolved (removed or updated with findings)

Audit reference: DRIFT-028 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: SETUP.md self-contradictions: missing sections and contradictory path description
description:
## Root Cause

SETUP.md contains forward references to content that does not exist and contradictory descriptions of file locations. The intro promises "backup, and uninstall documentation" that is absent, and the code-reviewer path description says "original repository's `~/.claude/agents/`" which is contradictory since `~/.claude/agents/` is a per-machine directory, not a repository path.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/SETUP.md:5` -- promises backup and uninstall documentation that doesn't exist (from clarity review)
- `orchestration/SETUP.md:36` -- "original repository's `~/.claude/agents/`" contradicts filesystem reality (from clarity review)

## Fix

Remove "backup, and uninstall" from the intro sentence. Rewrite the code-reviewer path description to say "on the machine where the system was first configured, at `~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md`."

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/SETUP.md:5`: Remove "backup, and uninstall" from the intro sentence
- `orchestration/SETUP.md:36`: Rewrite to "You can find the source file on the machine where the system was first configured, at `~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md`. Copy it to the same path on your new machine."

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SETUP.md intro sentence only references sections that actually exist in the document
- [ ] Code-reviewer path description does not use "repository" to describe a per-machine filesystem path

title: SSV PASS retains human approval gate vs ant-farm-s0ak criterion 3
description: Root cause: ant-farm-s0ak acceptance criterion 3 states 'PASS allows workflow to continue without human approval.' The implementation in RULES.md:94 and checkpoints.md:698 explicitly preserves user approval after SSV PASS. A deliberate fix commit (3510d66) re-introduced user approval to align with RULES.md design, leaving the bead criterion unmet. Affected files: orchestration/RULES.md:94, orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:698. Fix: Either (a) update ant-farm-s0ak criterion 3 to reflect retained approval gate, or (b) remove the user approval gate. Acceptance criteria: (1) Bead spec and implementation agree on whether SSV PASS requires human approval. (2) If approval retained, bead documents rationale. Found by: Correctness (CR-1 P2).

title: dependency-analysis.md says parallel bd show but scout.md uses sequential write-each-immediately
description: Root cause: orchestration/reference/dependency-analysis.md:58 says '(parallel bd show calls)' but scout.md Step 3 uses sequential write-each-immediately pattern for partial progress safety. Inconsistent guidance. Fix: Qualify or remove 'parallel' mention in dependency-analysis.md to match scout.md's sequential approach. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: No tmux guard for dummy reviewer spawn
description: Root cause: RULES.md Step 3b-v runs tmux display-message without checking whether the Queen is inside a tmux session. Current single-Queen workflow does not require tmux. Affected files: orchestration/RULES.md:168-177. Fix: Add if [ -z "$TMUX" ] guard. Acceptance criteria: (1) Dummy reviewer section does not error outside tmux. (2) Inside tmux, behavior unchanged. Found by: Edge Cases (EC-4 P3).

title: RULES.md post-push sync check relationship to pre-push hook undocumented
description:
## Root Cause

The new post-push `diff -rq` sync check in RULES.md Step 6 was added without documenting its relationship to the existing pre-push hook (which also runs `sync-to-claude.sh`), and its exclusion list does not include `reference/` which could cause spurious warnings if that directory gains session-local files.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:L365-371` -- sync check overlaps pre-push hook without noting the relationship (drift review); exclusion list potentially incomplete (correctness review)

## Fix

Add a one-line note in the RULES.md Step 6 post-push sync block: "Note: the pre-push hook also runs `sync-to-claude.sh` automatically, but it is non-fatal. This post-push check detects cases where the hook ran but sync failed silently."

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Add hook-relationship note to Step 6 post-push sync block (~L365-371)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RULES.md Step 6 sync block mentions the pre-push hook and explains why both mechanisms exist
- [ ] A reader of RULES.md can understand the post-push check is complementary to the hook without reading install-hooks.sh

title: ESV Check 2 root-commit exclusion missing reviewer guidance
description:
## Root Cause
The fix for ant-farm-ccg8 (ESV Check 2 git log no guard for repo root commit) added a fallback using `git log {SESSION_START_COMMIT}..{SESSION_END_COMMIT}` (without `^`). The note correctly warns that SESSION_START_COMMIT is excluded from the output, but there is no guidance on how Pest Control should handle the scenario where the session-start commit is also the root commit AND it is unaccounted for in the exec summary. A reviewer encountering this may incorrectly FAIL Check 2 or silently accept a missing commit.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:795` -- root-commit fallback missing reviewer guidance (from Edge Cases review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clarification added stating that in the root-commit case, omission of SESSION_START_COMMIT from the exec summary is an expected gap, not a Check 2 FAIL

title: Installation guide verification test leaves docs/test.md committed permanently
description: Root cause: docs/installation-guide.md:65-70 instructs users to create docs/test.md, commit, and push to verify hooks. This leaves a test file permanently in the repository. Fix: Use a non-committed change or add a cleanup step (git revert). Found by: excellence-reviewer (P3).

title: pantry.md fail-fast conditions use inconsistent signal words
AGG-035: Add remediation path for missing Nitpicker reports
description:
reviews.md instructs Big Head not to proceed until all 4 reports are present but never says what to DO about a missing report. No messaging, no error return, no timeout specified.

Fix: Add remediation: Return an error to the Queen listing missing reports and requesting re-spawn. Do NOT wait indefinitely.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md Big Head section includes a remediation step for missing reports
- [ ] The step specifies: return error to Queen, list missing reports, request re-spawn
- [ ] A timeout or maximum wait is specified before triggering the remediation path

title: Epic artifact directory creation not enforced in RULES.md
description: RULES.md says the Queen creates epic directories at Step 2 but provides no explicit timing relative to Pantry spawn. reviews.md separately says to create review-reports/ before composing review prompts. A fresh Queen must manually create directories at different times with no consolidated checklist. Add a pre-spawn directory setup block to RULES.md Steps 2 and 3b.

title: Stale line reference in RULES.md Step 3c points to wrong reviews.md section
description: RULES.md line 113 references 'reviews.md L485-514 (test-writing + fix workflow)' but after commit 46a776a inserted ~32-38 new lines (Step 4 checkpoint gate), lines 485-514 now contain the consolidated summary template. The actual fix workflow is at approximately L609-629. Fix: replace hardcoded line reference with section-name anchor: 'See reviews.md section Queen Step 3c: User Triage on P1/P2 Issues'. Acceptance: RULES.md Step 3c reference points to correct section.

title: compose-review-skeletons.sh exit 1 in || block redundant with set -euo pipefail
reviews.md section ordering -- Round-Aware Protocol placed after sections that reference it
shasum macOS-only tool in RULES.md session ID generation
description:
## Root Cause

`orchestration/RULES.md:378-380` uses `shasum` in the session ID generation command. `shasum` is available on macOS/BSD by default but may not be installed on Linux systems, where the equivalent is `sha1sum`. The README and SETUP.md do not document a macOS-only constraint. The primary target platform appears to be macOS (darwin), but adopters on Linux would encounter a "command not found" error.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:378-380` — `shasum` command in session ID generation (from Edge Cases EC-006)

## Fix
Either use a portable command with fallback (e.g., `shasum -a 1 2>/dev/null || sha1sum`), or document the macOS-only constraint explicitly in README.md/SETUP.md.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Replace `shasum` with a cross-platform alternative or add a macOS-only comment on line 378-380

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session ID generation command either works on both macOS and Linux, or the macOS-only constraint is documented in README.md or SETUP.md

title: AGG-032: Add fallback for missing or malformed Scout metadata in Pantry
description:
The Pantry extracts data from Scout metadata files and is forbidden from running bd show to fill gaps. If Scout wrote incomplete metadata, the Pantry has no fallback. Placeholder validation syntax also risks false positives.

Fix: Add fail-fast instructions: If metadata is incomplete or contains placeholder text, FAIL immediately with error details. Distinguish instruction syntax from surviving-placeholder syntax.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] pantry.md contains fail-fast instructions for incomplete or placeholder-contaminated metadata
- [ ] The placeholder detection syntax is distinct from template instruction text (no false positives)
- [ ] A partial verdict table is returned when metadata fails, not a silent skip

title: fix: docs don't clarify code-reviewer is a custom agent outside the repo
description:
checkpoints.md:17 references `code-reviewer` as the agent type Pest Control spawns. It is not a built-in Claude Code agent type — it exists only at ~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md (user global), not in the repo's agents/ directory. sync-to-claude.sh does not deploy it, so new adopters on fresh machines would not have it.

Neither checkpoints.md nor SETUP.md clarify this. The Agent Types table in RULES.md:278-286 does not list code-reviewer.

Note: The spawning architecture itself is contradicted by DRIFT-002 (ant-farm-h94m). If Option A is chosen there (remove spawn language), this finding becomes moot. If Option B is chosen (enable spawning), code-reviewer.md should be moved into the repo.

Fix: If the code-reviewer reference is retained, add a note clarifying it is a custom agent requiring manual setup, or move it into the repo agents/ directory.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] code-reviewer deployment path is documented OR agent moved into repo agents/
- [ ] Decision aligns with DRIFT-002 resolution

Audit reference: DRIFT-020 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Add explicit team-shutdown prohibition to RULES.md Step 3c
description: Queen prematurely shut down all Nitpicker team members after Big Head handoff, breaking the persistent-team model. Root cause: RULES.md describes the team as 'persistent' but never explicitly says WHEN shutdown is authorized. Fix: (1) Add 'DO NOT send shutdown_request' prohibition at the Step 3c decision fork, (2) Make the termination check (zero P1/P2) the ONLY place authorizing shutdown, (3) Add 'NEVER send shutdown_request before Step 4' to Queen Prohibitions section.

title: big-head-skeleton.md cross-references reviews.md line 322 but model is at line 323
description: Root cause: orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:18 says 'specified in orchestration/templates/reviews.md line 322; currently opus' but the Model declaration is at line 323 (line 322 is blank). Fix: Change to line 323 or use section reference instead of line number. Found by: correctness-reviewer (P3), excellence-reviewer (P3).

title: AGG-044: Create CONTRIBUTING guide for extending the framework
description:
Adopters who want to add agents, modify templates, or change checkpoints have no guidance on safe extension without breaking cross-file contracts. No docs for adding checkpoints, modifying templates, testing changes, or cross-file dependencies.

Fix: Create CONTRIBUTING.md covering: Adding a new checkpoint, Modifying agent templates, Testing changes, and Syncing to ~/.claude/.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md exists with sections for adding checkpoints, modifying templates, testing, and syncing
- [ ] Each section lists the cross-file dependencies that must be updated together
- [ ] A testing procedure describes how to validate changes before deploying to ~/.claude/

title: Sentinel-file completion protocol for background subagents
description:
When the queen spawns background subagents (run_in_background: true), the only way to retrieve results is TaskOutput, which returns the raw JSONL session transcript — dumping thousands of tokens of hook progress events, tool results, and assistant turns into the queen's context window. This was observed in session 795d29 where Pest Control DMVDC and a dirt pusher were spawned in parallel as background tasks, and TaskOutput injected massive JSONL into the queen's context.

Root cause: TaskOutput returns the full session transcript for background Task agents, not just the agent's final message. Foreground Task calls don't have this problem (they return only the final message), but foreground-only prevents the queen from overlapping work (e.g., preparing next-wave prompts while dirt pushers run).

Interim mitigation: CLAUDE.md currently has a blanket prohibition on run_in_background for the queen. This prevents context leakage but sacrifices the ability to overlap work (e.g., preparing next-wave prompts while dirt pushers run). This bead replaces that blanket ban with a targeted sentinel-file protocol.

Fix: Implement a sentinel-file completion protocol:

1. Session dir setup adds a signals/ directory: mkdir -p ${SESSION_DIR}/{task-metadata,previews,prompts,signals}
2. Agent skeleton template adds a final step: "As your absolute last action, write your verdict summary to {SESSION_DIR}/signals/{TASK_SUFFIX}.done"
3. The .done file contains the agent's compact verdict (PASS/FAIL, file paths, commit hash) — the queen's clean result without JSONL
4. Queen polls for completion via Bash (stat on output file for activity, ls for .done file) — no TaskOutput, no Read on output_file
5. Inactive session + no sentinel = agent crashed, respawn

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md: Add signals/ to session dir setup, add polling protocol, update anti-patterns
- orchestration/templates/dirt-pusher-skeleton.md: Add sentinel write as final step
- CLAUDE.md: Replace blanket run_in_background prohibition with "NEVER use TaskOutput or Read on background agent output files — poll for sentinel files instead"
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md: Pest Control agents also need sentinel writes

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Session dir setup includes signals/ subdirectory
- [ ] dirt-pusher-skeleton.md includes sentinel file write as final step with verdict format
- [ ] CLAUDE.md blanket run_in_background ban replaced with targeted prohibition on TaskOutput/Read of output files, sentinel polling documented
- [ ] RULES.md documents the polling protocol (stat for activity, ls for .done, crash detection)
- [ ] Pest Control and other support agent skeletons include sentinel file write
- [ ] Queen never calls TaskOutput — verified by grep across orchestration templates

title: fix: CLAUDE.md Landing the Plane annotation says Step 6 but content spans Steps 4-6 with gaps
description:
CLAUDE.md:54 annotates the entire 8-step Landing the Plane section with "(Corresponds to RULES.md Step 6.)" — but the content actually spans RULES.md Steps 4, 5, and 6, with material missing in both directions.

RULES.md has steps that CLAUDE.md omits:
- Step 4: Documentation commit (CHANGELOG, README, CLAUDE.md in single commit)
- Step 5: Cross-reference verification

CLAUDE.md has steps that RULES.md omits:
- File issues for remaining work
- Run quality gates
- Review-findings gate (present P1 findings to user)
- Update issue status
- Verify (all changes committed AND pushed)
- Hand off (provide context for next session)

Root cause: The two files evolved independently. CLAUDE.md was extended with operational steps (quality gates, issue management) that RULES.md does not cover, while RULES.md has documentation and verification steps that CLAUDE.md does not include.

Current behavior: A Queen following CLAUDE.md's landing checklist would skip the CHANGELOG documentation commit (RULES.md Step 4) and cross-reference verification (RULES.md Step 5). The false annotation creates confusion about which file is authoritative.

Fix:
1. Update CLAUDE.md:54 annotation to "(Corresponds to RULES.md Steps 4-6.)"
2. Add missing RULES.md steps to CLAUDE.md checklist: documentation commit, cross-reference verification
3. Add missing CLAUDE.md steps to RULES.md: quality gates, issue status updates, git status verification
4. Ensure both files produce the same outcome when followed independently

Changes needed:
- CLAUDE.md:54: Fix annotation
- CLAUDE.md Landing section: Add documentation commit and cross-reference verification steps
- orchestration/RULES.md Steps 4-6: Add quality gates, issue management, git status verification

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CLAUDE.md annotation correctly references Steps 4-6
- [ ] Both files cover the same complete set of landing steps
- [ ] No step present in one file is absent from the other
- [ ] git status verification appears in both files

Audit reference: DRIFT-007 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: queen-state.md fix commit range has no crash-recovery reconstruction guidance
Incomplete round-transition spec: phantom briefs/ path and missing edge-cases output path
description:
## Root Cause
The round-transition SendMessage specification in `reviews.md` and `RULES.md` Step 3c-iv was partially written from an older workflow design and not fully updated for the current session directory structure. Two problems exist in the same protocol section:

1. `reviews.md:1091` references `{session-dir}/briefs/review-brief-<timestamp>.md`, but the `briefs/` subdirectory does not exist in the canonical session layout (`RULES.md:572` defines `task-metadata/`, `previews/`, `prompts/`, `pc/`, `summaries/`, `review-reports/` — no `briefs/`).
2. `RULES.md:393` and `reviews.md:1094` say "same fields as above" for the Edge Cases reviewer without defining a distinct output path, risking both reviewers writing to the same correctness path.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:1091` — phantom `briefs/` path reference (from clarity, correctness, drift reviews)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:393` — "same fields as above" for edge-cases reviewer, no distinct output path (from correctness review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:1094` — "same fields as above" with no edge-cases output path (from correctness review)

## Fix
1. Remove or replace the `Brief path: {session-dir}/briefs/review-brief-<timestamp>.md` field at `reviews.md:1091` with a valid path or remove the field entirely if unused.
2. Add explicit output path `{SESSION_DIR}/review-reports/edge-cases-r<N+1>-<timestamp>.md` at both `RULES.md:393` and `reviews.md:1094`.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Remove or replace phantom `briefs/` path at line 1091; add explicit edge-cases output path at line 1094
- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Add explicit edge-cases output path at line 393

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No references to `{session-dir}/briefs/` exist in reviews.md round-transition section
- [ ] Edge Cases reviewer has an explicit, distinct output path in the round-transition SendMessage fields
- [ ] Both RULES.md and reviews.md round-transition fields are consistent with each other

title: Fragile grep-based Future Work epic discovery with no error handling or dedup guard
Auto-approve Scout strategy in Step 1
description:
Change RULES.md Step 1/1b so the Queen auto-approves the Scout's execution strategy instead of presenting it to the user for confirmation. Currently the Queen waits for user approval after SSV PASS before spawning any agents.

Context: The persistent review team design (ant-farm-ygmj) already auto-approves fix-cycle Scout strategies. This bead extends that pattern to the main orchestration loop (Step 1). Identified during the persistent review team brainstorming session as a separate concern with a different risk profile -- auto-approving a 3-bead fix strategy is low-risk, auto-approving a 15-task session strategy is a bigger decision that deserves its own analysis.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md Step 1b: Remove the user approval gate after SSV PASS. Queen proceeds directly to Step 2 after SSV PASS.
- Consider adding a complexity threshold: auto-approve for <=N tasks, prompt user for >N tasks
- Document the rationale for auto-approval and any safety nets (SSV is the mechanical check)

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Step 1b no longer requires user approval after SSV PASS
- [ ] Risk analysis documented: what could go wrong with auto-approval, what safety nets exist
- [ ] Decision on complexity threshold documented (always auto-approve vs threshold-based)

title: Silent failure on bd list infrastructure error in Big Head cross-session dedup
description:
## Root Cause
The cross-session dedup block in `big-head-skeleton.md:L113-L117` (and duplicate at `reviews.md:L720-L724`) calls `exit 1` when `bd list --status=open` fails. This terminates Big Head entirely without writing a failure artifact to `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` or sending a structured error to the Queen. The Queen's only signal is Big Head going idle, which triggers the stuck-agent diagnostic with a long timeout. This is a silent failure mode that stalls the entire workflow.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L113-L117` — `bd list` failure block (from edge-cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L720-L724` — duplicate `bd list` failure block (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Before calling `exit 1`, write a failure artifact to `{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}` using the standard failure artifact format, and send a structured error message to the Queen via SendMessage.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md`: Add failure artifact write and SendMessage before `exit 1` in the `bd list` failure block
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Same change in the duplicate block

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bd list` failure in the cross-session dedup block writes a failure artifact to the consolidated output path
- [ ] `bd list` failure sends a structured error to the Queen via SendMessage before exiting
- [ ] Both big-head-skeleton.md and reviews.md blocks are updated consistently

title: Session directory passing mechanism not shown in RULES.md
description: RULES.md Step 0 generates SESSION_ID and creates the session dir. Scout.md expects {SESSION_DIR} as input. But RULES.md doesn't show HOW the Queen passes the session dir path to the Scout or any other agent. Add explicit instruction: pass session dir as a parameter in the spawn prompt.

title: Incomplete milestone key rename breaks crash recovery in parse-progress-log.sh
'Fix handoff' label misleading on pre-fix summary message in big-head-skeleton.md
description:
## Root Cause
`big-head-skeleton.md:187` uses "Fix handoff" as a message header for the step 12 SendMessage to the Queen. This message fires after bead filing but before any fixes happen. "Handoff" implies transferring work to fixers, but the message is a filing summary initiating triage.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:187` — "Fix handoff" label (from clarity review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The message header accurately reflects the message's purpose (e.g., "Review complete" or "Bead filing complete")
- [ ] The header does not imply work transfer to fix agents

title: big-head-skeleton.md: CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH missing leading dot in .beads/ path
description:
Root cause: Line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md defines {CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH} as 'beads/agent-summaries/...' instead of '.beads/agent-summaries/...'. The missing leading dot means the Queen would substitute a path that writes the consolidated report to a non-existent 'beads/' directory at the repo root instead of the canonical '.beads/' hidden directory.

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- placeholder definition uses 'beads/' instead of '.beads/'

Contributing reviews: Correctness (Finding 1, P2), Excellence (Finding 1, P2)
Both reviewers independently identified this and cross-referenced each other. Confirmed as a genuine path error that would cause wrong-directory writes at fill-time.

Fix: Change 'beads/agent-summaries/' to '.beads/agent-summaries/' on line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md starts with '.beads/agent-summaries/'
- The path matches the convention used in all other orchestration files

title: bd list failure handler uses SendMessage pseudocode in bash block and lacks agent-level halt
description:
## Root Cause
The fix for ant-farm-fp74 (silent failure on `bd list` infrastructure error) added a Queen notification as `SendMessage(Queen): "Big Head FAILED: ..."` inside a bash `if ! ... ; then` block. This is Claude tool-call syntax, not valid shell -- it will be treated as an unrecognized command and silently fail or error. The Queen notification that was the entire purpose of the fix will never fire. Additionally, `exit 1` in the bash block only terminates the Bash tool call subshell, not the Big Head agent process. Without a prose-level halt instruction, Big Head may continue past the error and attempt downstream steps (consolidation, bead filing) despite the failed `bd list`.

These are two symptoms of a single root cause: the fix mixed agent-level control flow (tool calls + process halt) with shell-level error handling where they cannot execute.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:742` -- `SendMessage(Queen)` as shell line in `if !` block (from Edge Cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:125` -- same construct (from Edge Cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:742-744` -- `exit 1` terminates shell, not agent (from Edge Cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:125-127` -- same construct (from Edge Cases review)

## Fix
Remove `SendMessage(Queen)` from inside the bash block entirely. After the bash code block, add prose instructions: "If the bash block above exits with code 1, stop immediately. Do NOT proceed to consolidation. Use SendMessage to notify the Queen of the failure and end your turn." This separates shell-level error handling (write failure artifact, exit 1) from agent-level control flow (SendMessage, halt).

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Remove `SendMessage(Queen)` line from the bash `if` block (~L742). Add prose instruction after the code block directing Big Head to halt and notify Queen via SendMessage tool.
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md`: Same change (~L125). Remove pseudocode from bash block, add prose halt instruction after code block.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `SendMessage` calls appear inside any bash/shell code blocks in reviews.md or big-head-skeleton.md
- [ ] Prose instruction after the `bd list` failure bash block explicitly tells Big Head to halt and use SendMessage to notify the Queen
- [ ] Both files (reviews.md and big-head-skeleton.md) are updated consistently

title: sync-to-claude.sh silently skips missing source scripts
description:
Root cause: sync-to-claude.sh:27-33 uses '[ -f "$script" ] || continue' to silently skip missing script files. If compose-review-skeletons.sh or fill-review-slots.sh is accidentally deleted/renamed, no warning is emitted and the synced directory may contain stale versions.

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/sync-to-claude.sh:27-33

Found by: Edge Cases (P3 - F8). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Add a warning to stderr when a script file is missing instead of silent continue.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Missing scripts produce a visible warning
- [ ] Present scripts continue to sync normally

title: fix: document artifact naming convention transition point for historical sessions
description:
checkpoints.md:27-33 documents the current artifact naming convention: task-specific `pc-{TASK_SUFFIX}-{checkpoint}-{timestamp}.md` and session-wide `pc-session-{checkpoint}-{timestamp}.md` with timestamp format YYYYMMDD-HHmmss (UTC).

Older sessions use different conventions:
- _session-cd9866: Wave-based naming with no timestamps (`pc-session-cco-impl-wave1.md`, `pc-session-wwd-wave2.md`)
- _session-7edaafbb: Mixed — session-scoped DMVDC (`pc-session-dmvdc-*.md`), wave-scoped (`pc-wave2-dmvdc-*.md`), and non-standard compound names (`pc-session-dmvdc-review-fixes-*.md`)
- _session-068ecc83 (most recent): Follows the documented convention correctly

Root cause: The naming convention evolved over sessions. checkpoints.md documents the current standard but does not acknowledge that historical sessions used different formats.

Current behavior: Anyone auditing or recovering historical sessions will find artifacts that do not match the documented naming convention. This is not a bug — it is expected historical variation — but the lack of documentation causes confusion.

Fix: Add a note to checkpoints.md documenting the transition point and historical naming patterns.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md: Add note near naming convention section that sessions prior to _session-068ecc83 used wave-based naming

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md acknowledges historical naming variation
- [ ] Transition point (_session-068ecc83 as first fully-compliant session) is documented
- [ ] Note clarifies that historical artifacts are expected to diverge from current convention

Audit reference: DRIFT-019 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Pantry empty file list guard does not validate that listed files exist on disk
description: The Pantry empty file list guard (pantry.md:217-228) checks if the Queen-provided file list is empty or whitespace-only, but does not validate that the listed files actually exist on disk. If git state changes between file list generation and Pantry execution (e.g., git reset, branch switch), the Pantry would compose review briefs referencing nonexistent files. This is extremely low likelihood since the Pantry runs immediately after list generation. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:217-228. Fix: Low priority. Optionally add a note: 'The Pantry trusts the Queen file list as authoritative. File existence is verified by Nitpickers at review time.'

title: Placeholder syntax inconsistent across orchestration files
description: Orchestration files mix three placeholder syntaxes: {curly-lowercase} for substitution, {UPPERCASE} for substitution, and <angle-brackets> for substitution. A fresh agent cannot tell which are literal vs to-be-substituted. Standardize: {UPPERCASE} = substitute, document convention at top of each file that uses placeholders.

title: pantry.md Section 2 deprecation notice insufficiently prominent
Placeholder syntax inconsistency: queen-state.md uses angle-brackets while other templates use curly-braces
AGG-012: Resolve agent-type authority conflict between skeleton and Pantry
description:
dirt-pusher-skeleton.md says The Queen MAY override the agent type, while pantry.md says Do NOT re-evaluate or override the Scout recommendation. This creates an unresolved authority branch.

Fix: Decide the authority chain explicitly (Scout recommends, Pantry passes through, Queen may override with documented rationale) and update both files.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] dirt-pusher-skeleton.md and pantry.md state the same authority chain for agent-type selection
- [ ] The override policy is documented in exactly one place with cross-references from both files
- [ ] If Queen override is allowed, the conditions and documentation requirements are specified

title: Pantry skips writing preview file to previews/ directory
description: When composing implementation prompts (Section 1), the Pantry agent writes the task brief to prompts/task-{suffix}.md but returns without writing the combined preview to previews/preview-{suffix}.md. The agent's return message narrates 'Now Step 3: Write the combined prompt preview...' but exits before executing it. This caused CCO to fail on first run in session 2bb21f22, requiring a second Pantry spawn. Root cause: the preview file is not clearly listed as a mandatory output artifact in the Pantry template. Fix: make previews/ output a hard requirement in pantry.md with explicit verification before returning.

title: Round cap in RULES.md Step 3c placed after fix-now action, allowing round 5 to start before cap fires
Wording tension: regardless of task count contradicts zero-task guard
description:
## Root Cause
At `orchestration/RULES.md:99`, the phrase "No complexity threshold applies; auto-approve regardless of task count." directly contradicts the zero-task guard added immediately below at lines 100-101: "If the briefing's task count is 0, do NOT auto-proceed to Step 2." The "regardless of task count" phrasing was not updated when the zero-task guard was inserted. No runtime failure — the guard is unambiguous in its own sentence — but the literal contradiction creates unnecessary reading friction.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:99` — "regardless of task count" phrase (from Correctness F-001)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:100-101` — zero-task guard (context for the contradiction)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Line 99 no longer contains "regardless of task count" without acknowledging the zero-task exception, OR the zero-task guard is placed before the "regardless" sentence

title: SETUP.md references wrong path for SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md
fix: checkpoints.md describes PC spawning code-reviewer but pest-control agent lacks Task tool
description:
Every checkpoint section in orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md (lines 17, 113, 191, 266, 339, 413, 504, 614) describes a two-layer spawn architecture: "Pest Control is the orchestrator — the Queen spawns it to run a checkpoint. Pest Control then spawns a code-reviewer agent to execute the actual checks." Each section includes "Agent type (spawned by Pest Control): code-reviewer."

Root cause: The agents/pest-control.md:4 frontmatter declares `tools: Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep` — no Task tool. Without the Task tool, Pest Control cannot spawn any subagent. The documented two-layer architecture (PC spawns code-reviewer) is architecturally impossible given PC tool permissions. Additionally, code-reviewer.md exists only at ~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md (user global), not in the repo agents/ directory, so sync-to-claude.sh does not deploy it to new environments.

Current behavior: In practice, Pest Control executes checkpoint logic directly using its Bash/Read/Glob/Grep tools — it never spawns a code-reviewer. The documentation describes an aspirational design that was never implemented. An agent reading checkpoints.md literally would attempt a spawn and fail at the tool boundary.

Fix (choose one path):
Option A (recommended — align docs to reality): Remove all "Pest Control spawns a code-reviewer" framing from checkpoints.md. Replace with direct execution language. Update the Pest Control Overview (lines 13-24) to describe PC as the executor, not the orchestrator-of-an-executor. Remove "Agent type (spawned by Pest Control)" fields from all 7 checkpoint sections.

Option B (align reality to docs): Add `Task` to pest-control.md tools frontmatter. Move code-reviewer.md into the repo agents/ directory so sync-to-claude.sh deploys it. Verify Pest Control actually spawns code-reviewer at runtime.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md: Lines 17, 113, 191, 266, 339, 413, 504, 614 — rewrite spawn language OR keep it and fix the agent
- agents/pest-control.md: Add Task tool (Option B only)
- agents/code-reviewer.md: Move from ~/.claude/agents/ to repo agents/ (Option B only)

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md spawn architecture matches pest-control.md tool permissions
- [ ] No reference to "spawns a code-reviewer" if PC lacks Task tool
- [ ] If code-reviewer is retained, it exists in repo agents/ directory (not just ~/.claude/)
- [ ] RULES.md Agent Types table reflects any agent changes

Audit reference: DRIFT-002 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Add review round counter to queen-state template
description:
Add a `## Review Rounds` section to `orchestration/templates/queen-state.md`, inserted between the existing `## Pest Control` table and the `## Queue Position` section. This section is a state-tracking template that the Queen (the orchestrating AI agent) reads to determine whether this is review round 1 (full review) or round 2+ (fix verification). The section must contain 4 placeholder fields: (1) current round number (1, 2, 3...), (2) round 1 commit range (first-session-commit..last-impl-commit), (3) fix commit range (first-fix-commit..HEAD, set after a fix cycle), and (4) termination status (pending, or terminated with round number and reason). The exact markdown content is in `docs/plans/2026-02-19-review-loop-convergence.md` Task 1 Step 1.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] `grep "## Review Rounds" orchestration/templates/queen-state.md` returns a match
- [ ] The section appears between `## Pest Control` and `## Queue Position` — verify by reading the file and confirming section order: Pest Control → Review Rounds → Queue Position
- [ ] The template includes all 4 placeholder fields: "Current round", "Round 1 commit range", "Fix commit range", "Termination"
- [ ] Existing `## Pest Control` and `## Queue Position` sections remain intact and unmodified

title: Update CCB checkpoint for round-aware report counts
description:
Update the CCB (Colony Census Bureau) checkpoint section in `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md`. CCB is a verification checkpoint that validates Big Head's consolidation work. (1) Header — change "after all 4 review reports" to mention round-dependent count ("4 reports in round 1, 2 in round 2+"). (2) Individual reports list — replace flat 4-file list with round-dependent lists. Add note that Queen provides round number. (3) Document count — change "Read all 5 documents" to round-aware ("round 1: 5 total = 4 reports + consolidated; round 2+: 3 total"). (4) Check 0 (Report Existence Verification) — replace hardcoded 4-file list with round-dependent verification (4 files round 1, 2 files round 2+). (5) Check 1 (Finding Count Reconciliation) — update finding count text and "Report the math" format to include both round patterns. Exact content in `docs/plans/2026-02-19-review-loop-convergence.md` Task 10.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CCB header line contains "4 reports in round 1, 2 in round 2+"
- [ ] Individual reports section has "Round 1:" (4 files) and "Round 2+:" (2 files) subsections
- [ ] Document count says "round 1: 5 total = 4 reports + consolidated; round 2+: 3 total = 2 reports + consolidated"
- [ ] Check 0 has "**Round 1** — verify exactly 4 report files:" and "**Round 2+** — verify exactly 2 report files:"
- [ ] Check 1 math format includes both "Round 1:" and "Round 2+:" counting patterns in the "Report the math" line

title: Verify cross-file consistency of convergence changes
description:
After all implementation tasks are complete, read all 7 modified files end-to-end and verify cross-file consistency. This is a verification-only task — no file edits unless inconsistencies are found. Check each of the following 11 invariants by reading the specified source file and confirming the "must match" files agree:

1. Round 1 = 6 team members, Round 2+ = 4 — Source: reviews.md Team Setup. Must match: RULES.md Step 3b, big-head-skeleton.md TeamCreate examples
2. Round 1 = 4 reports, Round 2+ = 2 — Source: reviews.md Step 0. Must match: checkpoints.md Check 0, big-head-skeleton.md template, pantry.md brief composition
3. Termination = 0 P1/P2 → proceed to RULES.md Step 4 — Source: reviews.md Queen's Step 3c. Must match: RULES.md Step 3c
4. Round 1 P3s → Queen's "Handle P3 Issues" flow — Source: reviews.md. Must match: RULES.md Step 3c
5. Round 2+ P3s → Big Head auto-files to Future Work — Source: reviews.md P3 Auto-Filing. Must match: big-head-skeleton.md step 10
6. `{REVIEW_ROUND}` placeholder in skeletons — Source: nitpicker-skeleton.md, big-head-skeleton.md. Must match: pantry.md fills it during brief composition
7. Queen state file has review round counter — Source: queen-state.md. Must match: RULES.md Step 3b reads it, Step 3c updates it
8. Pantry adapts polling loop per round — Source: pantry.md Step 4 note. Must match: reviews.md Step 0a polling loop template
9. CCB expects round-dependent report count — Source: checkpoints.md Check 0, Check 1. Must match: reviews.md Step 0
10. Re-review is MANDATORY after fixes (not optional) — Source: reviews.md "fix now" path. Must match: RULES.md Step 3c
11. "Handle P3 Issues" section marked Round 1 only — Source: reviews.md. Must not conflict with P3 Auto-Filing section

Also search all files for stale line references (e.g., `L631-651`, `line 322`) and stale hardcoded counts ("all 4", "4 report", "5 member", "4 individual") that should be round-aware.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Each of the 11 invariants checked — for each one, confirm the source file content and at least one "must match" file agree (report pass/fail per invariant)
- [ ] `grep -rn "L[0-9]" orchestration/templates/ orchestration/RULES.md` shows no stale line references to sections modified by this epic
- [ ] Every instance of "all 4", "4 report", "5 member", "4 individual" in the 7 files is verified to be either (a) in a round 1 context, or (b) updated to be round-aware. List each instance and its determination.
- [ ] If any inconsistencies found, they are fixed and committed with message "fix: resolve cross-file inconsistencies in review convergence implementation"

title: Add round-aware review protocol and team setup to reviews.md
description:
Make two edits to `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`. This file defines the review pipeline protocol used by AI reviewer agents. Edit 1: Insert a new `## Round-Aware Review Protocol` section before the `## Review 1: Clarity (P3)` heading (after Messaging Guidelines). This section defines: (a) Round 1 (Full Review) — 4 reviewers, all session commits, 6 team members; (b) Round 2+ (Fix Verification) — 2 reviewers (Correctness + Edge Cases only), fix commits only, 4 team members, with an out-of-scope finding bar (only runtime failures and silently wrong results reportable outside fix scope); (c) Termination Rule — loop ends when 0 P1/P2 findings; (d) Round 2+ Reviewer Instructions — scope constraint text the Pantry agent includes in each brief. Edit 2: Update the `### Team Setup` section — replace the current single 6-member team block with round-dependent blocks (6 members for round 1, 4 members for round 2+). Preserve the `### Messaging Guidelines` section that follows Team Setup. Exact markdown for both edits is in `docs/plans/2026-02-19-review-loop-convergence.md` Task 2.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] `grep "## Round-Aware Review Protocol" orchestration/templates/reviews.md` returns a match
- [ ] The section appears before `## Review 1: Clarity (P3)` — verify by reading the file and checking heading order
- [ ] The section contains all 4 subsections: `### Round 1 (Full Review)`, `### Round 2+ (Fix Verification)`, `### Termination Rule`, `### Round 2+ Reviewer Instructions`
- [ ] Team Setup shows "**Round 1**: The Queen creates the Nitpicker team with **6 members**" and "**Round 2+**: The Queen creates the Nitpicker team with **4 members**"
- [ ] `### Messaging Guidelines` section still exists immediately after Team Setup (not accidentally deleted or displaced)

title: Update Big Head verification and summary for round-aware report counts
description:

Replace with round-aware verification: round 1 checks 4 report files (clarity, edge-cases, correctness, excellence), round 2+ checks 2 (correctness, edge-cases only), each with its own bash `ls` block. (2) `### Step 0a: Remediation Path for Missing Reports` — replace the polling loop's hardcoded 4-variable check with a round-aware version. The new loop always checks correctness and edge-cases, then conditionally checks clarity and excellence inside `<IF ROUND 1>` / `</IF ROUND 1>` comment markers. Add a `**Pantry responsibility**` note after the code block. (3) `### Step 3: Write Consolidated Summary` — replace the hardcoded "Reviews completed: Clarity, Edge Cases, Correctness, Excellence" with a round-aware format, and replace the fixed 4-row Read Confirmation table with a dynamic round-aware table. Exact replacement content in `docs/plans/2026-02-19-review-loop-convergence.md` Task 3.

title: Add P3 auto-filing, termination check, and mandatory re-review to reviews.md
description:
Make four edits to `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`. (1) Add a `### P3 Auto-Filing (Round 2+ Only)` section after the bead filing block (after the `bd label add` line). In round 2+, Big Head (the consolidation agent) auto-files P3 findings to a "Future Work" epic using `bd epic create` and `bd dep add ... --type parent-child`, without user involvement. Round 1 P3s are NOT auto-filed here. (2) Add a `### Termination Check (zero P1/P2 findings)` subsection in `## Queen's Step 3c`, inserted BEFORE `### If P1 or P2 issues found:`. When consolidated summary shows 0 P1 and 0 P2, the loop has converged — Queen updates state and proceeds to documentation. (3) In the "fix now" path, change `c. **Re-run reviews** (optional):` to `c. **Re-run reviews** (MANDATORY):` — after fixes, always re-run Step 3b with round N+1. (4) Add `> **Round 1 only.**` blockquote immediately after the `### Handle P3 Issues (Queen's Step 4)` heading. Exact content in `docs/plans/2026-02-19-review-loop-convergence.md` Task 4.

Acceptance Criteria:

Zero-task guard lacks specified task count extraction method
description:
## Root Cause
The zero-task guard at `orchestration/RULES.md:100-101` says "If the briefing's task count is 0" but does not specify how the Queen determines task count from `briefing.md`. The briefing is Scout-authored with no formally defined count field; the Queen must infer the count from the wave plan structure. If the briefing format varies across Scout runs (e.g., tasks listed under headers with no explicit count), the Queen has no defined extraction rule and could silently skip the guard.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:100-101` — zero-task guard with unspecified extraction method (from Edge Cases R2-EC-01)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The zero-task guard includes a note specifying how task count is determined (e.g., "count of tasks in the briefing's wave plan; zero tasks across all waves = task count 0")

title: Add instrumented dummy reviewer via tmux for context usage measurement
description:
Root cause: We have no empirical data on how much context window reviewers consume during a review cycle. If total changed file volume pushes a reviewer past 70-80% of the 200k context limit, conversation compaction silently degrades review quality. Without measurement data, any planning-time file budget would be a guess.

Fix: Spawn an additional dummy reviewer as a tmux window alongside the Nitpicker team for the purpose of measuring context usage. The dummy reviewer:

- Mirrors the correctness reviewer (heaviest context load — reads acceptance criteria against actual code for every task)
- Receives the same data file and reads the same changed files
- Follows the same review template and writes a report
- Runs in its own tmux pane so the user can observe context usage via the Claude Code UI
- Its report is NOT wired into Big Head's consolidation — findings are discarded

Note: This task uses tmux within iTerm2's control mode (tmux -CC). See the prerequisite research task (ant-farm-lajv) for the exact invocation commands. Standard tmux send-keys may behave differently in control mode.

The user manually records per session:
- Number of files the reviewer was asked to read
- Total line count across those files (from bead file references)
- Observed context usage percentage (from Claude Code UI)
- Whether compaction occurred (yes/no)
- At what point compaction occurred (during file reading, during report writing, or not at all)

This produces a reliable mapping from file/line counts to context usage percentage, which informs the planning-time review budget for the Scout and meta-scout.

Sunset clause: Remove after 30 sessions of data collection or when a confident file-budget threshold is established, whichever comes first.

Affected files:
- orchestration/RULES.md — Add step to spawn dummy reviewer tmux pane during review phase
- orchestration/templates/pantry.md — Compose a data file for the dummy reviewer (clone of correctness data file)

Acceptance criteria:
1. Dummy reviewer spawns as a tmux window (using iTerm2-compatible tmux control mode commands from prerequisite task) during the review phase
2. Dummy reviewer receives identical input to the correctness reviewer
3. Big Head does not read or consolidate the dummy reviewer's report
4. User can observe context usage in the dummy reviewer's tmux pane
5. After data collection period, the dummy reviewer can be removed without affecting the rest of the review workflow

title: AGG-003: Scrub personal identifiers from .beads/issues.jsonl
