title: Missing guards for zero-task briefing and unbounded SSV FAIL retry loop
description:
## Root Cause
Removing the user-approval gate at `orchestration/RULES.md:97-101` (ant-farm-fomy) eliminated an implicit human safety net without adding automated equivalents for two edge conditions:

1. **Zero-task briefing** (`RULES.md:97-99`): If Scout produces a valid briefing with zero tasks, SSV will PASS (nothing to conflict), and the Queen will auto-proceed to Pantry with an empty task list, wasting a full Pantry + CCO cycle before the empty-task condition is surfaced. Previously the user would have caught this at the approval checkpoint.

2. **Unbounded SSV FAIL retry loop** (`RULES.md:100-101`): The SSV FAIL branch re-runs Scout and re-runs SSV with no retry cap. The Retry Limits table (line 524) covers "Scout fails or returns no tasks" (1 retry) but does not clearly cover the SSV FAIL -> re-Scout -> re-SSV cycle. If Scout repeatedly produces a briefing that fails SSV, the loop has no defined termination. Previously the user could break the loop by refusing approval.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:97-99` — SSV PASS branch: no zero-task guard (from Edge Cases EC-01)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:100-101` — SSV FAIL branch: no retry cap (from Edge Cases EC-02)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:524` — Retry Limits table: missing SSV FAIL loop entry (from Edge Cases EC-02)

## Fix
1. After SSV PASS (line 99), add: "If the briefing's task count is 0, do not auto-proceed. Escalate to the user with the zero-task briefing for review."
2. Add a retry cap to the SSV FAIL -> re-Scout loop (1 retry, matching Scout retry limit). After exhaustion: "Surface the SSV violations to the user and await instruction."
3. Add or update the Retry Limits table entry at line 524 to explicitly cover "SSV FAIL -> re-Scout loop" with the cap and escalation action.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md:99`: Add zero-task guard after SSV PASS auto-proceed instruction
- `orchestration/RULES.md:100-101`: Add retry cap to SSV FAIL branch
- `orchestration/RULES.md:524`: Add/update Retry Limits table entry for SSV FAIL loop

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A zero-task briefing that passes SSV does NOT auto-proceed to Step 2; it escalates to user
- [ ] The SSV FAIL -> re-Scout loop has a defined retry cap (documented in both the Step 1b text and the Retry Limits table)
- [ ] After retry cap exhaustion, the Queen escalates to the user with SSV violations (not silently stuck)

title: Bead metadata / traceability inconsistencies (commit msg, acceptance criteria, line numbers)
compose-review-skeletons.sh: document single-delimiter assumption for future skeleton files
No concurrency safety in Big Head bead-filing workflow (temp file collision + bd list error handling)
description:
## Root Cause

External `bd` command invocations in the Big Head bead-filing workflow have no exit-code checking, no retry logic, and no abort-on-failure behavior. In the multi-Queen production environment (documented lock contention in MEMORY.md), concurrent Big Head agents write to the same hardcoded temp file `/tmp/bead-desc.md` and `bd list` failures silently produce empty output, causing duplicate bead filing.

## Affected Surfaces

- `/tmp/bead-desc.md` hardcoded path used in:
  - `agents/big-head.md:L23` (prose reference)
  - `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L794` (PASS branch bead filing)
  - `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L836` (P3 auto-filing)
  - `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L122` (PASS branch bead filing)
  Concurrent Big Head sessions write to the same file, causing silent content corruption between `write` and `bd create` calls.

- `bd list --status=open` for cross-session dedup:
  - `agents/big-head.md:L22`
  - `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L679`
  No exit-code check means a lock failure silently produces empty output, so dedup is skipped and duplicates get filed.

## Fix

1. **Temp file collision**: Use session-specific or process-specific temp file names: `/tmp/bead-desc-{SESSION_SUFFIX}-$$.md` (where `$$` is the shell PID and `{SESSION_SUFFIX}` is from the session context).

2. **bd list error handling**: Check exit code and abort filing if the dedup check fails:
   ```bash
   if ! bd list --status=open -n 0 > /tmp/open-beads.txt 2>&1; then
     echo "ERROR: bd list failed (lock contention?). Aborting bead filing to prevent duplicates."
     exit 1
   fi
   ```
   Alternatively, add a retry loop consistent with MEMORY.md guidance on lock contention.

## Changes Needed

1. `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md` -- Replace all `/tmp/bead-desc.md` references with session-specific path
2. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md` -- Replace all `/tmp/bead-desc.md` references with session-specific path (L794, L836)
3. `agents/big-head.md` -- Update prose reference to use session-specific path pattern
4. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md` -- Add exit-code check around `bd list` call at L679
5. `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md` -- Add exit-code check around `bd list` call in cross-session dedup step

## Acceptance Criteria

- No hardcoded `/tmp/bead-desc.md` path remains in any template or agent file
- All temp file paths include a session-unique or process-unique component
- `bd list` calls in the dedup step check the exit code and abort bead filing on failure
- Two concurrent Big Head sessions running simultaneously cannot corrupt each other's bead descriptions

title: Pest Control CCO review section doesn't specify where commit range comes from
description: checkpoints.md CCO section for review prompts says 'Run git diff --name-only <first-commit>..<last-commit>' but doesn't explain how Pest Control receives these values. A fresh Pest Control agent would waste time looking for the source. Add: 'The Queen provides the commit range in the spawn prompt.'

title: Task ID variable naming inconsistent across templates
description: Some files use {task-id-suffix}, some use {task-id}, some use {TASK_ID}. These may refer to the same value or different values. A fresh agent cannot tell. Standardize variable names: pick one convention and find-replace across all templates.

title: Priority calibration examples use web-UI vocabulary in orchestration context
description: RULES.md:407-413 P2 severity examples reference web-frontend concepts (visual regression, accessibility issue) that do not apply to an orchestration-only codebase. Fix: replace with orchestration-relevant examples. Consolidated from: Clarity F4.

title: parse-progress-log.sh DOCS_COMMITTED and XREF_VERIFIED stale CHANGELOG references
description:
## Root Cause
CHANGELOG ownership moved to Scribe (Step 5b) but the parse-progress-log.sh human-readable descriptions for DOCS_COMMITTED and XREF_VERIFIED still reference CHANGELOG as a Queen Step 4 artifact.

## Affected Surfaces
- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:L86` -- DOCS_COMMITTED step_label mentions CHANGELOG (from Correctness, Drift reviews)
- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:L104` -- DOCS_COMMITTED resume_action mentions CHANGELOG (from Correctness, Drift reviews)
- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:L105` -- XREF_VERIFIED resume_action mentions CHANGELOG entries (from Correctness, Drift reviews)

## Fix
(1) L86: change label to "Documentation (README/CLAUDE.md)"; (2) L104: change to "update README and CLAUDE.md in a single commit"; (3) L105: remove CHANGELOG entries reference.

## Changes Needed
- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh`: Update 3 lines to remove stale CHANGELOG references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DOCS_COMMITTED label no longer mentions CHANGELOG
- [ ] DOCS_COMMITTED resume_action no longer mentions CHANGELOG
- [ ] XREF_VERIFIED resume_action no longer mentions CHANGELOG entries

title: Shell code blocks in reviews.md lack production quality: magic numbers, inverted sentinel, buried constraints
checkpoints.md dangling cross-reference to non-existent 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' section
description:
Root cause: Three locations in checkpoints.md reference a section titled 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' that does not exist in the file. The closest match is 'Pest Control Overview' (line 9). The dangling cross-references appear at lines 67, 243, and 300.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:67 -- Dirt Pusher Checkpoint A prompt
- checkpoints.md:243 -- Dirt Pusher Checkpoint B prompt
- checkpoints.md:300 -- Nitpickers Checkpoint B prompt

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 7, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 as this is a documentation inconsistency that could confuse Pest Control but does not break functionality.

Fix: Either (a) rename 'Pest Control Overview' section to 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' to match the references, or (b) update the three references to say 'Pest Control Overview'.

Acceptance criteria:
- The section name referenced at lines 67, 243, and 300 matches an actual section heading in checkpoints.md

title: Commit range and file list validation gaps in RULES.md Step 3b and Pantry guard
shasum not cross-platform in RULES.md session ID generation
Missing guards on critical setup paths: SESSION_DIR and SSV retry
description:
## Root Cause

Failures in setup/initialization paths are either silent or discovered only after significant downstream work has been done. Two specific gaps: SESSION_DIR could be empty (causing mkdir at filesystem root), and the SSV retry loop has no count limit (could loop indefinitely).

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:383` -- no guard on empty SESSION_DIR before `mkdir -p`; if SESSION_ID generation fails, this creates directories at filesystem root (from edge-cases review)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:88-98` -- no retry limit for SSV->Scout->SSV loop; Retry Limits table covers Scout and DMVDC retries but not SSV (from edge-cases review)

## Fix

Add a SESSION_DIR emptiness guard before mkdir. Add an SSV retry limit to the Retry Limits table.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md:383`: Add guard: `[[ -z "${SESSION_DIR}" ]] && { echo "ERROR: SESSION_DIR is empty" >&2; exit 1; }`
- `orchestration/RULES.md` Retry Limits table: Add "SSV fails after Scout revision: 1" with the same escalation path as other retries

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SESSION_DIR is validated as non-empty before mkdir
- [ ] SSV retry limit is documented in the Retry Limits table
- [ ] SSV retry escalation path is specified (e.g., "present to user after 1 retry")

title: scout.md Step 3 does not explicitly say to continue iteration after bd show failure
description: Root cause: orchestration/templates/scout.md:77-80 Step 3 writes metadata per task. Error handling (lines 200-204) says to write error metadata on bd show failure, but Step 3 prose does not say to continue to the next task. A fresh Scout might stop on first failure. Fix: Add 'If bd show fails, write error metadata and continue to next task' in Step 3. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: Read Confirmation finding counts can be copied rather than independently verified
AGG-036: Define checkpoint verdict thresholds and tie-breaking rules
description:
Multiple checkpoint verdict criteria lack precise thresholds. CCO WARN uses small file without defining it. DMVDC says pick 2 most critical criteria without tie-breaking guidance. WWD WARN doesnt clarify whether it blocks the queue.

Fix: Define: small file = <100 lines, tie-breaking = first 2 listed, WARN = soft PASS (continue to DMVDC but flag for Queen review).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md defines small file with a concrete line count threshold
- [ ] DMVDC section includes tie-breaking rule for equally critical criteria
- [ ] WWD section defines WARN behavior explicitly (blocks vs continues with flag)

title: AGG-025: Create canonical glossary for key terms
description:
Key operational terms lack canonical definitions. Wave appears without defining boundaries. CCO/WWD/DMVDC/CCB are expanded differently across files. Ant metaphor names are never mapped to role descriptions in a single table.

Fix: Add a Glossary section to README.md or create orchestration/GLOSSARY.md with canonical definitions for all terms, acronyms, and metaphor mappings.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A glossary document exists with definitions for all framework terms (wave, checkpoint, etc.)
- [ ] All checkpoint acronyms (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) are expanded with one-line descriptions
- [ ] All ant metaphor names (Queen, Scout, Pantry, Dirt Pusher, etc.) map to role descriptions

title: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE protocol drift from current architecture
Dense team roster progression bullet hard to parse in RULES.md
description:
## Root Cause
`RULES.md:168` packs names, counts, and round-suffix rules into a single dense parenthetical that is hard to parse on a single read.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:168` — team roster progression bullet (from clarity review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The team roster bullet is broken into sub-bullets or cross-references the naming table in reviews.md
- [ ] Names, counts, and round-suffix conventions are each independently scannable

title: big-head-skeleton.md not updated during terminology standardization (ant-farm-ss6 gap)
description:
Root cause: Commit 03f6299 (ant-farm-ss6) standardized task ID terminology across orchestration templates, adding canonical term definitions blocks and normalizing placeholder naming. big-head-skeleton.md was missed. Three separate issues stem from this single omission:

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- uses {epic-id} (lowercase/hyphenated) instead of {EPIC_ID} (uppercase/underscored)
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- uses <timestamp> (angle brackets) instead of {timestamp} (curly braces)
- big-head-skeleton.md:1-47 -- missing the canonical term definitions block present in pantry.md:5-8, checkpoints.md:4-7, dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:8-11

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Findings 1,2,3 -- P3), Correctness (Finding 2 -- P2), Excellence (Findings 2,3 -- P3)
Correctness reviewer rated P2 because it directly violates the acceptance criteria of ant-farm-ss6. Taking the highest severity: P2.

Fix: (1) Add canonical term definitions block after line 6 of big-head-skeleton.md. (2) Change {epic-id} to {EPIC_ID} on line 14. (3) Change <timestamp> to {timestamp} on line 14.

Acceptance criteria:
- big-head-skeleton.md contains the same term definitions block as pantry.md, checkpoints.md, and dirt-pusher-skeleton.md
- All placeholders use {UPPERCASE} naming convention
- All timestamp references use {timestamp} (curly braces)

title: MANDATORY keyword formatting inconsistent across templates
description: implementation.md uses plain 'MANDATORY', dirt-pusher-skeleton uses 'MANDATORY keyword present', checkpoints uses '(MANDATORY GATE)'. No consistent formatting. Pick one style (e.g. **MANDATORY**) and standardize.

title: Failure taxonomy (INFRASTRUCTURE vs SUBSTANCE) not defined in a shared location
description: The orchestration templates use two failure labels -- INFRASTRUCTURE FAILURE and SUBSTANCE FAILURE -- to distinguish failure root causes. These labels appear across multiple files (pantry.md, big-head-skeleton.md, checkpoints.md, reviews.md) but are never formally defined in one canonical location. A reader encountering one label in isolation has no reference to understand the full taxonomy. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:33/45, big-head-skeleton.md:58, checkpoints.md:333, reviews.md. Fix: Add a brief taxonomy definition in a shared location (e.g., checkpoints.md or a new reference doc) and reference it from each template.

title: RULES.md bash blocks missing edge-case handling: tmux readiness and exit-code catch-all
description:
## Root Cause

Two bash code blocks in RULES.md have missing edge-case handling: (1) the tmux readiness check at Step 3b-v uses `grep -q '>'` which can false-positive on any output containing `>` (not just shell prompts), and (2) the crash recovery block only handles exit codes 0, 1, 2 from `parse-progress-log.sh` with no catch-all for unexpected codes (signal codes, command-not-found, etc.).

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:L253` -- tmux `grep '>'` readiness check can false-positive (edge-cases review)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L67-75` -- crash recovery exit-code handling has no catch-all for codes > 2 (edge-cases review)

## Fix

1. Use a more specific pattern for tmux readiness: `grep -qE '^[>$%#] *$'` (anchored prompt detection)
2. Add a catch-all case to crash recovery: "On any other exit code: treat as exit 1 -- surface the error to the user and await instruction."

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Update tmux readiness grep pattern at ~L253; add catch-all exit-code handler at ~L75

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] tmux readiness check uses an anchored pattern that only matches shell prompt characters at start-of-line
- [ ] Crash recovery block handles all possible exit codes, including unexpected ones, with a documented fallback

title: Pass 1-I: Verify 24 cross-file and orphan beads
description:
Scope: Verify 24 open beads that reference multiple files or have no single primary file. This is the catch-all batch for beads that did not match any specific file-scoped batch.

Bead IDs: ant-farm-1pa0, ant-farm-1r2o, ant-farm-2r4j, ant-farm-32r8, ant-farm-4hj, ant-farm-56ue, ant-farm-bkco, ant-farm-bnyn, ant-farm-bva6, ant-farm-cfp8, ant-farm-cqfv, ant-farm-e5o, ant-farm-f3t0, ant-farm-fkfw, ant-farm-i9y5, ant-farm-jss, ant-farm-nnf7, ant-farm-qzj, ant-farm-s7l8, ant-farm-s7vu, ant-farm-w1dn, ant-farm-x31, ant-farm-xvmn, ant-farm-z8lq

Codebase files to read: No fixed file list — the agent must determine which files to read based on each bead's title and description. Common files referenced include AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, queen-state.md, various templates, and README.md.

Context: These beads cover cross-cutting concerns: AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md sync mechanism, canonical term definitions copy-pasted across templates, bead metadata traceability, README fork instructions, extract_agent_section delimiter assumption, Future Work epic management, installation guide test file cleanup, fallback workflow round-awareness, queen-state.md crash recovery, grep-based epic discovery fragility, Read Confirmation verification gaps, development artifacts in production templates, and tmux-based agent spawning exploration. Known exact duplicates: ant-farm-32r8/ant-farm-i9y5 (bead metadata), ant-farm-56ue/ant-farm-nnf7 (extract_agent_section delimiter). Near-duplicates: ant-farm-bnyn/ant-farm-e5o (installation guide test file).

This batch has the most variety — beads span many files and topics. 16 of 24 lack descriptions.

Process for each bead:
1. Read the bead's full record from .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-I-input.jsonl
2. Read the bead's title and description (16 of 24 lack descriptions — infer intent from title)
3. Check the referenced location(s) in the current codebase
4. Assign verdict: STILL_VALID, ALREADY_FIXED, IRRELEVANT, NEEDS_CONTEXT, or DUPLICATE_SUSPECT
5. For DUPLICATE_SUSPECT, check against .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/all-bead-titles.txt

Output: Write JSON array to .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-I-output.json

Per-bead output schema: {"bead_id": "...", "title": "...", "verdict": "...", "confidence": "...", "evidence": "...", "priority_check": "...", "duplicate_of": null | "...", "notes": ""}

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Output file contains exactly 24 entries
- [ ] Output is valid JSON array
- [ ] Known duplicates (32r8/i9y5, 56ue/nnf7) and near-duplicates (bnyn/e5o) are marked DUPLICATE_SUSPECT
- [ ] Cross-file beads note which files were actually checked
- [ ] Title-only beads (16 of 24) have clear rationale for their verdict
- [ ] Feature requests (ant-farm-xvmn tmux exploration) are not marked ALREADY_FIXED without evidence

title: SETUP.md test checklist says Queen runs bd show, contradicts Information Diet
Documentation polish: terminology, wording, and examples across review files
description: 9 standalone P3 documentation items grouped for efficiency: (1) parse-progress-log.sh:61-62 step_key terminology inconsistency. (2) RULES.md:158-180 Step numbering collision. (3) checkpoints.md:431 floor-of-3 wording. (4) pantry.md:141-167 missing deprecation note on Step 3.5. (5) reviews.md:11 run-on sentence. (6) checkpoints.md:674-686 missing SSV PASS example. (7) reviews.md:10 timestamp ordering unspecified. (8) checkpoints.md:362-366 DMVDC fallback missing guard. (9) checkpoints.md:670-683 example uses ant-farm- prefix IDs. Fix: Address each in a documentation polish pass. Found by: Clarity (CL-3,CL-4,CL-5,CL-6,CL-7), Correctness (CR-5,CR-6), Edge Cases (EC-5), Excellence (EX-7).

title: Template placeholder substitution not validated at consumption point
description: Upstream producers (Pantry/Queen) fill template placeholders, but downstream consumers (Big Head, CCO) have no independent validation that substitution occurred. Failed substitution produces cryptic timeout/abort errors. Affected: reviews.md:519-526 (Big Head polling loop), checkpoints.md:198-199 (CCO input guard). Fix: add placeholder-presence guard at consumption point. Consolidated from: Edge Cases F5, Edge Cases F7.

title: queen-state.md Source of Truth column mixes abstraction levels
description:
## Root Cause
queen-state.md Source of Truth table "Authoritative Source" column mixes abstraction levels -- tool+command, directory pattern, file name, database name.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/queen-state.md:L67-L77` (from Clarity review)

## Fix
Standardize column to consistent pattern.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/queen-state.md:L67-L77`: Standardize column entries

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Authoritative Source column uses consistent abstraction level

title: Research tmux + iTerm2 control mode integration for spawning Claude Code sessions
description:
Root cause: The meta-orchestration design and dummy reviewer instrumentation both require spawning Claude Code sessions in tmux windows that are visible and interactive within iTerm2. iTerm2 has native tmux integration but requires control mode (tmux -CC) rather than standard tmux invocation. The exact commands for creating windows, sending keystrokes, and monitoring panes within iTerm2's control mode may differ from standard tmux usage.

Fix: Research the correct invocation patterns for:
- Starting a tmux session in iTerm2 control mode (tmux -CC)
- Creating new tmux windows that appear as native iTerm2 tabs
- Sending commands to specific windows (tmux send-keys equivalent in control mode)
- Monitoring whether a window's process is still running
- Any iTerm2-specific gotchas (startup timing, key encoding, session attachment)

This is an internet research task — search iTerm2 docs, tmux control mode docs, and community resources rather than trial-and-error.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Document the exact commands needed to: start a tmux control mode session, create a new window, send a prompt to that window, and check window status — all within iTerm2
2. Verify whether tmux send-keys works as expected in control mode or if an alternative is needed
3. Update the dummy reviewer bead's description with the correct iTerm2-compatible commands for launching the dummy reviewer
4. Update the meta-orchestration plan (docs/plans/2026-02-19-meta-orchestration-plan.md) tmux examples with correct iTerm2 control mode commands

title: Scout metadata write-each-immediately has no truncation/atomicity check downstream
fix: double-brace placeholder tier {{SLOT}} absent from PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md
description:
orchestration/templates/reviews.md:502 uses `{{REVIEW_ROUND}}` — a double-brace placeholder substituted by fill-review-slots.sh before delivery to agents. Line 592 explains: "fill-review-slots.sh substitutes {{REVIEW_ROUND}} with the actual round integer before delivering this brief to Big Head."

PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md defines three tiers:
- Tier 1: {UPPERCASE} — Queen-provided at spawn time
- Tier 2: {lowercase-kebab} — agent-derived at runtime
- Tier 3: ${SHELL_VAR} — bash interpreter

The double-brace {{SLOT}} syntax is a fourth tier substituted by shell scripts (fill-review-slots.sh, compose-review-skeletons.sh) between Queen composition and agent delivery. It is not documented in PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md. Additionally, the File-by-File Audit table in that file marks reviews.md as PASS with "None" for all tiers — factually incorrect since reviews.md uses {{REVIEW_ROUND}}.

Root cause: The double-brace convention was introduced by the review slot-filling scripts without updating the placeholder conventions document.

Fix: Add Tier 4 to PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md and correct the audit table.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md: Add Tier 4 (Script-Substituted {{DOUBLE_BRACE}}) with explanation
- orchestration/templates/PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md: Fix File-by-File Audit table row for reviews.md

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md documents the {{DOUBLE_BRACE}} tier
- [ ] Tier 4 description identifies fill-review-slots.sh as the substitution mechanism
- [ ] File-by-File Audit table for reviews.md reflects the double-brace usage
- [ ] All {{SLOT}} markers across templates are accounted for in the new tier description

Audit reference: DRIFT-018 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: README.md Hard Gates table missing ESV row
description:
## Root Cause
ESV was added to the RULES.md hard gates table but the README.md summary Hard Gates table was not updated. The README table lists only SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB.

## Affected Surfaces
- `README.md:L264-273` -- Hard Gates table missing ESV row (from Correctness review, Drift review)

## Fix
Add ESV row to README hard gates table after CCB: `| **ESV** -- exec summary verification | Git push | haiku |`

## Changes Needed
- `README.md`: Add ESV row to Hard Gates table

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README Hard Gates table includes an ESV row
- [ ] ESV row matches RULES.md definition (blocks git push, haiku model)

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md audit table lacks update guidance
description: The audit table at PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md:109-115 is marked Completed with no date, version, or instructions for updating when new templates are added. Fix: add timestamp and update instructions. Consolidated from: Clarity F3.

title: fill-review-slots.sh resolve_arg accepts empty file content without warning
description:
Root cause: fill-review-slots.sh:59-71 resolve_arg handles @file references. When file exists but is empty (0 bytes), cat returns empty string without error. The script continues with blank CHANGED_FILES or TASK_IDS, producing review prompts with empty slot fills.

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/fill-review-slots.sh:59-71

Found by: Edge Cases (P3 - F3). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: After resolving both CHANGED_FILES and TASK_IDS, validate non-empty content. Print warning to stderr or hard-error if empty.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Empty @file content produces a warning or error
- [ ] Non-empty files continue to work unchanged

title: big-head.md references nonexistent P4 severity level
description:
Root cause: agents/big-head.md:14 uses 'P2 vs P4' as an example of a 2-level severity gap. The project severity scale only defines P1, P2, P3. P4 does not exist. This is internally inconsistent and could confuse Big Head or human readers.

Affected surfaces:
- agents/big-head.md:14

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F11), Excellence (P3 - F5)
Both identified the same P4 reference on the same line.

Fix: Change the example to '(e.g., P1 vs P3)' and remove the P4 reference.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] No references to P4 in big-head.md
- [ ] Example uses only P1-P3 severity levels

title: Scout error metadata template lacks context fields (Title, Epic) present in success template
Epic ID vs epic suffix terminology mixed across files
description: Some places say 'epic ID' (e.g. RULES.md), some say 'epic suffix' (e.g. checkpoints.md line 29: 'Use 3-char epic suffix'). These refer to the same value (e.g. 74g). Pick one term and standardize.

title: Find a better name for Pantry 'data files'
description: The Pantry template references 'data files' — find a more descriptive/thematic name that fits the ant-farm metaphor.

title: Related definitions and calibration guidance split across distant file locations
description:
## Root Cause

Secondary definitions (Priority Calibration), summaries (Information Diet), and calibration targets (Review Quality Metrics) are placed far from their primary usage context. This forces readers to scan back-and-forth and risks conflicting interpretations -- most critically, the Priority Calibration section at RULES.md:572 defines P1/P2/P3 with project-level meanings (build failure, visual regression, style) that differ from the Nitpicker review severity meanings (blocks shipping, important but not blocking, polish) used 200 lines earlier in Step 3c of the same file.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:L572-579` -- Priority Calibration defines P1/P2/P3 differently from Nitpicker severity system used in Step 3c (clarity review)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L389-399` -- Information Diet partially duplicates Queen Read Permissions at L23-54 without clear rationale (clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L1049-1063` -- Review Quality Metrics at end of file, far from reviewer instructions they govern (clarity review)

## Fix

1. Rename "Priority Calibration" to "Bead Priority Calibration" and add a note: "Note: This section defines project-level issue priorities for beads. Nitpicker review severity (P1/P2/P3) is defined separately in `reviews.md`."
2. Consolidate Information Diet into Queen Read Permissions (remove the summary section) or retitle to "Quick Reference: Information Diet" with a note indicating which is authoritative.
3. Move Review Quality Metrics to immediately after the four review type sections and before the Nitpicker Report Format section in reviews.md.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md`: Rename Priority Calibration section (~L572) and add disambiguation note; consolidate or cross-reference Information Diet (~L389) with Queen Read Permissions (~L23)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Move Review Quality Metrics section (~L1049) to after review type sections

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Priority Calibration section title and/or body clearly distinguishes bead priorities from review severity
- [ ] Information Diet section either removed (consolidated into Queen Read Permissions) or explicitly labeled as non-authoritative quick reference
- [ ] Review Quality Metrics section appears before the Nitpicker Report Format section in reviews.md

title: Pantry reads dirt-pusher-skeleton.md without file existence check
description:
## Root Cause

Pantry Section 1 Step 3 instructs reading `dirt-pusher-skeleton.md` with no fallback or fail-fast if the file is missing. The Pantry has established fail-fast patterns for other preconditions (Conditions 1/2/3 in Step 2), but template file existence is not guarded. A future rename, move, or deletion of this file would cause the Pantry to fail with no diagnostic artifact written to disk.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L143` -- Step 3 reads `~/.claude/orchestration/templates/dirt-pusher-skeleton.md` with no existence check or fallback behavior
- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L414-417` -- Section 3 error handling covers per-task-brief failures but not missing template files

## Fix

Add a fail-fast check before reading the skeleton file, consistent with the existing Condition 1/2/3 pattern in Section 1 Step 2:

```
Before reading: verify dirt-pusher-skeleton.md exists at the expected path.
If missing: write a FAILED artifact to {session-dir}/prompts/task-previews-FAILED.md with:
  - Missing file path
  - Expected location
  - Instruction to verify the file exists or update the path reference
Then halt (do not proceed to Step 4).
```

## Changes Needed

1. `orchestration/templates/pantry.md` -- Add file existence check before Step 3's read instruction, following the Condition 1/2/3 fail-fast pattern from Step 2

## Acceptance Criteria

- Pantry Step 3 includes an explicit existence check for `dirt-pusher-skeleton.md` before attempting to read it
- If the file is missing, a FAILED artifact is written to the session prompts directory with a diagnostic message
- The fail-fast pattern is consistent with existing Conditions 1/2/3 in Section 1 Step 2

title: Progress log key tasks_approved misleading after auto-approve change
description:
## Root Cause
The progress log line at `orchestration/RULES.md:111` was not fully updated when the user-approval gate was removed in ant-farm-fomy. The key name `tasks_approved=<N>` implies a human approved the task list, which is no longer accurate — approval is now automatic after SSV PASS. Additionally, the derivation of `<N>` is unspecified: under the old flow, N came from the explicit approval event; under auto-approval, there is no discrete event from which to derive it.

Three reviewers independently identified this at the same file:line: Clarity (misleading key name), Correctness (label holdover), and Edge Cases (unspecified placeholder derivation).

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:111` — progress log key `tasks_approved=<N>` (from Clarity CLR-1, Correctness F-001, Edge Cases EC-03)

## Fix
Rename the progress log key from `tasks_approved` to `tasks_accepted` or `tasks_scheduled` to reflect that acceptance is now automatic. Document that `<N>` is derived from the count of tasks in the briefing's task list after SSV PASS. Specify that N=0 should not be logged (it should be caught by the zero-task guard instead).

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md:111`: Rename `tasks_approved=<N>` to `tasks_accepted=<N>` and add a comment specifying how N is derived (count of tasks in briefing task list after SSV PASS)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Progress log key at RULES.md:111 no longer uses the word "approved"
- [ ] The derivation of `<N>` (task count from briefing) is documented inline or in an adjacent comment
- [ ] `parse-progress-log.sh` (if it parses `tasks_approved`) is updated to match the new key name

title: RULES.md Step 3b is too dense for reliable agent parsing under pressure
description:
Root cause: RULES.md:97-127 Step 3b (Review) packs ~30 lines of unbroken prose with embedded code, bullet lists, and conditional logic (round 1 vs round 2+). This is the most cognitively demanding step in RULES.md and requires reading the entire block to find specific sub-steps like 'how to spawn round 2+ team'.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/RULES.md:97-127

Found by: Clarity (P2)
Single reviewer finding, but rated P2 because the Queen agent must parse this block reliably under time pressure. Structural density directly impacts agent execution reliability.

Fix: Break Step 3b into labeled sub-steps (e.g., 'Step 3b-i: Gather inputs', 'Step 3b-ii: Fill slots', 'Step 3b-iii: Spawn reviewers (round-dependent)').

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Step 3b is broken into 3+ labeled sub-steps
- [ ] Each sub-step can be found by scanning for its label without reading the full block
- [ ] No content is lost in the restructuring

title: fix: review-skeletons/ and review-reports/ missing from Step 0 session directory setup
description:
The Step 0 session directory setup in orchestration/RULES.md:336 creates 5 subdirectories:
`mkdir -p "${SESSION_DIR}"/{task-metadata,previews,prompts,pc,summaries}`

Two additional directories appear in every session that reaches the review phase but are not listed:
- review-skeletons/ — created by compose-review-skeletons.sh (called by Pantry in Step 2)
- review-reports/ — created by mkdir -p at RULES.md:175 (Step 3b-iii)

Root cause: These directories were introduced after the original Step 0 setup was written. They are created lazily by their respective workflow phases, but the Session Directory section gives no hint they will exist.

Current behavior: The Step 0 mkdir command is treated as the complete session directory setup. A reader has an incomplete mental model. Crash recovery between Step 2 and Step 3b could fail if it expects these directories to exist before the lazy-creation step runs.

Fix: Add a note after RULES.md:336 documenting the additional directories created during later phases. Do NOT add them to the mkdir command (lazy creation is intentional — sessions that never reach reviews should not have empty review dirs).

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md: Add note after line 336 listing review-skeletons/ and review-reports/ with their creation points

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Session Directory section documents all 7 subdirectories that appear in practice
- [ ] Note clarifies lazy creation (review dirs created by their respective phases, not at Step 0)
- [ ] Crash recovery documentation accounts for directories that may not yet exist

Audit reference: DRIFT-006 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: README.md Dirt Pushers mislabeled as review subagents
Scout Step 5.5 coverage verification missing wave capacity validation
Polling loop shell-state caveat and round-conditional logic ambiguity
RULES.md Model Assignments note misleading about Nitpicker model config location
CLAUDE.md and RULES.md Landing the Plane step divergence
description:
Root cause: When synchronizing CLAUDE.md and RULES.md for ant-farm-f1xn, structural differences remained. CLAUDE.md has an explicit standalone 'Verify' step (step 9: 'All changes committed AND pushed') that has no named equivalent in RULES.md Step 6. The parenthetical '(Corresponds to RULES.md Steps 4-6.)' is an imprecise mapping. The repo CLAUDE.md and synced ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md copy have also diverged (extra steps in repo copy not in synced copy).

Affected surfaces:
- CLAUDE.md:54 — Parenthetical '(Corresponds to RULES.md Steps 4-6.)' is imprecise
- CLAUDE.md:54-76 — Steps not explicitly named in RULES.md (Verify step)
- CLAUDE.md:63 — Steps 4-5 exist in repo CLAUDE.md but not in synced ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
- orchestration/RULES.md:267-280 — RULES.md covers substance inline in Step 6 but does not name 'Verify' as a discrete step

This is the one acceptance criterion (ant-farm-f1xn AC3: 'No step present in one file is absent from the other') that is technically unmet.

Fix: Add an explicit 'Verify' beat to RULES.md Step 6. Update or remove the parenthetical mapping in CLAUDE.md. Resynchronize both copies of CLAUDE.md.

Acceptance criteria: RULES.md Step 6 includes a named Verify substep. CLAUDE.md parenthetical accurately maps to RULES.md steps. diff CLAUDE.md ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md shows no substantive differences.

title: Wave failure threshold ambiguous on retry counting timing
description:
## Root Cause
`RULES.md:L674-L682` defines a wave failure threshold (>50% of agents fail) but does not specify whether an agent counts as "failed" before or after retries exhaust.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L674-L682` — wave failure threshold (from edge-cases review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The threshold definition specifies when an agent counts toward the failure percentage (after all retries exhausted)
- [ ] The counting rule distinguishes between DMVDC retries (2) and stuck agents (0 retries)

title: SendMessage pseudo-API examples use incorrect parameter names
description: reviews.md Step 4 SendMessage example (lines 529-532) uses to= and message= but actual tool uses recipient= and content=. big-head-skeleton.md line 66 uses display name 'Pest Control' instead of member name 'pest-control'. Fix: standardize all SendMessage examples to use actual parameter names (type, recipient, content, summary). Update skeleton to use kebab-case member name. Acceptance: all SendMessage examples use correct parameter names.

title: prompts/ directory creation is redundant between RULES.md Step 0 and pantry.md Review Mode
description:
Root cause: RULES.md Step 0 (line 86/115) creates the prompts/ directory via brace expansion in mkdir. pantry.md Review Mode Step 3 (line 107/110) also says 'Create the prompts directory if needed.' The redundancy is harmless (mkdir -p is idempotent) but creates confusion about who owns directory creation.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:115 -- mkdir -p includes prompts/ via brace expansion
- pantry.md:107-110 -- 'Create the prompts directory if needed'

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 11, P3), Correctness (Finding 7, P3)
Both reviewers independently identified the same redundancy. P3 because the behavior is correct either way.

Fix: Keep both (belt-and-suspenders) but add a comment in pantry.md: 'The Queen pre-creates this directory at Step 0, but create if needed as a safety net.'

Acceptance criteria:
- The intentional redundancy is documented with a comment in pantry.md

title: Pass 1-H: Verify 15 beads against README, CONTRIBUTING, SETUP, GLOSSARY docs
description:
Scope: Verify 15 open beads that reference README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SETUP.md, GLOSSARY.md, MEMORY.md, or SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md against the current file state.

Bead IDs: ant-farm-0jn7, ant-farm-28aq, ant-farm-4lcv, ant-farm-9hxz, ant-farm-c1n2, ant-farm-dwfe, ant-farm-h41z, ant-farm-maml, ant-farm-pxsk, ant-farm-q3o6, ant-farm-rhfl, ant-farm-vvm, ant-farm-y719, ant-farm-yzj, ant-farm-zv46

Codebase files to read: README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, orchestration/SETUP.md, orchestration/GLOSSARY.md, orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md, and the user's memory file at the path referenced by MEMORY.md beads

Context: These beads cover documentation issues: README architecture diagram missing Pest Control dual role, deprecated agent row formatting, SETUP.md wrong path for SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md, SETUP.md duplicate content between Quick Setup and Recipe Card, GLOSSARY.md pre-push hook entry gaps, MEMORY.md stale references (deleted session dir, colony-tsa elimination status, TBD caveats), and SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE stale hardcoded values. Known exact duplicates: ant-farm-0jn7/ant-farm-zv46 (README architecture diagram), ant-farm-4lcv/ant-farm-c1n2 (deprecated agent row), ant-farm-9hxz/ant-farm-h41z (SETUP.md wrong path), ant-farm-q3o6/ant-farm-y719 (SETUP.md duplicate content).

Process for each bead:
1. Read the bead's full record from .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-H-input.jsonl
2. Read the bead's title and description (10 of 15 lack descriptions — infer intent from title)
3. Check the referenced location(s) in the current codebase
4. Assign verdict: STILL_VALID, ALREADY_FIXED, IRRELEVANT, NEEDS_CONTEXT, or DUPLICATE_SUSPECT
5. For DUPLICATE_SUSPECT, check against .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/all-bead-titles.txt

Output: Write JSON array to .beads/agent-summaries/_session-39adef65/audit/pass1-batch-H-output.json

Per-bead output schema: {"bead_id": "...", "title": "...", "verdict": "...", "confidence": "...", "evidence": "...", "priority_check": "...", "duplicate_of": null | "...", "notes": ""}

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Output file contains exactly 15 entries
- [ ] Output is valid JSON array
- [ ] Known duplicates (4 pairs) are marked DUPLICATE_SUSPECT
- [ ] Every ALREADY_FIXED verdict cites specific evidence
- [ ] MEMORY.md beads account for the fact that MEMORY.md is a user-maintained file outside the repo

title: reviews.md polling loop angle-bracket placeholders lack explanatory comment
Session 7edaafbb R1: miscellaneous P3 polish findings (7 items)
description: Collection of 7 standalone P3 findings from round 1 review: SETUP.md nested code fence, extract_agent_section docstring, comment precision issues.

title: Undefined $CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH shell variable in failure artifact bash blocks
description:
## Root Cause

The fix for ant-farm-84qf added `cat > "$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH"` heredoc writes in three failure-path bash blocks. In all three instances, `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` is referenced as a shell variable (dollar-prefix syntax) but is never assigned within the bash block scope. The variable originates as a template placeholder (`{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}`) that `build-review-prompts.sh` fills via `fill_slot`, but the bash code uses shell variable syntax (`$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH`) which bypasses the placeholder substitution mechanism entirely. At runtime, the variable expands to an empty string (or triggers an unset-variable error with `set -u`), and the `cat >` command fails -- no FAILED artifact lands at the expected path.

This means ant-farm-84qf's core goal -- distinguishing "Big Head failed" (FAILED artifact exists) from "Big Head never ran" (no file at path) -- is not achieved on any failure path.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:588` -- polling timeout bash block uses `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` without assignment
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:777` -- Pest Control timeout bash block uses `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` without assignment
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:93` -- skeleton polling timeout bash block uses `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` without assignment (lower severity: LLM can read the literal path from the brief's "Consolidated output:" field, but the bash block itself is still broken if executed literally)

## Fix

In each bash block that uses `"$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH"`, either:

Option A (preferred): Add an explicit shell variable assignment at the top of each block that uses the template placeholder syntax so `fill_slot` substitutes the literal path:
```bash
CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH="{{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}}"
```

Option B: Replace the shell variable reference with the template placeholder directly in the heredoc redirect target, relying on Pantry substitution before delivery.

For `reviews.md` specifically (which is documentation/instructions content copied verbatim, not processed by `fill_slot`), Option A requires that the assignment use a literal path or that the file be routed through `fill_slot`. If neither is feasible, add a clear instruction adjacent to each bash block that Big Head must define the variable from the brief before running the block.

## Changes Needed

1. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:~586` -- Add `CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH=` assignment before the `cat >` at L588
2. `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:~775` -- Add `CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH=` assignment before the `cat >` at L777
3. `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:~91` -- Add `CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH=` assignment before the `cat >` at L93, or convert to `{{CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH}}` placeholder syntax

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every failure-path bash block that references `$CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH` has an in-scope shell variable assignment for it
- [ ] Running each bash block in isolation (no enclosing variable scope) produces a file at the expected consolidated output path
- [ ] ant-farm-84qf AC3 is re-verified: downstream consumers can distinguish "Big Head failed" from "Big Head never ran"

title: fix: DMVDC Nitpicker artifact naming in checkpoints.md does not match actual filenames
description:
checkpoints.md:475 documents DMVDC Nitpicker artifact naming as `pc-{TASK_SUFFIX}-dmvdc-review-{timestamp}.md` with example TASK_SUFFIX values like `review-clarity`, `review-edge` (line 478).

Actual filenames in _session-068ecc83/pc/:
- pc-review-correctness-dmvdc-20260221-182700.md
- pc-review-edge-cases-dmvdc-20260221-182700.md

Two deviations from the documented format:
1. The `-review` suffix after `dmvdc` is absent in actual files (documented: `...-dmvdc-review-{ts}`, actual: `...-dmvdc-{ts}`)
2. The TASK_SUFFIX values used are bare review type names (`correctness`, `edge-cases`) rather than the documented compound `review-{type}` (`review-clarity`, `review-edge`)

Root cause: The naming convention in checkpoints.md was written speculatively and never validated against actual Pest Control output.

Current behavior: Anyone querying pc/ for the documented pattern `*dmvdc-review*` will find zero matches. The actual pattern is `*-dmvdc-{timestamp}*` with the review type before `dmvdc`.

Fix: Update checkpoints.md:475 to match actual convention and update example values on line 478.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:475: Change to `pc-{review-type}-dmvdc-{timestamp}.md`
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:478: Update example values to `correctness`, `edge-cases`, `clarity`, `excellence`

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md DMVDC Nitpicker naming matches actual artifact filenames
- [ ] Example TASK_SUFFIX values match actual Nitpicker review type names
- [ ] Querying pc/ with the documented pattern finds actual files

Audit reference: DRIFT-010 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Pantry review return format doesn't show Big Head consolidation file
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 6 return table shows 4 review types but the Big Head consolidation data file path is listed separately below the table. Big-head-skeleton.md Step 0 says 'Read your consolidation brief from {DATA_FILE_PATH}'. A fresh Queen might miss the consolidation file path when parsing Pantry output. Make it prominent.

title: extract_agent_section exactly one delimiter assumption unguarded
RULES.md Step 3 prose polish: milestone placement and variable naming
description: Root cause: Two scanability issues in RULES.md Step 3. (a) WAVE_WWD_PASS progress log milestone at L131 is embedded mid-paragraph, hard to spot. (b) Bash variable TIMESTAMP (L147-149) differs from template placeholder REVIEW_TIMESTAMP, adding mental overhead. Fix: Move progress log line to visually distinct position; rename bash variable to REVIEW_TIMESTAMP. Found by: Excellence (EX-F001), Clarity (CL-F001).

title: parse-progress-log.sh hardening gaps (overwrite, dead branch, corruption)
description: Root cause: First-pass implementation handles happy path but lacks defensive coding for edge scenarios. (1) scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:157-224 silently overwrites existing resume-plan.md. (2) scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:148-151 redundant dead branch with misleading comment. (3) scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:117-124 no validation for corrupted/malformed log lines. Fix: Single hardening pass. Acceptance criteria: (1) Overwrite produces stderr notice. (2) Dead branch comment is accurate. (3) Malformed lines rejected with timestamp validation. Found by: Excellence (EX-2, EX-4, EX-8 all P3).

title: Progress log milestone naming diverges from ant-farm-0b4k spec
description: Root cause: Progress log implementation uses step-numbered keys (step0-step6) with per-wave granularity, while ant-farm-0b4k specifies event-named milestones (SCOUT_COMPLETE, DIRT_PUSHER_COMPLETE per agent, SESSION_CLOSE_STARTED, etc.) with per-agent granularity. Per-agent recovery is less precise than spec intended. Affected files: orchestration/RULES.md:59,98,115,123,186,207,210,213,216 and scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:130. Fix: Either (a) add per-dirt-pusher log entries and rename milestone keys to match bead spec, or (b) update ant-farm-0b4k to reflect implemented design. Acceptance criteria: (1) Progress log milestone names match authoritative spec. (2) Per-agent recovery granularity is either implemented or explicitly descoped. Found by: Correctness (CR-3 P2, CR-4 P3).

title: AGG-030: Fix silent task dropping when Scout bd show fails
description:
scout.md instructs the Scout to skip a tasks metadata file if bd show fails. But the Pantry expects metadata for every task ID the Queen passes. If metadata is missing, the task is silently dropped without the Queen knowing.

Fix: Scout writes a placeholder metadata file with error details instead of skipping. Pantry fails the individual task with clear error message if metadata is missing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md instructs writing placeholder error metadata instead of skipping failed tasks
- [ ] pantry.md includes fail-fast logic for missing or error-placeholder metadata files
- [ ] A failed bd show produces a metadata file that the Pantry can detect and report to the Queen

title: GLOSSARY.md ESV checkpoint not propagated to Checkpoint Acronyms section
description:
## Root Cause
ESV was added to the GLOSSARY.md Workflow Concepts table (L61) but was not propagated to the Checkpoint Acronyms section. Three prose locations still say "five checkpoints" instead of "six", the Checkpoint Acronyms table has no ESV row, and the Pest Control role description at L86 lists only five checkpoints.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:L46` -- "five checkpoints" count (from Correctness, Drift reviews)
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:L56` -- "five checkpoints" count (from Drift review)
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:L67` -- "five checkpoints" count + section header (from Correctness, Drift reviews)
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:L69-L76` -- Checkpoint Acronyms table missing ESV row (from Correctness, Drift reviews)
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:L86` -- Pest Control role says "five checkpoints (SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB)" (from Drift review)

## Fix
In one pass over GLOSSARY.md: (1) change "five" to "six" at L46, L56, L67; (2) add ESV row to Checkpoint Acronyms table after CCB; (3) update Pest Control role at L86 to "six checkpoints (SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB, ESV)".

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/GLOSSARY.md`: Update count in 3 locations, add ESV table row, update Pest Control description

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GLOSSARY.md mentions "six checkpoints" in all three locations (L46, L56, L67)
- [ ] Checkpoint Acronyms table has an ESV row with full definition
- [ ] Pest Control role description at L86 lists all six checkpoints including ESV

title: scrub-pii.sh email regex has limited pattern coverage
description: The email regex does not match RFC 5321 quoted local parts or uncommon characters. Low risk given typical data sources. Fix: widen pattern or document intentional coverage scope. Consolidated from: Edge Cases F8.

title: Minor style and organization inconsistencies across documentation (composite)
description:
## Root Cause

Documentation evolved organically without a consistent style guide. Multiple minor formatting, labeling, and organizational inconsistencies exist across files. These are individually trivial polish items grouped as a composite to preserve traceability without creating excessive bead noise.

## Affected Surfaces

- `CLAUDE.md:38` -- mixed bold+caps vs caps-only in same prohibition list (CL-01, clarity)
- `CLAUDE.md:56` -- near-duplicate sentence with different casing (CL-02, clarity)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:44` -- One-TeamCreate constraint buried in agent-specific subsection (CL-03, clarity)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md:165` -- comment claims simpler approach used but adjacent code uses "old" approach (CL-07, clarity)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:197` -- round 1 team unnamed; round 2+ named inline (CL-11, clarity)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:212-246` -- sunset clause buried at end of step (CL-12, clarity)
- `orchestration/SETUP.md:63` -- checkbox format used for non-interactive example content (CL-16, clarity)
- `orchestration/templates/scout.md:11-13` -- inconsistent depth of inline definitions for three terms (CL-17, clarity)
- `orchestration/templates/scout.md:119-121` -- letter-based sub-step labels unique to this step (CL-18, clarity)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:227-237` -- tmux send-keys failure path undocumented; low impact given sunset clause (EC-02, edge-cases)
- `orchestration/templates/scout.md:266-289` -- bd show failure fallback references bd list output that may not exist in all modes (EC-05, edge-cases)

## Fix

Address as part of a documentation style guide enforcement pass. Individual fixes are described in the original reviewer reports (clarity-review-20260222-162459.md and edge-cases-review-20260222-162459.md).

## Changes Needed

- See individual finding descriptions in the reviewer reports for specific file:line fixes
- Consider establishing a shared style guide to prevent future organic drift

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Prohibition bullets in CLAUDE.md use consistent formatting
- [ ] RULES.md round 1 bullet names all 4 reviewers inline
- [ ] Sunset clause for Step 3b-v is visible at the top of the step, not buried at the end
- [ ] scout.md sub-step labels use numbered lists (not letter labels) consistent with other steps

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md enforcement strategy references unimplemented automation
description: The enforcement strategy at PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md:203-207 mentions optional grep validation via git hook or CI but this is not implemented. Creates false impression of automated enforcement. Fix: implement automation or update text to state compliance is manual. Consolidated from: Excellence F5.

title: CCB Check 7 calendar date scope too broad for same-day sessions
description:
## Root Cause
CCB Check 7 in `checkpoints.md:L615-L618` scopes `bd list` to `--after={SESSION_START_DATE}` using calendar-date granularity. If two sessions run on the same day, beads from the earlier session appear in this list, producing false positives.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:L615-L618` — CCB Check 7 date scope (from edge-cases review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bead scoping uses session-level timestamp precision (ISO 8601 datetime) or cross-references the session's progress.log
- [ ] Same-day multi-session scenarios do not produce false positives

title: compose-review-skeletons.sh extract_agent_section includes YAML frontmatter body
description:
Root cause: compose-review-skeletons.sh:71-74 uses awk '/^---$/{found=1; next} found{print}' to extract everything after the first '---'. But YAML frontmatter uses a PAIR of '---' delimiters. This pattern starts printing after the first ---  including frontmatter content between the two delimiters.

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/compose-review-skeletons.sh:71-74

Found by: Edge Cases (P3 - F5). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Count --- occurrences and start printing after the second: awk '/^---$/{count++; next} count>=2{print}'

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Only content after closing --- of frontmatter is extracted
- [ ] Frontmatter fields are not included in skeleton body

title: Mixed placeholder conventions and minor template polish in reviews.md
description: reviews.md line 591 uses {session-dir} (curly braces) while all other 19 occurrences use <session-dir> (angle brackets). Project convention is angle brackets for instructional placeholders. Also: polling loop placeholder at lines 379-382 lacks explicit substitution instruction (low risk, convention established by brief), and escalation format block at lines 540-547 has minor nesting risk. Fix: change line 591 from {session-dir} to <session-dir>. Other items informational only. Acceptance: grep '{session-dir}' reviews.md returns 0 matches.

title: parse-progress-log.sh UNREACHABLE comment reasoning incomplete
AGG-016: Fix contradictory Big Head model assignment
description:
reviews.md specifies Big Head: opus as the model, but the skeleton template has no model specification. A Queen following both instructions has no mechanism to set the Big Head model to opus in the TeamCreate call.

Fix: Add model configuration instructions to TeamCreate call format or move model specification into the skeleton template.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] The Big Head model (opus) is specified in exactly one authoritative location
- [ ] The TeamCreate call format or skeleton template includes the model parameter
- [ ] A cold Queen can determine the correct model for Big Head without ambiguity

title: Big Head spawning mechanics unclear for fresh Queen
description: big-head-skeleton.md says Big Head is a team member (NOT a separate Task agent) but uses {PLACEHOLDER} syntax from Task tool templates. A fresh Queen would not know how to wire this through TeamCreate + SendMessage. Add explicit Queen-facing instructions showing the TeamCreate → SendMessage workflow with an example.

title: Incomplete pantry-review deprecation across docs and agent configs
description:
Root cause: The deprecation of pantry-review was applied to RULES.md and pantry.md but not propagated to all downstream references. Multiple files still reference pantry-review as if it were a live agent type.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/RULES.md:182-183 -- duplicated deprecated row with inconsistent wording
- orchestration/templates/scout.md:61 -- stale pantry-review in exclusion list
- orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:28,81 -- references pantry-review.md as live
- README.md:275 -- lists pantry-review as active agent

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F5), Edge Cases (P3 - F10), Excellence (P3 - F6, F7)
4 findings across 3 reviewers, all P3. Same root cause: incomplete deprecation rollout.

Fix: Remove or mark deprecated all pantry-review references in GLOSSARY.md, README.md, scout.md exclusion list, and unify the RULES.md deprecated row wording.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] No file references pantry-review without a deprecation marker
- [ ] GLOSSARY.md and README.md updated
- [ ] scout.md exclusion list cleaned up

title: reviews.md: REVIEW_ROUND guard relies on LLM interpreting exit code (defense-in-depth note)
Correctness Redux instructs Nitpickers to run bd show with undocumented tool dependency
Incomplete ant-farm-4l0t fix: WARN block still lists DMVDC, contradicting new PARTIAL definition
Pantry partial verdict table placement ambiguous -- per-failure or end-of-loop
description: The 'On any failure above' block in pantry.md (lines 68-74) with the partial verdict table appears after Condition 3 at the same indentation level. It is ambiguous whether this block runs once after processing ALL tasks (intended behavior) or after EACH individual failure (mid-loop). The placement after Condition 3 could be read as specific to that condition rather than a general end-of-loop instruction. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:68-74. Fix: Move the partial verdict table instruction to after the per-task loop ends and clarify: 'After processing all tasks, if any failures occurred, return a single partial verdict table.'

title: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md has policy text embedded in placeholder list
description: The AGENT_TYPE placeholder entry at dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:19-22 includes a multi-line authority chain and override policy, making the compact list harder to scan. Fix: extract policy into separate subsection. Consolidated from: Clarity F9.

title: pantry.md fail-fast conditions use inconsistent signal words
CLAUDE.md and 3 other files still instruct Queen to wait for user approval
description:
## Root Cause
The approval-gate removal in `orchestration/RULES.md` (ant-farm-fomy) was not propagated to four other files that encode the same "wait for user approval" assumption. Most critically, `CLAUDE.md:50` is loaded as the system prompt for every session and contains "Key rule: WAIT for user approval of execution strategy before spawning any agents." Because system prompt instructions take precedence over mid-turn file reads (like RULES.md), this rule could override the RULES.md change and make it functionally inert.

The four affected files span system prompt, checkpoint definitions, reference documentation, and user-facing README — all encoding the old approval-gate assumption.

## Affected Surfaces
- `CLAUDE.md:50` — System prompt "Key rule: WAIT for user approval" directly contradicts RULES.md:97 auto-approve (P1 — from Drift D-5)
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:689` — SSV verdict section says Queen will present strategy to user for approval (P1 conditional — from Drift D-3)
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:717` — Queen's Response section: "User approval is required even on SSV PASS — this is a deliberate design choice, not an omission" (P1 conditional — from Drift D-3)
- `orchestration/reference/dependency-analysis.md:64` — Scout output description says Queen waits for approval (P2 — from Drift D-4)
- `README.md:85` — User-facing docs describe approval checkpoint that no longer exists (P3 — from Drift D-6)

## Fix
Sweep all four files and update references to match the new auto-approve behavior:
1. `CLAUDE.md:50`: Remove or update the "Key rule" to reflect auto-proceed after SSV PASS. This is the most urgent fix — without it, the RULES.md change may be a no-op.
2. `checkpoints.md:689,717`: Update SSV verdict and Queen's Response to describe auto-proceed behavior instead of user approval.
3. `dependency-analysis.md:64`: Update Scout output description to remove "waits for approval."
4. `README.md:85`: Update user-facing description to reflect automatic approval after SSV PASS.

## Changes Needed
- `CLAUDE.md:50`: Remove or rewrite "WAIT for user approval" key rule
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:689`: Update SSV PASS verdict text
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:717`: Rewrite Queen's Response "On PASS" section
- `orchestration/reference/dependency-analysis.md:64`: Update Scout process description
- `README.md:85`: Update user-facing workflow description

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` no longer instructs the Queen to wait for user approval of execution strategy
- [ ] `checkpoints.md` SSV verdict and Queen's Response sections describe auto-proceed (not user approval)
- [ ] `dependency-analysis.md` Scout output description no longer says Queen waits for approval
- [ ] `README.md` workflow description reflects automatic approval after SSV PASS
- [ ] No file in the repository contains instructions for the Queen to seek user approval of strategy after SSV PASS (grep verification)

title: ant-farm-01a8 acceptance criteria formally unmet after conditional-check revert
description:
## Root Cause
The fix for ant-farm-01a8 (placeholder guard incomplete for round-1 paths when REVIEW_ROUND is corrupt) went through two commits: 06cf404 made all four report path checks unconditional (satisfying acceptance criteria 1 and 2), then 365a0d9 reverted to a conditional check (`if [ "$REVIEW_ROUND" -eq 1 ]; then`) for clarity/drift paths. The revert was technically correct -- round 2+ briefs contain unsubstituted angle-bracket placeholders for clarity/drift paths, so unconditional checking would always trigger a false PLACEHOLDER_ERROR. However, the bead's acceptance criteria as written still say "All four report paths are checked for unresolved placeholders unconditionally" (criterion 1) and "A corrupt REVIEW_ROUND value still triggers an error but does not skip validation of other paths" (criterion 2). These criteria are formally unmet.

The runtime behavior is correct due to the REVIEW_ROUND pre-validation invariant (the `case` statement catches corruption before reaching the conditional branch). The issue is a documentation/tracking gap: the criteria text does not reflect the implemented conditional approach.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:584` -- acceptance criteria text in the placeholder guard section (from Correctness review)

## Fix
Update bead ant-farm-01a8 acceptance criteria to reflect the conditional-check approach. Criterion 1 should state that correctness/edge-cases paths are checked unconditionally while clarity/drift paths are checked conditionally (round 1 only), with rationale. Criterion 2 should note the REVIEW_ROUND pre-validation invariant as the safety guarantee.

## Changes Needed
- Bead `ant-farm-01a8`: Update acceptance criteria text to match implemented behavior. Add a note referencing commit 365a0d9 rationale.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bead ant-farm-01a8 acceptance criteria accurately describe the conditional-check approach (unconditional for correctness/edge-cases, conditional for clarity/drift)
- [ ] Criteria reference the REVIEW_ROUND pre-validation invariant as the safety guarantee
- [ ] A bead note documents the 365a0d9 revert rationale

Big Head cannot receive Pest Control messages — timeout on every CCB exchange
description:
In session 2bb21f22, Pest Control sent CCB PASS verdicts to Big Head multiple times (observed by the Queen in Pest Control's idle notification summaries), but Big Head consistently timed out waiting for them. This happened in both round 1 and round 2, requiring Queen intervention each time to confirm the CCB verdict and instruct Big Head to proceed with bead filing.

Root cause (confirmed by Big Head): After sending the consolidated report path to Pest Control, Big Head runs `sleep 60` in Bash to "wait" for the reply. Messages in Claude Code teams are delivered as new conversation turns. While Big Head is blocked inside a Bash `sleep`, it cannot process incoming turns. Pest Control's reply arrives during the sleep window but Big Head has no way to see it. When the sleep completes, Big Head is already in the timeout branch and declares failure. The sleep-based wait pattern fights against the turn-based message delivery model.

Interim mitigation: The Queen manually confirms CCB PASS (having observed it in idle notifications) and instructs Big Head to proceed. This works but adds latency and Queen context cost for every review round.

Fix: Replace sleep-based waiting with turn-based waiting:

1. Big Head sends consolidated report path to Pest Control (existing behavior, keep as-is)
2. Big Head ends its turn immediately after sending — no sleep, no polling
3. Pest Control's reply arrives as a new teammate message on Big Head's next turn
4. Big Head processes the verdict and proceeds with bead filing

The "wait up to 60 seconds; retry once if no response; escalate after 120s" language in reviews.md Step 4 assumes an active polling mechanism. For the team-based messaging model, replace with: "Send the report to Pest Control. End your turn. If no reply is received after 2 subsequent turns of processing other work, retry once. If still no reply after 2 more turns, escalate to Queen."

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/reviews.md: Update Big Head Consolidation Protocol Step 4 — replace wall-clock timeout language with turn-based waiting protocol
- orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md: Update Step 10 await instructions — remove any language that implies sleep/poll, explicitly say "end your turn and await Pest Control's reply as a new message"

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Big Head receives Pest Control's CCB verdict without timeout in a test session
- [ ] No `sleep` calls used for message waiting in Big Head's workflow
- [ ] No Queen intervention required to relay CCB verdicts
- [ ] reviews.md Step 4 timeout protocol uses turn-count language, not wall-clock seconds
- [ ] big-head-skeleton.md Step 10 explicitly instructs "end your turn" after sending to Pest Control

title: Dual TIMESTAMP/REVIEW_TIMESTAMP naming convention creates cognitive burden
description:
## Root Cause

RULES.md introduces two different identifiers for the same concept: `${TIMESTAMP}` as a shell variable and `{REVIEW_TIMESTAMP}` as a placeholder. The guidance says to use one in bash and the other in prose/templates. No other placeholder in the system uses this dual-name convention, creating ongoing cognitive burden for every reader.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:148-149` -- dual naming: shell `${TIMESTAMP}` vs placeholder `{REVIEW_TIMESTAMP}` (from clarity review)

## Fix

Unify the names. Either rename the shell variable to `REVIEW_TIMESTAMP` throughout, or rename the placeholder to match the shell variable. The bash snippet would then use the unified name, eliminating the mapping entirely.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md:148-149`: Choose one name and use it consistently in both shell and prose contexts
- All references to the old name in RULES.md and any templates that use `{REVIEW_TIMESTAMP}`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Only one name is used for the review timestamp concept (shell and prose use the same identifier)
- [ ] No inline mapping explanation is needed between two different names
- [ ] All bash snippets and template references use the unified name

title: Add pre-flight Scout strategy verification checkpoint via haiku PC agent
description:
Root cause: The Scout's execution strategy (wave groupings, task-to-wave assignments, file conflict analysis) is currently validated only by human approval. This creates a bottleneck for automation and doesn't catch mechanical errors like file/task mismatches or intra-wave dependency violations that a human reviewer might miss.

Fix: Add a lightweight haiku-model Pest Control checkpoint that runs automatically after the Scout produces its strategy. This verifies the strategy's correctness through three mechanical checks before any Queens or dirt-pushers are spawned.

Verification checks (all are set comparisons, ideal for haiku):

1. No file overlaps within a wave: For each wave, collect all affected files from all tasks in that wave. If any file appears in two or more tasks within the same wave, FAIL with details.

2. File lists match bead descriptions: For each task in the strategy, compare the Scout's reported affected files against the bead's actual affected files (from bd show). Mismatch → FAIL.

3. No intra-wave dependency violations: For each wave, check that no task in wave N depends on (is blocked by) another task in wave N. Dependencies must flow across waves, not within them.

Flow:
1. Scout produces strategy with wave plan
2. Haiku PC agent reads strategy + beads for all tasks in the plan
3. Runs all three checks
4. PASS → proceed to Pantry/implementation. FAIL → re-run Scout or escalate to user.

This checkpoint is valuable independently of meta-orchestration — it hardens the existing single-Queen workflow by catching Scout errors before they cascade into implementation. In a meta-orchestration context, it replaces the human approval gate entirely.

Model: haiku (mechanical set comparisons, no judgment needed)
Agent type: pest-control or dedicated pre-flight-pc variant

Affected files:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md — Add new checkpoint definition (Scout Strategy Verification / SSV)
- orchestration/RULES.md — Add SSV checkpoint after Scout returns, before Pantry spawns
- Potentially a new skeleton template for the SSV PC agent

Acceptance criteria:
1. Haiku PC agent runs after Scout returns and before Pantry is spawned
2. All three checks (file overlap, file list match, dependency ordering) are performed
3. PASS allows workflow to continue without human approval
4. FAIL halts workflow and reports specific violations (which tasks, which files, which dependencies)
5. Checkpoint report written to {session-dir}/pc/pc-session-ssv-{timestamp}.md

title: AGG-017: Remove circular reference in Pantry Big Head data file instructions
description:
pantry.md instructs the Pantry to read reviews.md before composing the Big Head data file, but pantry.md Section 2 already IS the review mode instructions. This creates a circular dependency where Pantry reads pantry.md which says read reviews.md which may reference pantry.md.

Fix: Inline required Big Head information into pantry.md Section 2, or clarify the reference is forward-only (read reviews.md for Big Head output format, not Pantry workflow).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] pantry.md Section 2 is self-contained for Big Head data file composition (no circular refs)
- [ ] If reviews.md is still referenced, the reference specifies exactly which section to read and why
- [ ] A cold Pantry agent can compose the Big Head data file by reading only pantry.md

title: AGG-028: Standardize timestamp format string across templates
description:
checkpoints.md specifies YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS (uppercase) but examples show YYYYMMDD-HHmmss (lowercase minutes). big-head-skeleton.md defines yet another format with UTC qualifier. These could cause mismatched paths or naming errors.

Fix: Standardize to YYYYMMDD-HHmmss with UTC qualifier. Define in one canonical location and reference from all templates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] One canonical location defines the timestamp format string with UTC qualifier
- [ ] grep for timestamp format definitions across orchestration/ shows all match the canonical format
- [ ] All examples in templates use the standardized format string

title: queen-state.md escalation cap field missing post-decision terminal state
Termination Rule wording ambiguity: 'directly' may imply skipping Round 1 P3 handling
AGG-014: Fix undefined AskUserQuestion tool reference in CLAUDE.md
description:
The prompt-engineering workflow in CLAUDE.md requires AskUserQuestion as a tool name, but no compatibility note exists for environments where the tool is named differently (e.g., AskQuestion in Cursor). Cold agents will fail invoking a nonexistent tool.

Fix: Use platform-correct tool name, add compatibility note for variants, or abstract to ask the user using the available question tool.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CLAUDE.md prompt-engineering workflow uses a platform-agnostic tool reference or documents variants
- [ ] The instruction works without modification in Claude Code (where AskUserQuestion exists)
- [ ] A compatibility note lists known tool name variants across platforms

title: fix: remove archived pantry-review from scout.md exclusion list
description:
orchestration/templates/scout.md:63 lists `pantry-review` in the agent exclusion list: "scout-organizer, pantry-impl, pantry-review, pest-control, nitpicker, big-head". The pantry-review agent is archived (orchestration/_archive/pantry-review.md) and has no file in agents/.

GLOSSARY.md correctly uses strikethrough for pantry-review. pantry.md correctly warns against spawning it. Only the scout.md exclusion list still references it as if it were active.

Since pantry-review has no agent file in agents/, it would not appear in the Scout's catalog anyway — the reference is harmless but signals the list was not updated on deprecation.

Fix: Remove `pantry-review` from the exclusion list in scout.md:63.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md exclusion list no longer references pantry-review

Audit reference: DRIFT-021 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Stale 'user approval' label in parse-progress-log.sh SCOUT_COMPLETE step
description:
## Root Cause

`scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:80` still contains the label "Scout Complete: Recon (Scout + SSV gate + user approval)" but user approval after SSV PASS was removed in ant-farm-fomy. The label is displayed in crash-recovery resume plans presented to users, describing a step that no longer requires user involvement as if it still does.

## Affected Surfaces

- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:L80` -- stale "user approval" in SCOUT_COMPLETE step label (from correctness review, drift review)

## Fix

Update line 80 to remove the stale "user approval" reference: `echo "Scout Complete: Recon (Scout + SSV gate)"`.

## Changes Needed

- `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh`: Change line 80 label from `"Scout Complete: Recon (Scout + SSV gate + user approval)"` to `"Scout Complete: Recon (Scout + SSV gate)"`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:80` does not contain the string "user approval"
- [ ] SCOUT_COMPLETE step label accurately reflects current workflow (SSV gate, no user approval)
- [ ] `grep -r "user approval" scripts/` returns no matches

title: Stale briefing.md descriptions in RULES.md still reference user approval
description:
## Root Cause
When the Step 1b approval gate was removed in ant-farm-fomy, the two prose descriptions of `briefing.md` in other sections of `orchestration/RULES.md` were not updated. Both still describe the file in terms of the now-removed approval decision:

- Line 28 (Queen Read Permissions): "required for Step 1 approval decision"
- Line 469 (Session Directory): "read by Queen before user approval"
