This is an incomplete sweep of approval-related descriptions within the same file that was otherwise correctly updated.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:28` — "required for Step 1 approval decision" (from Drift D-1)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:469` — "read by Queen before user approval" (from Drift D-2)

## Fix
Update both descriptions to reflect the new auto-proceed behavior:
- Line 28: Change to "Scout-generated strategy summary, reviewed after SSV PASS before auto-proceeding to Step 2"
- Line 469: Change to "written by Scout (Step 1a); strategy summary read by Queen; auto-approved after SSV PASS"

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md:28`: Update briefing.md description in Queen Read Permissions section
- `orchestration/RULES.md:469`: Update briefing.md description in Session Directory section

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Line 28 no longer references "approval decision"
- [ ] Line 469 no longer references "user approval"
- [ ] Both descriptions accurately reflect the auto-proceed-after-SSV-PASS behavior

title: [Review RC-4] REVIEW_ROUND placeholder not guarded in reviews.md polling loop
description: P2: reviews.md:502 has REVIEW_ROUND={{REVIEW_ROUND}} shell assignment. If fill-review-slots.sh is bypassed, the unsubstituted {{REVIEW_ROUND}} causes a confusing arithmetic error. Fix: add a guard check after the assignment.

title: Missing preflight validation for required code-reviewer.md agent
description:
## Root Cause

The `code-reviewer.md` agent file is a hard dependency for Nitpicker team spawning, but it must be manually installed at `~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md`. There is no automated preflight check. Failure is only discovered at runtime during the review phase, potentially hours into a session, making recovery disruptive.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/SETUP.md:39-42` -- documents manual install requirement with no automated validation (from edge-cases review)

## Fix

Add a preflight check to Quick Setup or `sync-to-claude.sh` that warns when the file is missing before a session begins.

## Changes Needed

- `scripts/sync-to-claude.sh` or `orchestration/SETUP.md`: Add a preflight check:
  ```bash
  [ -f ~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md ] || echo "WARNING: ~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md missing -- Nitpicker team will fail to spawn. Copy it manually before starting a session."
  ```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A warning is emitted if `~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md` is missing when `sync-to-claude.sh` runs or during Quick Setup
- [ ] The warning message names the file path and explains the consequence (Nitpicker team spawn failure)

title: [Review RC-5] SETUP.md troubleshooting instructs Queen to run bd show
description: P2: SETUP.md:210-215 troubleshooting block tells the Queen to run bd show directly, contradicting the Scout-delegation discipline in RULES.md. Fix: reword to instruct spawning the Scout instead.

title: Task ID suffix terminology inconsistent across templates
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md uses <task-id>, pantry.md uses {task-id-suffix}, checkpoints.md uses both interchangeably. A fresh agent cannot tell if it should use the full ID (hs_website-74g.1) or just the suffix (74g.1). Standardize to one term across all orchestration files.

title: Stale step6 reference at RULES.md:310 not updated by ant-farm-fuje rename
description: The ant-farm-fuje fix (commit d6e4927) renamed all milestone keys from old numeric names (step0-step6) to event-based names (SESSION_INIT through SESSION_COMPLETE) in parse-progress-log.sh and RULES.md progress.log examples. However, it missed the Session Directory crash-recovery summary at RULES.md line 310, which still reads 'Exit 2: session already completed (step6 logged)' instead of '(SESSION_COMPLETE logged)'. This creates a contract inconsistency between the script behavior and Queen-facing documentation. Fix: change 'step6 logged' to 'SESSION_COMPLETE logged' at RULES.md:310. Acceptance criteria: grep for 'step6' in RULES.md returns zero matches.

title: Pantry failure artifact path collision across conditions for same task
description: All three Pantry failure conditions (Condition 1: missing files, Condition 2: incomplete metadata, Condition 3: placeholder contamination) write their failure artifacts to the same path: {session-dir}/prompts/task-{TASK_SUFFIX}-FAILED.md. While only one condition fires per task in the current sequential waterfall, the shared path means: (1) the failure type is only distinguishable by reading the artifact content, not the filename; (2) a future refactoring that makes checks non-sequential could cause silent overwrites. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:33, pantry.md:47, pantry.md:58. Fix: Low priority. Add a comment documenting the sequential-check invariant. Optionally add condition type to filename.

title: sync-to-claude.sh script selection has no explanatory comment
description:
Root cause: sync-to-claude.sh:27-33 iterates over two hardcoded script names without explaining why only these two are synced and not others (like sync-to-claude.sh itself).

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/sync-to-claude.sh:27-33

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F10). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Add a brief comment: '# Sync only orchestration pipeline scripts (not sync-to-claude.sh itself)'.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comment explains the script selection rationale

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md Enforcement Strategy mixes completed and aspirational items
pantry-review.md self-validation checklist lacks remediation guidance per item
Standalone documentation polish items (4 sub-issues)
description: Four independent P3 documentation gaps grouped for filing efficiency: (a) SSV acronym not expanded at first use in RULES.md:L88 Step 1b header. (b) Shell quoting error in CONTRIBUTING.md:L147 example (backslash-n in double quotes is literal, not newline). (c) Ambiguous priority labels on Nitpicker specializations in GLOSSARY.md:L84. (d) No-delete rsync consequence undocumented in CONTRIBUTING.md:L161 (stale files accumulate in ~/.claude/). Found by: Clarity (CL-F002, CL-F003, CL-F004), Excellence (EX-F004).

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md not cross-referenced from RULES.md or any template
pantry.md references undefined 'two-script approach'
description:
Root cause: pantry.md:238-243 Section 2 deprecation notice says 'superseded by the two-script approach introduced in Section 1 Step 2.5' without naming the scripts. The term 'two-script approach' appears only once in the file with no definition or link.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/templates/pantry.md:238-243

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F7). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Add script names parenthetically: 'the two-script approach (compose-review-skeletons.sh + fill-review-slots.sh)'.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Deprecation notice names both scripts explicitly

title: scout.md PICK ONE bracket syntax resembles regex/BNF notation
description:
Root cause: scout.md:118-119 uses 'PICK ONE: [type-a | type-b]' format for tie-breaking results. The bracket-pipe syntax resembles regex or BNF notation and could be confused with optional/alternative notation rather than a literal list.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/templates/scout.md:118-119

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F6). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Use plainer format like 'PICK ONE: debugger OR performance-engineer'.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Tie-breaking format uses plain English connectors instead of bracket notation

title: checkpoints.md Checkpoint A Nitpickers uses 0-based numbering (0-6) while Dirt Pushers uses 1-based (1-7)
description:
Root cause: Within checkpoints.md, the Checkpoint A section for Nitpickers numbers its 7 checks as 0-6, while the Checkpoint A section for Dirt Pushers numbers its 7 checks as 1-7. Both claim '7 checks' (correct count), but the inconsistent indexing style within the same file and same checkpoint type creates confusion and could cause off-by-one reporting errors if Pest Control references checks by number.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:154 -- Nitpickers Checkpoint A checklist uses 0-based indexing (0-6)
- (Contrast: Dirt Pushers Checkpoint A at line 96 uses 1-based indexing 1-7)

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 6, P2)
Single reviewer finding but rated P2 due to the potential for off-by-one errors in verification reporting.

Fix: Renumber the Nitpickers Checkpoint A checklist from 1-7 to match the Dirt Pushers Checkpoint A convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both Checkpoint A sections (Dirt Pushers and Nitpickers) use 1-based numbering
- Check count text ('7 checks') matches the actual numbered items

title: Session ID collision risk from truncated shasum with epoch-second input
description:
Root cause: RULES.md line 85 generates session IDs via 'date +%s | shasum | head -c 6' which produces a 6-char hex string. The input is epoch seconds, so two Queens started within the same second would produce identical session IDs, causing artifact directory collisions.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:85 -- Session ID generation command

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 1, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because concurrent Queens in the same repo are already implied to be unsupported by concurrency rules.

Fix: Either add per-invocation randomness (e.g., '(date +%s%N; echo $$) | shasum | head -c 6') or document that concurrent Queens are unsupported.

Acceptance criteria:
- Either the session ID has sufficient entropy to avoid same-second collisions, or the single-Queen constraint is explicitly documented

title: Polling loop in reviews.md Step 0a uses fragile wc -l line-counting with globs
description: The polling loop in reviews.md Step 0a (lines 370-383) uses a fragile pattern: it chains 4 ls commands with && into a MISSING_REPORTS variable, then checks wc -l equals 4 to determine all reports are present. Problems: (1) Variable name is inverted -- it stores found files, not missing ones. (2) If a glob matches multiple files (e.g., from a re-run), wc -l exceeds 4 and the loop never breaks, silently timing out. (3) No post-loop check distinguishes timeout from success -- the script falls through without error handling. (4) Shell state does not persist between Bash tool calls in Claude Code, so the loop variables would reset if Big Head splits execution across calls. Affected surfaces: reviews.md:370-383. Fix: Replace wc -l counting with individual [ -f ] checks per exact filename. Add post-loop timeout detection. Add comment requiring single-invocation execution.

title: fill-review-slots.sh accepts review round 0 as valid input
description:
Root cause: fill-review-slots.sh:80 validates review round with regex '^[0-9]+$' which accepts 0. The review system uses 1-based rounds. Round 0 would silently fall into the round 2+ branch (only correctness + edge-cases), skipping clarity and excellence reviews.

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/fill-review-slots.sh:78-83

Found by: Edge Cases (P3 - F4), Correctness (P3 - C3)
Both identified the same validation gap at the same line.

Fix: Change regex to '^[1-9][0-9]*$' or add explicit check for round >= 1.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Round 0 is rejected with an error message
- [ ] Round 1 and higher continue to work

title: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE stale review decision logic contradicts RULES.md
description:
## Root Cause

SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md was not updated after the review workflow was redesigned. The Review Wave section describes sequential reviews with per-agent time estimates, but the current workflow uses parallel TeamCreate. The Review Follow-Up Decision block has thresholds (<5/5-15/>15 raw issues) that contradict RULES.md Step 3c (<=5 root causes, round cap at 4).

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:207-224` -- Review Wave section describes sequential model; current uses parallel TeamCreate (from clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:226-237` -- Review Follow-Up Decision thresholds contradict RULES.md Step 3c (from clarity review)

## Fix

Update the Review Wave section to describe the TeamCreate-based parallel review model. Replace the decision thresholds block with a reference to RULES.md Step 3c, or mark the section as deprecated.

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:207-224`: Replace sequential review description with parallel TeamCreate model
- `orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md:226-237`: Replace thresholds with reference to RULES.md Step 3c

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Review Wave section describes parallel TeamCreate model (not sequential)
- [ ] Review Follow-Up Decision block references RULES.md Step 3c or is marked deprecated
- [ ] No specific issue-count thresholds that contradict the root-cause-based triage in RULES.md

title: fix: one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint undocumented in operator-facing docs
description:
Claude Code supports only one TeamCreate call per session. This constraint shapes the entire review architecture: Pest Control is included as a Nitpicker team member (rather than spawned separately after the team completes) because a second TeamCreate for a standalone PC spawn would fail at runtime.

The constraint is documented only in MEMORY.md:19-21: "Claude Code only supports one TeamCreate per session. The Nitpicker team (4 reviewers + Big Head) uses this slot. QA coordination (Pantry ↔ Pest Control) must use file-based handoff, not teams."

Root cause: The constraint was discovered empirically and captured in MEMORY.md but never propagated to RULES.md, CLAUDE.md, or CONTRIBUTING.md.

Current behavior: An operator extending the workflow (e.g., trying to create a second team for QA coordination) will encounter a runtime failure with no prior warning in any authoritative doc. The architectural rationale for why PC is inside the Nitpicker team is invisible to anyone reading RULES.md.

Fix: Document the constraint in RULES.md near the Nitpicker team setup.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md Step 3b-iv (near team setup): Add note explaining one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint and why PC is a team member

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md documents the one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint
- [ ] Note explains the architectural implication (PC must be team member, not separate spawn)
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md or SETUP.md mentions the constraint for framework extenders

Audit reference: DRIFT-015 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Missing prompts/ directory creation before Pantry writes
description: Pantry writes data files to {session-dir}/prompts/ but nobody creates that directory. RULES.md Step 0 creates {session-dir}/{task-metadata,previews} but prompts/ is not included. A fresh Pantry agent would fail on its first write attempt. Fix: add prompts/ to the mkdir -p command in RULES.md Step 0, or have Pantry create it.

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md example missing pc and summaries subdirs
description: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md:88-91 mkdir example omits pc and summaries subdirs present in authoritative RULES.md:312. Also, audit table at :104 dual-classifies SESSION_DIR tier. Fix: update example to match RULES.md; correct audit table tier classification. Consolidated from: Correctness F3, F4.

title: ESV checkpoint input specification incomplete
description:
## Root Cause
The ESV checkpoint template in checkpoints.md requires three Queen-supplied values (SESSION_START_COMMIT, SESSION_END_COMMIT, SESSION_START_DATE) that the RULES.md Step 5c spawn prompt does not explicitly provide. Additionally, commit range boundary semantics (.. vs ^..) are unspecified and the "no open beads" scenario has no explicit handling.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L320` -- ESV spawn prompt missing explicit fields (from Edge Cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:L765` -- ESV Check 2 commit range boundary ambiguous (from Edge Cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:L781` -- ESV Check 3 empty bead list scenario unhandled (from Edge Cases review)

## Fix
(1) Expand RULES.md ESV spawn prompt to pass all three fields explicitly; (2) Specify ^.. range or document that SESSION_START_COMMIT is the commit before the first session commit; (3) Add explicit branch for "Open Issues: None" scenario.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/RULES.md:L320`: Expand ESV spawn prompt with all three fields
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:L765`: Clarify commit range boundary semantics
- `orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:L781`: Add empty-beads handling branch

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ESV spawn prompt in RULES.md passes SESSION_START_COMMIT, SESSION_END_COMMIT, SESSION_START_DATE explicitly
- [ ] ESV Check 2 documents whether range includes or excludes the start commit
- [ ] ESV Check 3 handles "Open Issues: None" with explicit PASS/FAIL logic

title: Templates lack failure artifact specification for error paths
description: Root cause: Multiple templates specify FAIL conditions but do not specify what artifact to write on failure. Big Head Step 0 (reviews.md:349-352) says 'Do NOT proceed' when reports missing but does not instruct writing a failure artifact. Downstream consumers (Queen, CCB) have no written record of the failure, making state recovery ambiguous. Related to existing beads ant-farm-e9k and ant-farm-zeu. Fix: Establish convention that when any template reaches a FAIL condition, it writes a brief artifact to the expected output path explaining the failure. Acceptance criteria: (1) Big Head Step 0 writes failure artifact when reports missing. (2) Convention documented for all templates. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2).

title: Nested markdown code fences in reviews.md error return template break rendering
description: The error return template in reviews.md Step 0a (lines 390-420) contains a nested markdown code fence. The outer fence opens at line 390 with triple backticks. The inner fence at lines 414-416 (for the re-spawn instruction) uses the same triple backticks, which prematurely closes the outer fence in standard markdown rendering. Lines 417-420 render as regular text instead of inside the code block. An agent parsing this template literally could misinterpret the template boundaries. Affected surfaces: reviews.md:414-420. Fix: Use quadruple backticks for the outer fence, or indent the inner block with 4+ spaces, or use tildes for the inner fence.

title: [Review RC-3] Stale _archive/ files persist after rsync --delete removal
description: P2: Removing --delete from rsync in sync-to-claude.sh without adding --exclude='_archive/' leaves previously-synced deprecated instruction files at ~/.claude/orchestration/_archive/ where agents using glob patterns could read them. Fix: add --exclude='_archive/' to the rsync command.

title: awk sub() in fill_slot treats & and \ as special in replacement values
description:
Root cause: fill-review-slots.sh:176 uses awk `sub(slot, val)` for text replacement. POSIX awk's sub() interprets `&` in the replacement string as 'insert matched text' and `\` as an escape character. If any slot value (file path, commit range) contains these characters, the substitution silently corrupts output.

Affected surfaces:
- fill-review-slots.sh:151-183 (fill_slot function, specifically line 176)

Found by: Edge Cases (P2), Correctness (P3 - 'theoretical')
Edge Cases rated P2 because it is a latent corruption risk in production code. Correctness rated P3 because current inputs are unlikely to contain & or \. Taking the higher severity: P2.

Fix: After reading val from the temp file in the awk script, escape special chars:
```awk
gsub(/\\/, "&&", val)   # escape backslashes first
gsub(/&/, "\\&", val)   # then escape ampersands
```

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Slot values containing & and \ are substituted literally
- [ ] Existing slot values (paths, timestamps, task IDs) continue to work unchanged

title: [Review RC-1] extract_agent_section awk pattern requires 2 delimiters but skeletons have 1
description: P1 BLOCKER: The awk change from found=1 to count>=2 in compose-review-skeletons.sh:73 requires two --- delimiters, but skeleton files only have one --- separator. This silently produces empty agent instruction bodies, breaking the review pipeline. Fix: revert to count>=1 (skip first ---) or add a second --- to skeleton files.

title: reviews.md: minor comment precision issues in polling loop
description: Several comments in the polling loop could be more precise about their intent.

title: MANDATORY GATE style inconsistency in reviews.md
Big Head retry protocol '2 subsequent turns' ambiguous in team context
description:
## Root Cause
`reviews.md:L799-L810` tells Big Head to retry Pest Control "if no reply after 2 subsequent turns." In a team context, "turn" is ambiguous: it could mean messages Big Head sends, messages received from any source, or exchanges specifically with Pest Control.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L799-L810` — Big Head retry protocol (from edge-cases review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] "Turn" is defined precisely (e.g., "2 incoming messages from any teammate that are not the expected Pest Control reply")
- [ ] The retry trigger cannot be accidentally tripped by unrelated team activity

title: Verification Pipeline no defined precedence when Big Head and CCB checks disagree
sed '$d' in write_big_head_brief deletes last report path instead of trailing newline
description:
Root cause: fill-review-slots.sh:238 uses `sed '$d'` to 'remove trailing newline' from the expected_paths list, but `sed '$d'` deletes the LAST LINE, not a trailing newline character. This silently drops the last review type's report path from Big Head's expected paths list.

Impact: In round 1, excellence-review path is dropped (3 of 4 emitted). In round 2+, edge-cases-review path is dropped (1 of 2 emitted). Big Head cannot verify all reports exist before starting consolidation.

Affected surfaces:
- fill-review-slots.sh:234-238 (write_big_head_brief function)

Found by: Correctness (P1), Edge Cases (P2), Clarity (P3 - comment mismatch), Excellence (P3 - fragility)
All 4 reviewers independently identified this as an issue, confirming it is the highest-priority finding.

Fix: Replace `sed '$d'` with a loop that builds the list without a trailing newline, or use `sed '/^$/d'` to strip blank lines.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] All review type paths appear in Big Head expected paths for both round 1 (4 types) and round 2+ (2 types)
- [ ] No trailing blank line in the expected paths output
- [ ] Shell test confirms correct output for 1, 2, and 4 review types

title: [Review RC-2] BSD grep \s not recognized in scrub-pii.sh verification
description: P2: macOS BSD grep does not support \s in ERE mode. The PII verification grep at scrub-pii.sh:49,65 uses \s which silently fails to match on macOS, causing the post-scrub verification to falsely report clean. Fix: replace \s with [[:space:]] or [ \t].

title: SETUP.md Prerequisites section duplicates Quick Setup commands
description: Root cause: orchestration/SETUP.md lines 8-16 (Prerequisites) and lines 19-29 (Quick Setup) show nearly identical install commands. Maintainability concern: changes must be made in two places. Fix: Remove code block from Prerequisites, keep it only in Quick Setup. Found by: excellence-reviewer (P3).

title: Pantry skips writing preview file to previews/ directory
description: When composing implementation prompts (Section 1), the Pantry agent writes the task brief to prompts/task-{suffix}.md but returns without writing the combined preview to previews/preview-{suffix}.md. The agent's return message narrates 'Now Step 3: Write the combined prompt preview...' but exits before executing it. This caused CCO to fail on first run in session 2bb21f22, requiring a second Pantry spawn to produce the preview. Root cause: the preview file is not clearly listed as a mandatory output artifact in the Pantry template. Fix: make previews/ output a hard requirement in pantry.md with explicit verification before returning.

title: Development artifacts left in production templates (NEW annotation, CRITICAL FIX note)
reviews.md section ordering -- Round-Aware Protocol placed after sections that reference it
scrub-pii.sh: perl \s* vs grep [[:space:]]* pattern divergence (cosmetic)
Fragile comment-delimited conditionals and missing placeholder validation in template system
parse-progress-log.sh trap ordering -- trap installed after map_init
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md does not document or validate angle-bracket placeholder syntax
Improve Scout agent type tie-breaking with deeper catalog reads and explicit fallback
description:
Root cause: When the Scout cannot clearly differentiate between agent types for a task, it falls back to returning 'group' as the agent type. This reaches the user (via the Queen's strategy presentation) as an opaque label with no information about which types were tied or why. The user has no actionable way to break the tie.

The underlying issue is that the Scout's agent catalog (Step 2.5) only reads YAML frontmatter — one sentence of description per agent. This is intentionally lightweight but provides insufficient signal for 'description match' (selection criterion 3), making ties more likely than necessary.

Fix (two-pronged):

1. Deeper catalog reads on tie: When the Scout's four-step selection criteria (file extension, task nature, description match, fallback) produce a tie between two or more agent types, the Scout should read the full agent MD files for ONLY the tied candidates. This keeps context cost proportional to the ambiguity while giving the Scout enough signal to differentiate. If reading the full files resolves the tie, use the winner. If the tie persists after deeper reads, proceed to step 2.

2. Explicit fallback format for unresolved ties: When a tie cannot be broken, the Scout's task metadata and strategy presentation must list all tied agent types with the task title for context, not 'group'. Format in strategy output should be:
   {task-id}: {task-title} — PICK ONE: [python-pro | debugger]
This gives the user task context and the specific candidates in one line, making the tie-break decision self-contained without requiring them to look up the task.

Multiple ties across different tasks should each list their own candidates independently.

Affected files:
- orchestration/templates/scout.md — Step 2.5 catalog build (add deep-read-on-tie logic), Step 3 agent type selection (update tie handling), Step 5 strategy presentation (update format for tied types)

Acceptance criteria:
1. Scout reads full agent MD files for tied candidates (and only tied candidates) before falling back
2. Unresolved ties surface in strategy as '{task-id}: {task-title} — PICK ONE: [type-a | type-b]' instead of 'group'
3. Each task with a tie lists its own candidates independently
4. No increase in Scout context usage when there are no ties (frontmatter-only reads remain the default)

title: Literal <N> placeholder in RULES.md dummy reviewer shell block should be ${REVIEW_ROUND}
description:
## Root Cause
`RULES.md:257` uses a literal `<N>` in the `DUMMY_WINDOW="dummy-reviewer-round-<N>"` variable assignment, while the rest of the same shell block uses `${REVIEW_ROUND}`. This mix of conventions requires manual substitution.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:257` — DUMMY_WINDOW assignment (from clarity review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `DUMMY_WINDOW` uses `${REVIEW_ROUND}` instead of literal `<N>`
- [ ] The corresponding "Replace `<N>`" note is removed if made redundant

title: fix: installed pre-push hook is fatal on sync failure, contradicting install-hooks.sh non-fatal design
description:
The installed git hook at .git/hooks/pre-push runs sync-to-claude.sh under `set -euo pipefail` with no error handling (line 11: `"$SYNC_SCRIPT"`). Any sync failure exits non-zero and blocks the push.

The hook template in scripts/install-hooks.sh (lines 50-57) wraps the sync call in a non-fatal check: `if ! "$SYNC_SCRIPT"; then echo WARNING ... fi` — sync failure prints a warning but exits 0, allowing the push to proceed. The comment at line 50 explains the design intent: "Sync failure is intentionally non-fatal: the push should never be blocked by a local sync tool."

Root cause: The installed hook is an older version that was not regenerated after install-hooks.sh was updated to make sync failure non-fatal. The two files have diverged: the installed hook enforces fatal sync, while the installer generates non-fatal sync.

Current behavior: A developer pushing code will experience a blocked push if sync-to-claude.sh fails for any reason (disk full, permission error, rsync failure). This contradicts the documented design intent. Running `./scripts/install-hooks.sh` would fix the installed hook, but until re-run, every push is at risk of being blocked by a transient sync error.

Fix:
1. Re-run `./scripts/install-hooks.sh` to replace the installed hook with the current non-fatal version
2. Add a note to CONTRIBUTING.md reminding contributors to re-run install-hooks.sh after pulling changes to that script
3. Consider adding a version marker to generated hooks so staleness can be detected

Also fix CONTRIBUTING.md:161 which claims rsync uses `--delete` — the actual script intentionally omits `--delete` (with an explanatory comment at lines 24-25). Update to reflect the non-delete policy and the _archive/ exclusion.

Changes needed:
- .git/hooks/pre-push: Regenerate via install-hooks.sh
- CONTRIBUTING.md:161: Fix rsync --delete claim and add _archive/ exclusion note
- CONTRIBUTING.md: Add reminder about re-running install-hooks.sh after changes

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Installed .git/hooks/pre-push matches output of install-hooks.sh (non-fatal sync)
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md rsync description matches actual sync-to-claude.sh behavior (no --delete, excludes _archive/)
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md includes guidance on re-running install-hooks.sh after pulling changes
- [ ] Push succeeds even when sync-to-claude.sh fails (manual test)

Audit reference: DRIFT-003 + DRIFT-011 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: AGG-022: Standardize variable naming across templates
description:
The same concepts use different variable names across files: {task-id-suffix} vs {TASK_SUFFIX}, standalone vs _standalone, and task ID vs bead ID. This creates confusion and drift.

Fix: Standardize on canonical variable names ({TASK_ID} for full ID, {TASK_SUFFIX} for suffix, {SESSION_DIR} for session directory). Create a shared glossary and reference from all templates. Remove local redefinitions.

Note: {EPIC_ID} was removed from all active templates in commits dabb764/f838cef (session-scoping shift). It is no longer a variable to standardize.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] All templates use the same variable name for each concept (no synonyms like task-id-suffix vs TASK_SUFFIX)
- [ ] A glossary section defines each canonical variable name with its meaning
- [ ] grep for deprecated variable names across orchestration/ returns zero matches

title: Checkpoints CCO FAIL verdict format has no example
description: checkpoints.md shows PASS verdict format but no example of a FAIL verdict with specific check failures listed. A fresh Pest Control agent might format FAIL output incorrectly. Add a FAIL example showing check number, name, and evidence.

title: Round 2+ scope instructions inconsistently presented between nitpicker-skeleton and reviews.md
pantry-review.md (archived) tense inconsistency
compose-review-skeletons.sh no SESSION_DIR existence check before mkdir -p
scrub-pii.sh does not re-stage issues.jsonl when run standalone outside pre-commit
description: Root cause: When scrub-pii.sh runs standalone (not as pre-commit hook), it modifies issues.jsonl in place but does not re-stage it. The git add is in install-hooks.sh's generated hook, not in scrub-pii.sh itself. Undocumented manual step. Fix: Add a reminder note in the script or detect non-hook context and print 'Run git add .beads/issues.jsonl'. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: checkpoints.md sampling formula contradicts 'minimum 3' prose text
description:
Root cause: checkpoints.md line 311 specifies the formula min(5, ceil(N/3)) for selecting findings to spot-check, but the same line states 'minimum 3, or all findings if fewer than 3'. The formula can produce values below 3 -- e.g., for N=6, ceil(6/3)=2, then min(5,2)=2. The English text says minimum 3 but the formula does not enforce this floor. Pest Control could either under-sample (following formula) or ignore the formula (following prose), creating verification ambiguity.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:311 -- formula/prose contradiction in Checkpoint B Nitpickers sampling rule

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 8, P3 with cross-ref to edge-cases), Edge Cases (Finding 10, P2)
Edge-cases reviewer confirmed this as a genuine behavioral risk at P2. Taking highest: P2.

Fix: Replace min(5, ceil(N/3)) with max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3))) to enforce the stated minimum of 3. Rewrite the prose in plain English: 'Pick at least 3 findings (or all findings if fewer than 3 exist), up to a maximum of 5.'

Acceptance criteria:
- The formula on line 311 enforces a floor of 3
- The English description matches the formula exactly
- No contradiction between formula and prose

title: parse-progress-log.sh misleading POSIX portability comment vs bash-only constructs
description: Root cause: scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:104-150 uses a temp-directory key-value store headed with a 'POSIX-compatible' comment, but the script freely uses bash-only constructs elsewhere (e.g. [[ =~ ]] at line 169, set -euo pipefail at line 25). The actual portability target is bash 3+ (not POSIX sh). The misleading comment could confuse future maintainers about the portability baseline. Fix: Reword the comment heading to 'Bash 3+-compatible key-value store (replaces bash 4+ declare -A). Uses a temp directory to avoid associative arrays.' Affected surfaces: scripts/parse-progress-log.sh:104-150, :169. Source: Excellence Review Finding 2 (P2), Clarity Review Finding 9 (P3).

title: Wave concept used in RULES.md but never defined
description: RULES.md Hard Gates table references 'Next agent in wave' and checkpoints.md says 'BEFORE spawning next agent in same wave'. The concept of a wave (batch of parallel agents) is never defined. Add to glossary: 'Wave: a batch of agents spawned in parallel. Wave N completes before Wave N+1 begins.'

title: Verification Pipeline Rationale has redundant 'Why both?' paragraph
AGG-031: Add data file format specification to skeleton templates
description:
Skeleton templates say Read your task context from {DATA_FILE_PATH} but never specify the file is markdown, dont list expected sections, and provide no parsing guidance. A cold agent might expect JSON or YAML.

Fix: Add a brief format note to each skeleton (e.g., The data file is markdown with sections: Context, Scope Boundaries, Focus.).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] dirt-pusher-skeleton.md specifies the data file format and expected sections
- [ ] nitpicker-skeleton.md specifies the data file format and expected sections
- [ ] Both skeletons explicitly state the file is markdown (not JSON/YAML)

title: fix: Scout agent frontmatter declares model: sonnet, contradicting RULES.md model: opus
description:
The Scout agent definition at agents/scout-organizer.md:5 declares `model: sonnet` in its frontmatter. The Model Assignments table in orchestration/RULES.md:294 specifies `model: opus` for the Scout, stating "Every Task tool call the Queen makes MUST include the model parameter from this table."

Root cause: When the Scout was promoted from sonnet to opus (for orchestration complexity), the RULES.md Model Assignments table was updated but the agent frontmatter was not. The same stale model value propagated to GLOSSARY.md:80 and README.md:75, which both still say "sonnet."

Current behavior: If the Queen supplies `model: "opus"` as instructed by RULES.md, the frontmatter is silently overridden — no runtime failure but a false mental model. If the Queen omits the model parameter (e.g., following a partial read of instructions or during error recovery), Claude Code loads the frontmatter value and the Scout runs as sonnet instead of the intended opus. The frontmatter is the fallback, so this is a latent S1.

Fix: Update the model field in all three locations to match RULES.md:
1. agents/scout-organizer.md:5 — change `model: sonnet` to `model: opus`
2. orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:80 — change Scout row model from `sonnet` to `opus`
3. README.md:75 — change "a sonnet subagent" to "an opus subagent"

Also fix the Pantry model in GLOSSARY.md:81 (says sonnet, RULES.md:295 says opus).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] agents/scout-organizer.md frontmatter says `model: opus`
- [ ] orchestration/GLOSSARY.md Scout row says `opus`
- [ ] orchestration/GLOSSARY.md Pantry row says `opus`
- [ ] README.md Scout description says "opus" not "sonnet"
- [ ] No other files reference Scout or Pantry as sonnet-tier agents (grep verification)

Audit reference: DRIFT-001 + DRIFT-017 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: fix: README shows 5-member Nitpicker team but RULES.md requires 6 (Pest Control inside team)
description:
README.md:59 describes the Nitpicker team as "4 reviewers + Big Head" (5 members). README.md:218 shows the flow: "create Nitpicker team (4 reviewers + Big Head)" with the Queen spawning a separate Pest Control after the team completes (line 201).

RULES.md:179 specifies a 6-member team: "Round 1: 6 members — 4 reviewers + Big Head + Pest Control." RULES.md:182 explains why: "Pest Control MUST be a team member so Big Head can SendMessage to it." RULES.md:184 confirms: "After team completes, DMVDC and CCB have already run inside the team."

Root cause: README was written before Pest Control was added as a team member. The architectural change (driven by the one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint — see DRIFT-015) was applied to RULES.md but not propagated to the README.

Current behavior: An operator following the README Step 3b flow would spawn a 5-member team and then separately spawn Pest Control. Big Head would be unable to SendMessage to PC (different team/no team), breaking the team-internal DMVDC/CCB flow.

Fix: Update README architecture diagram, team description, and flow diagram to reflect 6-member team with PC inside.

Changes needed:
- README.md:59: Change to "4 reviewers + Big Head + Pest Control"
- README.md:218: Update flow diagram to show 6-member team
- README.md:201: Remove separate PC spawn; note DMVDC/CCB run inside team

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] README describes 6-member Nitpicker team (4 reviewers + Big Head + Pest Control)
- [ ] Flow diagram shows PC as team member, not separate spawn
- [ ] No reference to spawning PC separately after team completes

Audit reference: DRIFT-016 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: fix: SSV checkpoint missing from RULES.md Model Assignments table
description:
The Model Assignments table in orchestration/RULES.md:288-304 lists model assignments for all Pest Control checkpoint types (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) but has no row for SSV (Scout Strategy Verification). The table header at RULES.md:290 states: "Every Task tool call the Queen makes MUST include the model parameter from this table. Omitting model causes the agent to inherit the Queen's opus model, wasting tokens on agents that don't need it."

Root cause: When SSV was added as a checkpoint, it was documented inline in Step 1b (RULES.md:89 — `spawn Pest Control (pest-control, model: "haiku") for Scout Strategy Verification`) and in checkpoints.md:612 (`Model: haiku`), but the Model Assignments table was not updated.

Current behavior: A Queen following the table as the authoritative model reference will find no SSV entry and must hunt in Step 1b prose. Three independent audit agents flagged this same gap.

Fix: Add a row to the Model Assignments table between the current first and second rows:
`| PC — SSV | Task (pest-control) | haiku | Pure set comparisons — no judgment required |`

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md:297 (insert new row in Model Assignments table)

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Model Assignments table includes PC — SSV row with model haiku
- [ ] All 5 PC checkpoint types (SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) have table entries
- [ ] Table row note matches checkpoints.md:612 rationale

Audit reference: DRIFT-005 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Pantry fail-fast says Halt but behavior is skip-to-next-task
description: Line 30 of pantry.md says 'FAIL-FAST CHECK: Halt and report for any of these conditions.' The word 'Halt' implies stopping all processing. But the actual behavior in Conditions 1-3 is per-task skipping: 'Do not proceed with task brief composition for this task' and 'skip to the next task.' The Pantry continues processing remaining tasks after a failure. The 'Halt' wording became more visibly misleading after ant-farm-zeu added detailed per-condition behavior (write artifact, skip to next). Affected surfaces: pantry.md:30-31. Fix: Change 'Halt and report' to 'Check and skip on failure' or 'Validate before proceeding.'

title: Explore tmux-based agent spawning for real-time observability
description: Explore running orchestrated subagents in tmux windows instead of background Task agents, so you can observe agent work in real-time. Key research questions: (1) Can the boss-bot launch claude CLI sessions in tmux windows via Bash? (2) How to pipe prompts into those sessions? (3) How to collect results back? (4) Hook-based automation options? Goal: Each spawned agent gets its own tmux window you can switch to with ctrl+b n/p. May influence orchestration templates if feasible. (Moved from Documentation Gaps epic ant-farm-9wq)

title: RULES.md round 2+ composition phrasing inconsistency with reviews.md
description: RULES.md:171-177 says (Correctness + Edge Cases) for round 2+ but reviews.md explicitly notes Clarity and Excellence are dropped. Minor wording inconsistency. Fix: align RULES.md phrasing. Consolidated from: Excellence F7.

title: Stale pantry-review agent on disk unguarded by Scout exclusion list
description: Root cause: Commit ant-farm-oc9v removed pantry-review from the Scout exclusion list at orchestration/templates/scout.md:63, but sync-to-claude.sh never deletes files from ~/.claude/agents/. The stale ~/.claude/agents/pantry-review.md remains on disk. Without the exclusion list entry, the Scout could recommend pantry-review as a Dirt Pusher for tasks matching its description, producing a silent failure (orchestration agent spawned as implementation agent, yielding no code and no commit). Fix: Re-add pantry-review to the exclusion list at scout.md:63 until users have confirmed manual deletion of ~/.claude/agents/pantry-review.md. Affected surface: orchestration/templates/scout.md:63. Source: Edge Cases Review Finding 1.

title: Template-to-agent communication gaps: unactionable escalation path and unfilled placeholder
Retry Limits counter interaction note not extended to SSV FAIL row
description:
## Root Cause
The Retry Limits table counter interaction note at `orchestration/RULES.md:534` explicitly calls out only CCB re-runs as counting toward the session total of 5. The new "SSV FAIL -> re-Scout cycle" row at line 530 is not mentioned. It is ambiguous whether SSV FAIL retries consume session-wide retry slots. This is a pre-existing omission pattern (other rows like Pantry CCO, Scout, Scribe ESV are also unmentioned), not a regression — but the fix extended the table without addressing the coverage gap.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/RULES.md:534` — counter interaction note (from Edge Cases R2-EC-02)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:530` — new SSV FAIL retry row (context)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The counter interaction note covers all rows in the Retry Limits table, OR a blanket statement clarifies the default (e.g., "All rows consume one session-total slot unless otherwise noted")

title: SETUP.md duplicate/overlapping content between Quick Setup and Recipe Card
Incomplete deprecation cleanup: pantry-review agent file, Section 2, and RULES.md table rows still structurally present
Scribe skeleton missing empty-data fallback instructions
description:
## Root Cause
The Scribe skeleton template was written for the happy path and does not instruct the Scribe on what to do when input data is absent: (1) CHANGELOG.md does not exist; (2) no agent summaries exist in summaries/*.md.

## Affected Surfaces
- `orchestration/templates/scribe-skeleton.md:L148` -- CHANGELOG prepend assumes file exists (from Edge Cases review)
- `orchestration/templates/scribe-skeleton.md:L42` -- summaries/*.md glob may be empty (from Edge Cases review)

## Fix
Add explicit fallback instructions for both cases.

## Changes Needed
- `orchestration/templates/scribe-skeleton.md`: Add two fallback instructions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scribe skeleton handles nonexistent CHANGELOG with create-then-prepend instruction
- [ ] Scribe skeleton handles empty summaries glob with explicit "None this session." instruction

title: AGG-034: Fix circular distinct approaches definition in implementation.md
description:
implementation.md defines distinct approaches as requiring different tradeoff profiles, but genuinely different algorithms can share the same tradeoff profile. The definition is circular and confuses Dirt Pushers during design exploration.

Fix: Simplify to concrete criteria: Distinct means different algorithms, OR different data structures, OR different architectural patterns. Cosmetic variations do not count.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] implementation.md defines distinct approaches using concrete criteria, not tradeoff profiles
- [ ] The definition lists specific categories (algorithms, data structures, architectural patterns)
- [ ] A brief example distinguishes distinct from cosmetic variation

title: TIMESTAMP variable undefined in Step 3b-v dummy reviewer
description: Root cause: RULES.md Step 3b-v (line 176) uses ${TIMESTAMP} in a tmux send-keys command, but no prior instruction assigns this value to a variable named TIMESTAMP. Step 3b-i describes generating a timestamp but does not define the variable name. If unset, the dummy reviewer receives a malformed report path (dummy-review-.md with empty timestamp). Affected files: orchestration/RULES.md:176. Fix: Add explicit variable assignment in Step 3b-i: TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S) and add cross-reference in Step 3b-v. Acceptance criteria: (1) Step 3b-i explicitly assigns timestamp to a named variable. (2) Step 3b-v TIMESTAMP reference resolves correctly. Found by: Clarity (CL-2 P2), Edge Cases (EC-4b P2), Excellence (EX-6 P2).

title: pantry.md Section 2 circular reference fix incomplete: Big Head Step 0/0a and polling loop not inlined
Upgrade CCB to sonnet and add root cause spot-check
description:
Update the Colony Census Bureau (CCB) checkpoint in orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md to use sonnet instead of haiku, and add a new Check 3b for root cause spot-checking.

Context: CCB is now the quality gate that determines whether Big Head's beads are sufficient to serve as fix briefs (replacing the Pantry in the fix loop). Checks 3 (bead quality) and 6 (dedup correctness) require judgment that haiku cannot reliably provide -- assessing whether root cause explanations are substantive and whether merged findings genuinely share a code path.

Changes needed in orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:
1. Change CCB model assignment from haiku to sonnet in the checkpoint prompt header and any model references
2. Add new Check 3b (Root Cause Spot-Check) after existing Check 3: select up to 2 beads for deep validation (prioritize P1, then highest-surface-count P2s), read source files at referenced file:line locations, verify root cause matches actual code, assess fix direction
3. Define SUSPECT severity distinction: minor (vague/ambiguous, flag for amendment, continue) vs material (wrong root cause, triggers full bead review escalation)
4. Add Material Spot-Check Escalation Path: CCB verdict PARTIAL with context-degradation-suspected flag, Queen shuts down old Big Head, spawns fresh Big Head with handoff for full bead review, re-runs CCB, escalates to user if still SUSPECT

Also update in orchestration/RULES.md:
5. Model Assignments table: change PC -- CCB row from haiku to sonnet

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CCB prompt in checkpoints.md specifies model as sonnet (grep for 'Model.*sonnet' in CCB section)
- [ ] Check 3b exists between Check 3 and Check 4 with spot-check instructions for up to 2 beads
- [ ] Check 3b includes SUSPECT severity distinction (minor vs material) with different actions for each
- [ ] Material escalation path documented: PARTIAL verdict -> context-degradation-suspected flag -> fresh Big Head spawn -> full review -> re-run CCB -> user escalation
- [ ] RULES.md Model Assignments table shows CCB as sonnet, not haiku
- [ ] Existing CCB checks 0-7 are unchanged (no regression)

title: Add bead-list handoff to Big Head skeleton
description:
Update orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md to add a structured bead-list handoff message that Big Head sends to the Queen after filing beads.

Context: In the persistent review team design, Big Head files beads from review findings and then hands off a structured list to the Queen. The Queen uses this list to spawn a Scout for fix implementation strategy. Currently Big Head files beads but does not send a structured handoff -- the Queen has to infer what was filed from the consolidated report. The handoff message makes the interface explicit and machine-readable.

Changes needed in orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:
1. After the bead filing step (Step 4 in the skeleton), add a new step: "Send bead list to Queen"
2. The handoff message format should include: list of bead IDs with their priorities and root cause titles, total P1/P2/P3 counts, the consolidated report path
3. The message should be sent via SendMessage to the team lead (Queen)
4. Include the message template in the skeleton so Big Head produces consistent, parseable output

Handoff message format:
```
Fix handoff: <N> root causes filed.
P1: <count>, P2: <count>, P3: <count>

Beads requiring fixes:
- <bead-id-1> (P1): <root cause title>
- <bead-id-2> (P2): <root cause title>
...

Consolidated report: <path>
```

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] big-head-skeleton.md contains a handoff step after bead filing that sends a structured message to the Queen
- [ ] Handoff message format includes bead IDs, priorities, root cause titles, and consolidated report path
- [ ] Message is sent via SendMessage (not written to file) so the Queen receives it as a team notification
- [ ] P3 beads are included in the count but clearly separated from P1/P2 (Queen only acts on P1/P2 for fixes)
- [ ] Handoff step is clearly labeled and sequenced after CCB PASS confirmation

title: Rewrite fix workflow for in-team agents
description:
Rewrite the fix workflow section in orchestration/templates/reviews.md to reflect the persistent team design where fix Dirt Pushers and fix Pest Control agents spawn into the Nitpicker team instead of running as standalone Task agents.

Context: Currently, reviews.md describes a fix workflow where the Queen spawns fix Dirt Pushers as standalone Task agents outside the review team. In the persistent team design, fix agents join the team so they can message reviewers for clarification and iterate directly with fix PCs on DMVDC failures. The Pantry and CCO are skipped for fix briefs because Big Head's beads (validated by CCB) serve as the fix brief, and the Scout's fix strategy is validated by SSV.

Changes needed in orchestration/templates/reviews.md:

1. Update "Fix Workflow" section:
   - Fix DPs spawn into the persistent team (not standalone Task agents)
   - Fix PCs (fix-pc-wwd haiku, fix-pc-dmvdc sonnet) spawn into team alongside fix DPs
   - Fix DP prompt structure: lean prompt pointing to bead ID as source of truth
   - Naming convention: fix-dp-1..N, fix-pc-wwd, fix-pc-dmvdc (round 2+: fix-dp-r2-1, fix-pc-wwd-r2, etc.)

2. Document the fix inner loop protocol:
   - DP commits -> messages fix-pc-wwd (WWD check)
   - WWD PASS -> DP messages fix-pc-dmvdc (DMVDC check)
   - DMVDC PASS -> DP goes idle
   - DMVDC FAIL -> PC messages DP with specifics -> DP iterates -> max 2 retries -> escalate to Queen

3. Document Pantry/CCO skip for fix briefs:
   - Pantry skipped: beads ARE the briefs (root cause, surfaces, fix, acceptance criteria from PC-validated findings)
   - CCO skipped: bead content passed CCB, Scout strategy passed SSV

4. Update round transition to use SendMessage:
   - Queen messages Correctness + Edge Cases reviewers with round 2 scope (fix commit range, changed files, task IDs, brief path, report output path)
   - Queen messages Big Head with round 2 consolidation instructions (expected report count, paths, output path)
   - Clarity and Drift reviewers left idle after round 1

5. Document fix-cycle Scout auto-approval:
   - Scout strategy is auto-approved for the user (no user confirmation gate)
   - SSV gate still runs as mechanical safety net
   - SSV FAIL -> re-run Scout with violations, max 1 retry, then escalate

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Fix workflow section describes fix DPs and fix PCs spawning into the persistent team via Task with team_name parameter
- [ ] Fix inner loop protocol documented: DP -> fix-pc-wwd -> fix-pc-dmvdc -> iterate on fail (with max 2 retries)
- [ ] Pantry/CCO skip rationale documented with clear explanation of why beads + CCB + SSV are sufficient
- [ ] Round transition protocol uses SendMessage to re-task Correctness and Edge Cases reviewers
- [ ] Fix-cycle Scout documented as auto-approved with SSV gate
- [ ] Fix DP prompt structure shown (lean prompt, bead as source of truth, message fix-pc-wwd after commit)
- [ ] Naming convention for fix team members documented (fix-dp-N, fix-pc-wwd, fix-pc-dmvdc, round suffixes)

title: Update RULES.md for persistent team and fix inner loop
description:
Rewrite RULES.md Steps 3b and 3c to implement the persistent review team that stays alive across the entire review-fix-review loop, and add the fix inner loop protocol where fix agents spawn into the team.

Context: Currently RULES.md Step 3b creates a Nitpicker team that is implicitly torn down after round 1, and Step 3c's fix workflow spawns standalone Task agents. The persistent team design keeps the team alive so fix DPs can message reviewers, fix PCs run DMVDC within the team, and round 2+ reviews reuse the same team members via SendMessage re-tasking. The one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint means this is the only viable path for team-based round 2+ reviews.

Changes needed in orchestration/RULES.md:

1. Step 3b: Document that the team persists across the full review-fix-review loop
   - Team is NOT torn down after round 1 consolidation
   - Same team is reused for fix agents and round 2+ reviews
   - Team shutdown only happens at convergence (0 P1/P2) or round 4 cap

2. Step 3c: Rewrite fix decision flow to include fix inner loop
   - After Big Head handoff (bead list via SendMessage), Queen spawns Scout (outside team) for fix strategy
   - Queen spawns SSV PC (haiku, outside team) on fix briefing -- must PASS, auto-approved for user
   - Queen spawns fix agents INTO team: N fix DPs (sonnet) + fix-pc-wwd (haiku) + fix-pc-dmvdc (sonnet)
   - Fix inner loop: DP commits -> messages fix-pc-wwd -> WWD PASS -> messages fix-pc-dmvdc -> DMVDC PASS/FAIL with retry
   - After all fix DPs verified, Queen transitions to round 2 via SendMessage to Correctness + Edge Cases reviewers

3. Update team member roster documentation
   - Initial: 6 members (4 Nitpickers + Big Head + Pest Control)
   - After fix: + N fix DPs + fix-pc-wwd + fix-pc-dmvdc
   - Peak: 15 members (6 + 7 DPs + 2 PCs), but only N+2 active during fix phase
   - Fresh fix PCs spawned each round

4. Update Model Assignments table
   - CCB: haiku -> sonnet
   - Add fix-pc-wwd: haiku
   - Add fix-pc-dmvdc: sonnet
   - Add fix DPs: sonnet (same as regular DPs)

5. Update error handling table
   - Fix DP stuck/crashes: stuck-agent diagnostic, file bead, continue
   - Fix PC crashes: spawn replacement into team
   - Reviewer fails round 2: spawn fresh reviewer into team
   - Big Head crashes: spawn fresh with handoff
   - CCB material spot-check fail: shut down old Big Head, spawn fresh, full review

6. Add team naming conventions
   - fix-dp-1..N, fix-pc-wwd, fix-pc-dmvdc
   - Round 2+ suffixes: fix-dp-r2-1, fix-pc-wwd-r2, fix-pc-dmvdc-r2

7. Update progress log entries for new milestones
   - Fix Scout complete, SSV pass, fix agents spawned, fix DMVDC results, round transition

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Step 3b explicitly states team persists across the full loop (no teardown after round 1)
- [ ] Step 3c documents: Big Head handoff -> Scout (outside team) -> SSV -> fix agents spawn into team -> inner loop -> round transition
- [ ] Fix-cycle Scout is documented as auto-approved for the user with SSV gate as mechanical safety net
- [ ] Model Assignments table updated: CCB sonnet, fix-pc-wwd haiku, fix-pc-dmvdc sonnet, fix DPs sonnet
- [ ] Error handling covers fix DP stuck/crash, fix PC crash, reviewer failure, Big Head crash, CCB material fail
- [ ] Team naming conventions documented (fix-dp-N, fix-pc-wwd, fix-pc-dmvdc with round suffixes)
- [ ] Progress log format includes new milestones for fix cycle steps

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md validation regex Pattern 4 has false negatives for mixed casing
scrub-pii.sh regex is overly broad (global replace, not field-scoped)
description: The PII scrub regex is applied globally across all content in issues.jsonl rather than being scoped to the known PII-bearing JSON fields (owner, created_by). Any email-like pattern in any field (titles, descriptions, URLs) would be silently scrubbed. Fix: scope the perl regex to target only owner and created_by field values. Consolidated from: Excellence F2.

title: compose-review-skeletons.sh sed regex converts all {UPPERCASE} tokens, not just slot markers
description:
Root cause: compose-review-skeletons.sh:99-102 uses `sed 's/{\([A-Z][A-Z_]*\)}/{{\1}}/g'` to convert single-brace placeholders to double-brace. This blanket regex would also convert any legitimate {UPPERCASE} text in template prose or code examples that is NOT a slot marker.

Additionally, the comment says 'WORD is all-caps with underscores' but the regex requires at least 2 characters ([A-Z][A-Z_]*), so a single-char placeholder like {X} would not match. The comment does not document this.

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/compose-review-skeletons.sh:99-102 (sed pattern and its comment)

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F8, comment accuracy), Edge Cases (P3 - F6, overly broad regex), Excellence (P3 - F3, maintainability)
3 reviewers found overlapping issues with the same sed line. Root cause is the same: a blanket regex with undocumented assumptions.

Fix: Either (a) use a whitelist of known slot names, or (b) add a comment documenting the assumption + fix the min-length documentation.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comment accurately describes the regex behavior (2+ char minimum)
- [ ] Either whitelist approach or documented assumption added

title: Big Head data file format split between pantry.md and reviews.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain (4 report paths, dedup protocol, bead filing instructions, consolidated output path) but the actual format/structure is in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol section. A fresh Pantry would need to cross-reference but there's no explicit pointer. Add cross-reference from pantry.md Step 4 to reviews.md.

title: Deprecated pantry.md Section 2 retains stale executable code and drifted protocol
description:
## Root Cause

`orchestration/templates/pantry.md` Section 2 (lines ~227-423) is marked DEPRECATED but retains ~200 lines of detailed steps, executable bash blocks with unfilled placeholders (`{{REVIEW_ROUND}}`), and protocol descriptions that have drifted from the current authoritative sources in `reviews.md` and `build-review-prompts.sh`. The deprecation notice is not forceful enough to prevent accidental use, and the section's content is doubly stale: first superseded by `build-review-prompts.sh`, then the wait protocol inside it was replaced by the turn-based approach (ant-farm-q59z).

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L227-234` -- DEPRECATED block not visually distinct (clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L236-423` -- sleep-based CCB wait protocol still documented (drift review)
- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L326-335` -- Step 4 bead filing stale wording (drift review)
- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md:L501` -- unfilled `{{REVIEW_ROUND}}` placeholder in deprecated bash block (edge-cases review)

## Fix

Replace the body of pantry.md Section 2 with a short deprecation notice and forward-reference. Remove or comment all executable bash blocks. Example replacement:

```
## Section 2: Review Mode [DEPRECATED]

> **STOP: DO NOT FOLLOW THESE STEPS.** This section is deprecated as of build-review-prompts.sh introduction.
> For current review workflow, see:
> - `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh` (prompt generation)
> - `orchestration/templates/reviews.md` (review protocol and Big Head consolidation)
```

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/templates/pantry.md`: Replace Section 2 body (lines ~227-423) with a short deprecation notice and forward-references. Remove all executable bash blocks.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] pantry.md Section 2 body contains no executable bash code blocks
- [ ] pantry.md Section 2 has a bold STOP banner at the top
- [ ] pantry.md Section 2 includes forward-references to `build-review-prompts.sh` and `reviews.md`
- [ ] No `{{REVIEW_ROUND}}` or other unfilled placeholders remain in Section 2

title: SETUP.md nested code fences break standard markdown rendering
AGG-042: Add archive exclusion warnings to prevent contamination
description:
orchestration/_archive/ contains older documents that match active template glob patterns. An agent using Glob with orchestration/**/*.md would read stale instructions contradicting current workflows. No DO NOT READ _archive warnings exist.

Fix: Add DO NOT READ files in orchestration/_archive/ to active templates Information Diet section. Consider renaming to _deprecated/.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Information Diet section explicitly excludes orchestration/_archive/ from reads
- [ ] At least one other actively-searched template includes the _archive exclusion warning
- [ ] Agents following Information Diet rules will not read archived files via glob patterns

title: compose-review-skeletons.sh exit 1 in || block redundant with set -euo pipefail
Checkpoint thresholds undefined: small file, sampling N<3, CCB bd list unbounded
description: Root cause: checkpoints.md uses three undefined or broken thresholds: (1) CCO Check 7 uses 'small file' for WARN verdict but never defines what line count qualifies (line 82-84). Pre-existing bead ant-farm-jv4 tracks this. (2) DMVDC sampling formula max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3))) returns 3 when N=1 or N=2, but only 1-2 findings exist. Formula needs outer min(N, ...) guard (line 301). (3) CCB Check 7 runs bd list --status=open with no limit/scope, which can return thousands of items in mature repos. Fix: (1) Define small file = <100 lines. (2) Fix formula to min(N, max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3)))). (3) Scope CCB bead list to session-start date. Acceptance criteria: All three thresholds explicitly defined with concrete values. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2 x2, P3 x1).

title: Pre-push hook blocks all git pushes when sync-to-claude.sh fails
description: Root cause: install-hooks.sh:34-45 generates a pre-push hook with set -euo pipefail that runs sync-to-claude.sh. If sync fails (rsync error, permission denied, disk full), set -e causes the hook to exit non-zero, blocking the git push. Developers cannot push code because of an unrelated sync failure. Fix: Wrap sync call in error handler that degrades to warning: 'if \! "$SYNC_SCRIPT"; then echo WARNING; fi'. Never block pushes for non-git reasons. Acceptance criteria: (1) Sync failure produces warning but push proceeds. (2) Sync success still works normally. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2).

title: AGG-037: Clarify Big Head verification pipeline boundaries and gaps
description:
Both Big Head (Step 0) and Pest Control CCB (Check 0) verify all 4 Nitpicker reports exist. The redundancy is intentional but undocumented. Big Head also lacks verification that it actually read all reports.

Fix: Clarify: Big Head Step 0 is prerequisite (FAIL before reading), CCB Check 0 is audit (FAIL if skipped). Add read verification: After reading, confirm counts from each report.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md documents Big Head Step 0 as prerequisite and CCB Check 0 as audit with distinct descriptions
- [ ] Big Head output includes read confirmation with finding counts per report
- [ ] The two-layer verification design rationale is documented

title: Review task ID scoping includes out-of-range commits
description: Task ant-farm-1y4 was included in this review cycle but its fix commit predates the review range (f9ad7d9..HEAD). The reviewer cannot verify AC compliance. Fix: exclude tasks whose fix commits pre-date the session first commit when compiling review task IDs. Consolidated from: Correctness F2.

title: TASK_SUFFIX extraction algorithm uses first-hyphen logic but examples require last-hyphen
description: Root cause: The TASK_SUFFIX extraction algorithm in orchestration/reference/dependency-analysis.md:23-31 says 'Locate the first hyphen' and provides regex ^[^-]+-(.+)$ which both implement first-hyphen splitting. But the examples show last-hyphen splitting: ant-farm-9oa -> 9oa (not farm-9oa). The regex produces wrong suffixes for any project ID containing a hyphen in the project name (e.g., ant-farm-*). Wrong suffixes cascade to wrong artifact paths (pc-farm-9oa-cco-...md instead of pc-9oa-cco-...md), breaking DMVDC and CCB audits. Affected surfaces: dependency-analysis.md:23 (prose), dependency-analysis.md:25 (regex), dependency-analysis.md:28-29 (step-by-step algorithm). Fix: Change algorithm to use last hyphen, fix regex to ^.+-(.+)$. Acceptance criteria: (1) Algorithm prose says 'last hyphen'. (2) Regex is ^.+-(.+)$. (3) All examples still pass. (4) ant-farm-9oa -> 9oa verified. Found by: correctness-reviewer (P1), edge-cases-reviewer (P2), clarity-reviewer (P3).

title: rsync --delete in sync-to-claude.sh removes scripts/ dir on every sync
description:
Root cause: sync-to-claude.sh:23 uses `rsync -av --delete` to sync orchestration/ to ~/.claude/orchestration/. The scripts directory (~/.claude/orchestration/scripts/) is created by a later block (lines 27-33) from repo scripts/, NOT from orchestration/. The --delete flag removes anything in the target not in the source, so it deletes scripts/ on every run before the copy block recreates it. Currently works due to execution order, but fragile.

Affected surfaces:
- sync-to-claude.sh:23 (rsync --delete)
- sync-to-claude.sh:27-33 (script copy block depends on running AFTER rsync)

Found by: Excellence (P2), Edge Cases (P3)

Fix: Add `--exclude='scripts/'` to the rsync command, OR move compose-review-skeletons.sh and fill-review-slots.sh into orchestration/scripts/ in the repo.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] rsync does not delete ~/.claude/orchestration/scripts/ on subsequent runs
- [ ] Script sync works regardless of execution order within sync-to-claude.sh

title: Templates lack explicit guards for missing or empty input artifacts
description:
Root cause: Multiple templates assume their input artifacts exist and are well-formed without specifying explicit error behavior when they are missing or empty. This is a systematic gap across the template suite -- each template handles the happy path but does not specify what to write or return when a required input is absent.

Affected surfaces:
- pantry.md:26 -- No validation that task-metadata files exist before reading (Step 2)
- pantry.md:26-32 -- No handling for empty or malformed metadata fields (e.g., feature tasks with no 'Root cause')
- pantry.md:94 -- Review mode has no handling for empty changed-file list
- big-head-skeleton.md:23 -- Step 1 says 'FAIL immediately if any missing' but does not specify where to write a failure artifact
- checkpoints.md:259 -- No specification for handling bd show failures in Checkpoint B

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Findings 2, 3, 7, 8, 9 -- all P3)
All five findings share the same pattern: expected input artifact may be missing/empty, and the template does not specify explicit error behavior. P3 because each individual case is unlikely but the pattern is systemic.

Fix: Establish a convention across all templates: 'If an expected input artifact is missing or empty, write a brief failure artifact to the expected output path explaining the issue, then return FAIL with error details.' Apply this pattern to Pantry (missing/malformed metadata, empty file list), Big Head (missing reports), and Checkpoints (bd show failures).

Acceptance criteria:
- Each template has explicit instructions for handling missing/empty inputs
- Failure artifacts are written to expected output paths so downstream consumers are not left guessing
- Infrastructure failures (tool unavailability) are distinguished from substance failures (agent quality)

title: macOS (Darwin) incompatible shell commands in RULES.md
description:
## Root Cause

Shell commands in RULES.md assume GNU tooling behavior, but the documented platform (Darwin/macOS) uses BSD tooling. Most critically, `date +%s%N` silently produces literal `%N` on macOS instead of nanoseconds, reducing session ID entropy and weakening uniqueness guarantees when multiple sessions start rapidly.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/RULES.md:381` -- session ID generation uses `date +%s%N` which is GNU-only; macOS outputs literal `%N` (from edge-cases review, P2)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:156-176` -- TASK_IDS validation uses `tr | sed` pipeline when bash parameter expansion is documented as preferred two lines above (from edge-cases review, P3)
- `orchestration/RULES.md:157-159` -- REVIEW_ROUND validation uses `echo | grep` instead of bash-native `[[ =~ ]]` (from edge-cases review, P3)

## Fix

Replace platform-dependent commands with portable alternatives:
- Session ID: use `uuidgen | tr -d '-' | head -c 8 | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'` (available on macOS)
- TASK_IDS validation: use `[[ -z "${TASK_IDS//[[:space:]]/}" ]]` (matches the preferred pattern documented in the comment above)
- REVIEW_ROUND validation: use `[[ ! "${REVIEW_ROUND}" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]`

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/RULES.md:381`: Replace `date +%s%N` with `uuidgen`-based session ID generation
- `orchestration/RULES.md:172-176`: Replace `tr | sed` pipeline with bash parameter expansion
- `orchestration/RULES.md:157-159`: Replace `echo | grep` with `[[ =~ ]]` bash regex

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Session ID generation does not use `date +%s%N` or any GNU-only date format
- [ ] TASK_IDS validation uses bash parameter expansion (matching CHANGED_FILES pattern)
- [ ] REVIEW_ROUND validation uses bash-native regex matching
- [ ] All three changes verified to work on macOS (Darwin)

title: Scout strategy construction must verify all inventory tasks are assigned to agents
description:
During session 3be37d, the Scout listed 16 ready tasks in the inventory table but Strategy A only assigned 15 to agents. ant-farm-jv4 (checkpoint verdict thresholds, checkpoints.md) was listed in the inventory and file modification matrix but never placed in any agent grouping. The Queen caught the gap after Pantry composition but deferred instead of correcting before spawn.

Root cause: The Scout builds agent groupings file-first (what files does each hot file need?) rather than inventory-first (is every task accounted for?). Small, conflict-free tasks with no file overlaps are the most likely to be dropped since they don't trigger conflict-batching logic.

Fix: Add a mandatory verification step at the end of strategy construction in orchestration/templates/scout.md. After assigning all agents, count assigned tasks vs inventory count. If they differ, surface unassigned tasks explicitly before returning the briefing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md strategy construction section includes a post-assignment verification step
- [ ] The step compares assigned task count against inventory count and lists any unassigned tasks
- [ ] Unassigned tasks are flagged as errors that must be resolved before the briefing is returned

title: fix: WWD checkpoint skipped entirely in production session despite being documented as mandatory gate
description:
RULES.md:118-119 documents WWD (Wandering Worker Detection) as a mandatory hard gate: "after each agent commits, spawn Pest Control (model: haiku) for Wandering Worker Detection (WWD) (scope check before next agent in the wave can proceed)." checkpoints.md:264 echoes: "After agent commits, BEFORE spawning next agent in same wave." The Hard Gates table (RULES.md:259) lists WWD PASS as blocking "Next agent in wave."

Root cause: Two issues discovered in session artifact analysis:
1. In _session-7edaafbb (the largest observed session — 26 tasks, 7 parallel agents across multiple waves), zero WWD artifacts exist in the pc/ directory. The checkpoint was entirely skipped despite being documented as mandatory.
2. In _session-068ecc83 (smaller session), WWD did run but timing analysis shows all wave-1 agents committed within ~8 seconds of each other, with WWD artifacts appearing 47-78 seconds later in a batch. WWD was not actually gating individual agents — it was a post-hoc batch check.

The documentation describes per-agent serialized gating, but when agents run in parallel and commit nearly simultaneously, per-agent serialized gating is mechanically impossible. The docs do not acknowledge this reality. The known-failures reference (known-failures.md, Epic 74g) specifically cites WWD as the fix that would have caught a scope creep cascade — making its absence a real safety gap.

Current behavior: WWD is either skipped entirely (session-7edaafbb) or runs as a batch post-hoc check (session-068ecc83). Neither matches the documented "blocks next agent in wave" semantics.

Fix:
1. Investigate why _session-7edaafbb skipped WWD entirely — was it an intentional optimization for large waves, or a Queen process violation?
2. Update RULES.md Step 3 to document the actual behavior: when agents commit in parallel within a wave, WWD runs as a batch check after all commits rather than serializing individual agents
3. Define explicit criteria for when WWD can be batched vs when it must serialize (e.g., if agents share files, WWD must serialize; if agents have disjoint file sets, batch is acceptable)
4. Add a progress.log entry for WWD completion so skipped WWD is detectable during crash recovery

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md:118-119: Rewrite WWD timing description to match actual parallel-wave behavior
- orchestration/RULES.md Hard Gates table:259: Clarify "Next agent in wave" blocking semantics for parallel waves
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:264: Update "When" field to distinguish serial vs batch WWD
- orchestration/RULES.md: Add progress.log entry for WWD phase (currently missing — no WWD_COMPLETE milestone)

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Step 3 accurately describes when WWD runs in batch vs serial mode
- [ ] Hard Gates table clarifies blocking semantics for parallel waves
- [ ] checkpoints.md WWD "When" field matches RULES.md description
- [ ] Progress log includes a WWD milestone entry (detectable in crash recovery)
- [ ] Next production session with parallel agents produces WWD artifacts (verified post-fix)

Audit reference: DRIFT-004 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role
Pantry review mode has no fail-fast for empty changed-file list
New documents (GLOSSARY, PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS, SESSION_PLAN) missing from README File Reference
Template-vs-runtime placeholder confusion: no end-to-end substitution validation
description:
## Root Cause

Templates contain angle-bracket placeholders, "currently X" snapshot annotations, and self-referential placeholder fills that blur the line between template source code (documentation of what gets filled) and runtime code (what actually executes after substitution). There is no end-to-end validation that all placeholders are resolved after `build-review-prompts.sh` runs. The `fill_slot` self-reference pattern in `build_big_head_prompt()` has an undocumented ordering dependency.

## Affected Surfaces

- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L19` -- "currently `opus`" annotation will go stale (clarity review)
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md:L531-587` -- angle-bracket placeholders inside the guard that checks for angle brackets (clarity review, edge-cases review)
- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh:L254-298` -- DATA_FILE_PATH self-reference ordering dependency undocumented (edge-cases review)
- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh:L319` -- self-referencing fill_slot call has no ordering-dependency comment (clarity review, out-of-scope finding)
- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:L92-101` -- failure artifact heredoc with placeholder substitution risk (edge-cases review)

## Fix

1. Remove "currently `opus`" from big-head-skeleton.md:19 and replace with authoritative source pointer: "`{MODEL}`: Big Head model -- read from reviews.md > Big Head Consolidation Protocol > Model field. Do not hardcode here."
2. Add a comment block at the top of the reviews.md polling loop explaining these are template placeholders filled at runtime.
3. Add a post-write scan in `build-review-prompts.sh` that checks output files for unfilled `{{UPPERCASE}}` and `<angle-bracket>` patterns.
4. Add ordering-dependency comment above `fill_slot` calls in `build_big_head_prompt()`: "Step 4 runs AFTER the file is fully written by step 3. The DATA_FILE_PATH slot self-references $out_file, which is safe only because the file already exists at this point."

## Changes Needed

- `orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md`: Remove stale "currently opus" annotation at L19; replace with source-of-truth pointer
- `orchestration/templates/reviews.md`: Add template-source comment at top of polling loop code block (~L531)
- `scripts/build-review-prompts.sh`: Add post-write placeholder scan after all output files are written; add ordering-dependency comment above step 4 fill_slot block (~L317)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] big-head-skeleton.md does not contain "currently `opus`" or any hardcoded model value
- [ ] reviews.md polling loop code block has a comment explaining placeholders are filled at runtime
- [ ] build-review-prompts.sh emits an error if any output file contains unfilled `{{UPPERCASE}}` or `<angle-bracket>` patterns after all slots are filled
- [ ] build_big_head_prompt() has a comment explaining the fill_slot ordering dependency

title: bd show failure guard only on DMVDC Check 2, missing from other bd show callsites
description: The new bd show failure guard (ant-farm-zeu) is placed only on DMVDC Check 2 (checkpoints.md:332-337).
