However, other checkpoints also invoke bd show: WWD (line 245) and CCB Check 2 (lines 490-492). These callsites lack equivalent failure guards. If bd show can fail for DMVDC, it can fail for WWD and CCB too. Affected surfaces: checkpoints.md:245 (WWD), checkpoints.md:490-492 (CCB Check 2). Fix: Add similar guards to all bd show callsites, or create a shared 'bd show failure handling' note in the Pest Control Overview section.

title: Fix bd/beads references in agents/*.md
description: Replace bd CLI commands with crumb equivalents and .beads/ paths with .crumbs/ in: architect.md, big-head.md, scout-organizer.md, forager.md, surveyor.md

title: Fix bd/beads references in orchestration/templates/*.md
description: Replace bd CLI commands with crumb equivalents, .beads/ paths with .crumbs/, and Beads/Dolt terminology in: decomposition.md, architect-skeleton.md, checkpoints.md, review-focus-areas.md, forager.md, surveyor.md, surveyor-skeleton.md, implementation.md, pantry.md, big-head-skeleton.md, reviews.md

title: Fix bd/beads references in orchestration root and scripts

title: Add core test suite for crumb CLI
Add advanced test suite for crumb CLI
Audit and improve crumb.py documentation
Create test infrastructure and helper function tests
description: Create tests/test_crumb.py with the canonical crumbs_env fixture, tests/__init__.py, and initial test sections covering all helper functions and infrastructure. The crumbs_env fixture must monkeypatch find_crumbs_dir to use tmp_path. Tests must use pytest core only (no plugins). Cover: _priority_sort_key, _status_sort_key, now_iso, cleanup_stale_tmp_files, find_crumbs_dir (walk-up discovery + missing dir error), read_config/write_config, read_tasks/write_tasks, FileLock (acquire/release, context manager protocol), iter_jsonl, die() error output to stderr.
acceptance_criteria: tests/__init__.py exists as an empty file (new file).

tests/test_crumb.py exists and begins with a crumbs_env fixture matching the spec's canonical pattern: creates tmp_path/.crumbs, writes config.json with prefix/default_priority/next_crumb_id/next_trail_id, writes empty tasks.jsonl, monkeypatches crumb.find_crumbs_dir.
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_crumb.py -k test_priority_sort_key' passes and verifies _priority_sort_key returns 0 for P0, 1 for P1, 2 for P2, 3 for P3, 4 for P4, and 99 for unknown strings.
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_crumb.py -k test_status_sort_key' passes and verifies _status_sort_key returns 0 for in_progress, 1 for open, 2 for closed, and 99 for unknown strings.
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_crumb.py -k test_now_iso' passes and verifies now_iso returns a string matching ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS+00:00) and the timezone offset is +00:00.
Running 'python -m pytest tests/test_crumb.py -k test_cleanup_stale' passes and verifies cleanup_stale_tmp_files removes .tmp files older than a threshold from the .crumbs directory.

title: Add import and doctor command tests
description: Create tests/test_import.py with tests for the import command (both plain JSONL and --from-beads migration), and tests/test_doctor.py with tests for the doctor command (clean, malformed, duplicates, dangling refs, --fix). Tests use the crumbs_env fixture from conftest.py.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_import.py exists and uses the crumbs_env fixture from conftest.py (auto-discovered by pytest).

TestImportPlain: 'python -m pytest tests/test_import.py -k TestImportPlain' passes with tests verifying: cmd_import reads a plain JSONL file and adds records to tasks.jsonl; imported records get new IDs with the correct prefix; cmd_import raises SystemExit for nonexistent input file.

TestImportBeads: 'python -m pytest tests/test_import.py -k TestImportBeads' passes with tests verifying: cmd_import with --from-beads converts beads records to crumb format using _convert_beads_record; _resolve_beads_epic_refs resolves old epic references to new trail IDs; _apply_blocks_deps converts beads dependency relationships to crumb blocked_by links.
tests/test_doctor.py exists and uses the crumbs_env fixture from conftest.py (auto-discovered by pytest).

TestDoctor: 'python -m pytest tests/test_doctor.py -k TestDoctor' passes with tests verifying: cmd_doctor detects clean state (no issues found); cmd_doctor detects malformed JSONL records; cmd_doctor detects duplicate IDs; cmd_doctor detects dangling blocked_by references; cmd_doctor detects dangling parent references.
TestDoctor: a test verifies cmd_doctor with --fix argument repairs detected issues (removes dangling refs, deduplicates).
TestCleanup: 'python -m pytest tests/test_doctor.py -k TestCleanup' passes with a test verifying cleanup_stale_tmp_files integration with the doctor workflow.
notes: crumb.py is read-only for this crumb; do not modify it.
Import tests need sample JSONL files created in tmp_path during test setup.
Beads import tests need sample beads-format records — reference _convert_beads_record to understand the field mapping.
Doctor --fix modifies tasks.jsonl in place — verify by re-reading after fix.

title: Add CLI integration subprocess tests
description: Create tests/test_cli.py with a TestCLIIntegration class that runs crumb.py as a subprocess (via subprocess.run) and verifies end-to-end behavior including exit codes, stdout output, and stderr error messages. These tests complement the unit tests by verifying the CLI argument parsing layer.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_cli.py exists and contains a TestCLIIntegration class.

TestCLIIntegration: 'python -m pytest tests/test_cli.py -k TestCLIIntegration' passes with tests verifying: running 'python crumb.py create --title Test' via subprocess produces exit code 0 and prints the new crumb ID to stdout.
TestCLIIntegration: a test verifies 'python crumb.py list' via subprocess produces exit code 0.
TestCLIIntegration: a test verifies 'python crumb.py show NONEXISTENT' via subprocess produces nonzero exit code and prints an error message to stderr.
TestCLIIntegration: a test verifies 'python crumb.py --help' produces exit code 0 and output contains 'usage:'.
TestCLIIntegration: tests use a temporary .crumbs/ directory (set up via tmp_path and environment or monkeypatch) to avoid touching the real .crumbs/.
notes: crumb.py is read-only for this crumb; do not modify it.
Subprocess tests need a real .crumbs/ directory on disk — use tmp_path and set cwd to a directory containing it.
subprocess.run(['python', 'crumb.py', ...], capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=str(tmp_path)) with the crumb.py path resolved to absolute.

title: Audit and improve crumb.py docstrings and inline comments
description: Review all function and class docstrings in crumb.py for completeness. Every public function must have Args/Returns/Raises sections where applicable. Add or fix inline comments for complex logic sections. Do not change any runtime behavior — only documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Every public function (not prefixed with _) in crumb.py has a docstring with Args, Returns, and Raises sections where applicable.
Every private function (prefixed with _) in crumb.py has at least a one-line summary docstring.
The FileLock class has a class-level docstring explaining its purpose, and its __init__, __enter__, __exit__ methods have docstrings.
Complex logic sections (the import conversion pipeline: _convert_beads_record, _resolve_beads_epic_refs, _apply_blocks_deps) have inline comments explaining the transformation steps.
No runtime behavior changes — running 'python -c "import crumb; print(crumb.__doc__)"' succeeds, and 'python -m pytest tests/ -v' still passes with the same results as before this crumb.
Running 'python -m py_compile crumb.py' exits with code 0 (no syntax errors introduced).
notes: This is the only crumb that modifies crumb.py.
Focus on documentation only — no functional changes.
Use Google-style docstrings (already the pattern in the file).

title: Verify full test suite passes green with no warnings
description: Run the complete test suite with 'pytest tests/ -v' and fix any failures, xpass results, or pytest warnings. All tests must pass or be marked xfail (and show as xfail, not xpass). No pytest warnings in the output. This is the final verification step.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'python -m pytest tests/ -v' exits with code 0.
The pytest output shows zero failures (0 failed).
Any xfail-marked tests show as 'xfail' in output, not 'xpass'.
The pytest output contains no warnings section (no 'warnings summary' header).
All test files are discovered: test_helpers.py, test_crud.py, test_queries.py, test_links.py, test_trails.py, test_import.py, test_doctor.py, test_cli.py appear in the verbose output.
Running 'python -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short' produces output showing tests from all 8 test files.
notes: This crumb may need to fix issues in any test file or crumb.py.
The scope is 10 files which exceeds the 5-8 budget, but this is the final verification crumb that must be able to touch any file to fix issues. This is justified by the nature of the task.
If tests fail, fix the test or the code — do not delete tests.

title: AF-15 AC 4: _status_sort_key return values incorrect in acceptance criteria
description: AF-15 AC 4 states _status_sort_key returns 0 for in_progress, 1 for open. Implementation at crumb.py:469-470 returns open=0, in_progress=1. Tests match implementation. AC text is the error. Fix: update AF-15 AC 4 to open=0, in_progress=1, closed=2, unknown=3.

title: AF-18 AC 6: dangling blocker behavior incorrect in acceptance criteria
description: AF-18 AC 6 states cmd_link raises SystemExit for nonexistent blocker/parent ID. Implementation intentionally allows dangling refs (tests/test_links.py:7-16 documents this). Fix: update AF-18 AC 6 to reflect that cmd_link permits dangling references and crumb doctor validates integrity.

title: Dead dual-field access pattern in parent/discovered resolution
description: Code checks top-level crumb.get(parent) before links.get(parent) at crumb.py:517, crumb.py:613-620, crumb.py:622-626. No code path writes top-level parent/discovered_from. Creates schema confusion and inconsistency with _get_trail_children. Fix: remove all top-level field checks, use only links.get(...).

title: fcntl.flock blocks indefinitely with no timeout or platform guard
description: FileLock at crumb.py:340 uses fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) with no timeout. Blocks indefinitely on contention. fcntl is Unix-only with no platform guard. Fix: add LOCK_NB retry with timeout or document platform restriction.

title: .tmp file not cleaned on os.rename failure in write_config/write_tasks
description: write_config (crumb.py:209) and write_tasks (crumb.py:268) leave .tmp files on disk when os.rename fails. Fix: wrap os.rename in try/finally to unlink tmp_path on failure.

title: Weak assertion in test_import_updates_config_counter
description: tests/test_import.py:126 uses >= 11 but result is deterministically 11. Fix: tighten to == 11.

title: AF-15 AC text errors: priority sort key unknown=99 and now_iso format
description: AF-15 AC 3 says _priority_sort_key returns 99 for unknown (impl returns 5). AC 5 says now_iso uses +00:00 (impl uses Z). Fix: update AC text. No code changes.

title: Misleading test name test_update_invalid_status_raises_system_exit
description: tests/test_crud.py:503-525 test name says invalid status but tests closed->in_progress guard. Fix: rename to test_update_closed_crumb_status_raises_system_exit.

title: Config counter arithmetic edge cases in cmd_import
description: crumb.py:1868 counter can underflow to -1 on corrupt config. crumb.py:792-795 collision loop creates ID gaps. crumb.py:1897-1899 config not written when imported_count=0 but counters mutated. Fix: clamp counter, move write_config outside guard.

title: Redundant crumbs_dir() wrapper
description: crumb.py:113 crumbs_dir() is a one-liner wrapper that just calls find_crumbs_dir(). Fix: remove wrapper or document why split exists.

title: seen: List naming mismatch in _get_blocked_by
description: crumb.py:1016 dedup accumulator named seen but typed as List instead of set. Fix: rename to merged or result with comment.

title: Incomplete comment on protected fields set in cmd_update
description: crumb.py:888-891 comment explains status and closed_at but not id and created_at. Fix: expand comment to cover all four.

title: Silent last-wins loop in _convert_beads_record
description: crumb.py:1629-1631 loop over deps silently overwrites parent_id with no break or comment. Fix: add comment or break.

title: Unnecessary _blocked_args() test helper
description: tests/test_queries.py:95-97 passthrough helper with no defaults. Fix: remove and call Namespace() directly.

title: FileLock test conflates two concerns
description: tests/test_helpers.py:281-295 umbrella test overlaps with focused tests. Fix: rename or deduplicate.

title: Imprecise dual-or assertion in doctor test
description: tests/test_doctor.py:233-256 accepts not a trail or dangling interchangeably. Fix: assert specific message.

title: Module docstring missing cmd_import coverage note
description: tests/test_import.py:1 docstring Covers list omits cmd_import integration tests. Fix: add to Covers.

title: Misleading test name test_no_filters_shows_all_non_closed
description: tests/test_queries.py:113 name says non_closed but test asserts closed items ARE shown. Fix: rename to test_no_filters_shows_all_records_including_closed.

title: iter_jsonl dies inside generator with SystemExit
description: crumb.py:286-301 die() inside generator raises SystemExit on OSError. Function appears unused. Fix: document contract or remove if unused.

title: Silent drop of blocks dep to missing target in import
description: tests/test_import.py:422-433 confirms missing-target dep is silently skipped with no warning. Fix: emit stderr warning.

title: Duplicate cleanup_stale_tmp_files tests across two modules
description: tests/test_doctor.py:377-435 and tests/test_helpers.py:313-353 have identical scenarios. Fix: consolidate into test_helpers.py.
